members.name: AreaLightCreate
members.signature: public RID AreaLightCreate()
members.summary: Creates a new area light and adds it to the RenderingServer. It can be accessed with the RID that is returned. This RID can be used in most [code]light_*[/code] RenderingServer functions. Once finished with your RID, you will want to free the RID using the RenderingServer's [method free_rid] method. To place in a scene, attach this area light to an instance using [method instance_set_base] using the returned RID. [b]Note:[/b] The equivalent node is [AreaLight3D].

members.name: BakeRenderUv2
members.signature: public Image[] BakeRenderUv2(RID @base, RID[] materialOverrides, Vector2i imageSize)
members.summary: Bakes the material data of the Mesh passed in the [param base] parameter with optional [param material_overrides] to a set of [Image]s of size [param image_size]. Returns an array of [Image]s containing material properties as specified in [enum BakeChannels].

members.name: CallOnRenderThread
members.signature: public void CallOnRenderThread(Callable callable)
members.summary: As the RenderingServer actual logic may run on a separate thread, accessing its internals from the main (or any other) thread will result in errors. To make it easier to run code that can safely access the rendering internals (such as [RenderingDevice] and similar RD classes), push a callable via this function so it will be executed on the render thread.

members.name: CameraAttributesCreate
members.signature: public RID CameraAttributesCreate()
members.summary: Creates a camera attributes object and adds it to the RenderingServer. It can be accessed with the RID that is returned. This RID will be used in all [code]camera_attributes_[/code] RenderingServer functions. Once finished with your RID, you will want to free the RID using the RenderingServer's [method free_rid] method. [b]Note:[/b] The equivalent resource is [CameraAttributes].

members.name: CameraAttributesSetAutoExposure
members.signature: public void CameraAttributesSetAutoExposure(RID cameraAttributes, bool enable, float minSensitivity, float maxSensitivity, float speed, float scale)
members.summary: Sets the parameters to use with the auto-exposure effect. These parameters take on the same meaning as their counterparts in [CameraAttributes] and [CameraAttributesPractical].

members.name: CameraAttributesSetDofBlur
members.signature: public void CameraAttributesSetDofBlur(RID cameraAttributes, bool farEnable, float farDistance, float farTransition, bool nearEnable, float nearDistance, float nearTransition, float amount)
members.summary: Sets the parameters to use with the DOF blur effect. These parameters take on the same meaning as their counterparts in [CameraAttributesPractical].

members.name: CameraAttributesSetDofBlurBokehShape
members.signature: public void CameraAttributesSetDofBlurBokehShape(int shape)
members.summary: Sets the shape of the DOF bokeh pattern to [param shape]. Different shapes may be used to achieve artistic effect, or to meet performance targets.

members.name: CameraAttributesSetDofBlurQuality
members.signature: public void CameraAttributesSetDofBlurQuality(int quality, bool useJitter)
members.summary: Sets the quality level of the DOF blur effect to [param quality]. [param use_jitter] can be used to jitter samples taken during the blur pass to hide artifacts at the cost of looking more fuzzy.

members.name: CameraAttributesSetExposure
members.signature: public void CameraAttributesSetExposure(RID cameraAttributes, float multiplier, float normalization)
members.summary: Sets the exposure values that will be used by the renderers. The normalization amount is used to bake a given Exposure Value (EV) into rendering calculations to reduce the dynamic range of the scene. The normalization factor can be calculated from exposure value (EV100) as follows: [codeblock] func get_exposure_normalization(ev100: float): return 1.0 / (pow(2.0, ev100) * 1.2) [/codeblock] The exposure value can be calculated from aperture (in f-stops), shutter speed (in seconds), and sensitivity (in ISO) as follows: [codeblock] func get_exposure(aperture: float, shutter_speed: float, sensitivity: float): return log((aperture * aperture) / shutter_speed * (100.0 / sensitivity)) / log(2) [/codeblock]

members.name: CameraCreate
members.signature: public RID CameraCreate()
members.summary: Creates a 3D camera and adds it to the RenderingServer. It can be accessed with the RID that is returned. This RID will be used in all [code]camera_*[/code] RenderingServer functions. Once finished with your RID, you will want to free the RID using the RenderingServer's [method free_rid] method. [b]Note:[/b] The equivalent node is [Camera3D].

members.name: CameraSetCameraAttributes
members.signature: public void CameraSetCameraAttributes(RID camera, RID effects)
members.summary: Sets the camera_attributes created with [method camera_attributes_create] to the given camera.

members.name: CameraSetCompositor
members.signature: public void CameraSetCompositor(RID camera, RID compositor)
members.summary: Sets the compositor used by this camera. Equivalent to [member Camera3D.compositor].

members.name: CameraSetCullMask
members.signature: public void CameraSetCullMask(RID camera, int layers)
members.summary: Sets the cull mask associated with this camera. The cull mask describes which 3D layers are rendered by this camera. Equivalent to [member Camera3D.cull_mask].

members.name: CameraSetEnvironment
members.signature: public void CameraSetEnvironment(RID camera, RID env)
members.summary: Sets the environment used by this camera. Equivalent to [member Camera3D.environment].

members.name: CameraSetFrustum
members.signature: public void CameraSetFrustum(RID camera, float size, Vector2 offset, float zNear, float zFar)
members.summary: Sets camera to use frustum projection. This mode allows adjusting the [param offset] argument to create "tilted frustum" effects.

members.name: CameraSetOrthogonal
members.signature: public void CameraSetOrthogonal(RID camera, float size, float zNear, float zFar)
members.summary: Sets camera to use orthogonal projection, also known as orthographic projection. Objects remain the same size on the screen no matter how far away they are.

members.name: CameraSetPerspective
members.signature: public void CameraSetPerspective(RID camera, float fovyDegrees, float zNear, float zFar)
members.summary: Sets camera to use perspective projection. Objects on the screen becomes smaller when they are far away.

members.name: CameraSetTransform
members.signature: public void CameraSetTransform(RID camera, Transform3D transform)
members.summary: Sets [Transform3D] of camera.

members.name: CameraSetUseVerticalAspect
members.signature: public void CameraSetUseVerticalAspect(RID camera, bool enable)
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], preserves the horizontal aspect ratio which is equivalent to [constant Camera3D.KEEP_WIDTH]. If [code]false[/code], preserves the vertical aspect ratio which is equivalent to [constant Camera3D.KEEP_HEIGHT].

members.name: CanvasCreate
members.signature: public RID CanvasCreate()
members.summary: Creates a canvas and returns the assigned [RID]. It can be accessed with the RID that is returned. This RID will be used in all [code]canvas_*[/code] RenderingServer functions. Once finished with your RID, you will want to free the RID using the RenderingServer's [method free_rid] method. Canvas has no [Resource] or [Node] equivalent.

members.name: CanvasItemAddAnimationSlice
members.signature: public void CanvasItemAddAnimationSlice(RID item, float animationLength, float sliceBegin, float sliceEnd, float offset)
members.summary: Subsequent drawing commands will be ignored unless they fall within the specified animation slice. This is a faster way to implement animations that loop on background rather than redrawing constantly.

members.name: CanvasItemAddCircle
members.signature: public void CanvasItemAddCircle(RID item, Vector2 pos, float radius, Color color, bool antialiased)
members.summary: Draws a circle on the [CanvasItem] pointed to by the [param item] [RID]. See also [method CanvasItem.draw_circle].

members.name: CanvasItemAddClipIgnore
members.signature: public void CanvasItemAddClipIgnore(RID item, bool ignore)
members.summary: If [param ignore] is [code]true[/code], ignore clipping on items drawn with this canvas item until this is called again with [param ignore] set to [code]false[/code].

members.name: CanvasItemAddEllipse
members.signature: public void CanvasItemAddEllipse(RID item, Vector2 pos, float major, float minor, Color color, bool antialiased)
members.summary: Draws an ellipse with semi-major axis [param major] and semi-minor axis [param minor] on the [CanvasItem] pointed to by the [param item] [RID]. See also [method CanvasItem.draw_ellipse].

members.name: CanvasItemAddLcdTextureRectRegion
members.signature: public void CanvasItemAddLcdTextureRectRegion(RID item, Rect2 rect, RID texture, Rect2 srcRect, Color modulate)
members.summary: See also [method CanvasItem.draw_lcd_texture_rect_region].

members.name: CanvasItemAddLine
members.signature: public void CanvasItemAddLine(RID item, Vector2 from, Vector2 to, Color color, float width, bool antialiased)
members.summary: Draws a line on the [CanvasItem] pointed to by the [param item] [RID]. See also [method CanvasItem.draw_line].

members.name: CanvasItemAddMesh
members.signature: public void CanvasItemAddMesh(RID item, RID mesh, Transform2D transform, Color modulate, RID texture)
members.summary: Draws a mesh created with [method mesh_create] with given [param transform], [param modulate] color, and [param texture]. This is used internally by [MeshInstance2D].

members.name: CanvasItemAddMsdfTextureRectRegion
members.signature: public void CanvasItemAddMsdfTextureRectRegion(RID item, Rect2 rect, RID texture, Rect2 srcRect, Color modulate, int outlineSize, float pxRange, float scale)
members.summary: See also [method CanvasItem.draw_msdf_texture_rect_region].

members.name: CanvasItemAddMultiline
members.signature: public void CanvasItemAddMultiline(RID item, PackedVector2Array points, PackedColorArray colors, float width, bool antialiased)
members.summary: Draws a 2D multiline on the [CanvasItem] pointed to by the [param item] [RID]. See also [method CanvasItem.draw_multiline] and [method CanvasItem.draw_multiline_colors].

members.name: CanvasItemAddMultimesh
members.signature: public void CanvasItemAddMultimesh(RID item, RID mesh, RID texture)
members.summary: Draws a 2D [MultiMesh] on the [CanvasItem] pointed to by the [param item] [RID]. See also [method CanvasItem.draw_multimesh].

members.name: CanvasItemAddNinePatch
members.signature: public void CanvasItemAddNinePatch(RID item, Rect2 rect, Rect2 source, RID texture, Vector2 topleft, Vector2 bottomright, int xAxisMode, int yAxisMode, bool drawCenter, Color modulate)
members.summary: Draws a nine-patch rectangle on the [CanvasItem] pointed to by the [param item] [RID].

members.name: CanvasItemAddParticles
members.signature: public void CanvasItemAddParticles(RID item, RID particles, RID texture)
members.summary: Draws particles on the [CanvasItem] pointed to by the [param item] [RID].

members.name: CanvasItemAddPolygon
members.signature: public void CanvasItemAddPolygon(RID item, PackedVector2Array points, PackedColorArray colors, PackedVector2Array uvs, RID texture)
members.summary: Draws a 2D polygon on the [CanvasItem] pointed to by the [param item] [RID]. If you need more flexibility (such as being able to use bones), use [method canvas_item_add_triangle_array] instead. See also [method CanvasItem.draw_polygon]. [b]Note:[/b] If you frequently redraw the same polygon with a large number of vertices, consider pre-calculating the triangulation with [method Geometry2D.triangulate_polygon] and using [method CanvasItem.draw_mesh], [method CanvasItem.draw_multimesh], or [method canvas_item_add_triangle_array].

members.name: CanvasItemAddPolyline
members.signature: public void CanvasItemAddPolyline(RID item, PackedVector2Array points, PackedColorArray colors, float width, bool antialiased)
members.summary: Draws a 2D polyline on the [CanvasItem] pointed to by the [param item] [RID]. See also [method CanvasItem.draw_polyline] and [method CanvasItem.draw_polyline_colors].

members.name: CanvasItemAddPrimitive
members.signature: public void CanvasItemAddPrimitive(RID item, PackedVector2Array points, PackedColorArray colors, PackedVector2Array uvs, RID texture)
members.summary: Draws a 2D primitive on the [CanvasItem] pointed to by the [param item] [RID]. See also [method CanvasItem.draw_primitive].

members.name: CanvasItemAddRect
members.signature: public void CanvasItemAddRect(RID item, Rect2 rect, Color color, bool antialiased)
members.summary: Draws a rectangle on the [CanvasItem] pointed to by the [param item] [RID]. See also [method CanvasItem.draw_rect].

members.name: CanvasItemAddSetTransform
members.signature: public void CanvasItemAddSetTransform(RID item, Transform2D transform)
members.summary: Sets a [Transform2D] that will be used to transform subsequent canvas item commands.

members.name: CanvasItemAddTextureRect
members.signature: public void CanvasItemAddTextureRect(RID item, Rect2 rect, RID texture, bool tile, Color modulate, bool transpose)
members.summary: Draws a 2D textured rectangle on the [CanvasItem] pointed to by the [param item] [RID]. See also [method CanvasItem.draw_texture_rect] and [method Texture2D.draw_rect].

members.name: CanvasItemAddTextureRectRegion
members.signature: public void CanvasItemAddTextureRectRegion(RID item, Rect2 rect, RID texture, Rect2 srcRect, Color modulate, bool transpose, bool clipUv)
members.summary: Draws the specified region of a 2D textured rectangle on the [CanvasItem] pointed to by the [param item] [RID]. See also [method CanvasItem.draw_texture_rect_region] and [method Texture2D.draw_rect_region].

members.name: CanvasItemAddTriangleArray
members.signature: public void CanvasItemAddTriangleArray(RID item, PackedInt32Array indices, PackedVector2Array points, PackedColorArray colors, PackedVector2Array uvs, PackedInt32Array bones, PackedFloat32Array weights, RID texture, int count)
members.summary: Draws a triangle array on the [CanvasItem] pointed to by the [param item] [RID]. This is internally used by [Line2D] and [StyleBoxFlat] for rendering. [method canvas_item_add_triangle_array] is highly flexible, but more complex to use than [method canvas_item_add_polygon]. [b]Note:[/b] If [param count] is set to a non-negative value, only the first [code]count * 3[/code] indices (corresponding to [code skip-lint]count[/code] triangles) will be drawn. Otherwise, all indices are drawn.

members.name: CanvasItemAttachSkeleton
members.signature: public void CanvasItemAttachSkeleton(RID item, RID skeleton)
members.summary: Attaches a skeleton to the [CanvasItem]. Removes the previous skeleton.

members.name: CanvasItemClear
members.signature: public void CanvasItemClear(RID item)
members.summary: Clears the [CanvasItem] and removes all commands in it.

members.name: CanvasItemCreate
members.signature: public RID CanvasItemCreate()
members.summary: Creates a new CanvasItem instance and returns its [RID]. It can be accessed with the RID that is returned. This RID will be used in all [code]canvas_item_*[/code] RenderingServer functions. Once finished with your RID, you will want to free the RID using the RenderingServer's [method free_rid] method. [b]Note:[/b] The equivalent node is [CanvasItem].

members.name: CanvasItemGetInstanceShaderParameter
members.signature: public Variant CanvasItemGetInstanceShaderParameter(RID instance, StringName parameter)
members.summary: Returns the value of the per-instance shader uniform from the specified canvas item instance. Equivalent to [method CanvasItem.get_instance_shader_parameter].

members.name: CanvasItemGetInstanceShaderParameterDefaultValue
members.signature: public Variant CanvasItemGetInstanceShaderParameterDefaultValue(RID instance, StringName parameter)
members.summary: Returns the default value of the per-instance shader uniform from the specified canvas item instance. Equivalent to [method CanvasItem.get_instance_shader_parameter].

members.name: CanvasItemGetInstanceShaderParameterList
members.signature: public Dictionary[] CanvasItemGetInstanceShaderParameterList(RID instance)
members.summary: Returns a dictionary of per-instance shader uniform names of the per-instance shader uniform from the specified canvas item instance. The returned dictionary is in PropertyInfo format, with the keys [code]name[/code], [code]class_name[/code], [code]type[/code], [code]hint[/code], [code]hint_string[/code], and [code]usage[/code].

members.name: CanvasItemResetPhysicsInterpolation
members.signature: public void CanvasItemResetPhysicsInterpolation(RID item)
members.summary: Prevents physics interpolation for the current physics tick. This is useful when moving a canvas item to a new location, to give an instantaneous change rather than interpolation from the previous location.

members.name: CanvasItemSetCanvasGroupMode
members.signature: public void CanvasItemSetCanvasGroupMode(RID item, int mode, float clearMargin, bool fitEmpty, float fitMargin, bool blurMipmaps)
members.summary: Sets the canvas group mode used during 2D rendering for the canvas item specified by the [param item] RID. For faster but more limited clipping, use [method canvas_item_set_clip] instead. [b]Note:[/b] The equivalent node functionality is found in [CanvasGroup] and [member CanvasItem.clip_children].

members.name: CanvasItemSetClip
members.signature: public void CanvasItemSetClip(RID item, bool clip)
members.summary: If [param clip] is [code]true[/code], makes the canvas item specified by the [param item] RID not draw anything outside of its rect's coordinates. This clipping is fast, but works only with axis-aligned rectangles. This means that rotation is ignored by the clipping rectangle. For more advanced clipping shapes, use [method canvas_item_set_canvas_group_mode] instead. [b]Note:[/b] The equivalent node functionality is found in [member Label.clip_text], [RichTextLabel] (always enabled) and more.

members.name: CanvasItemSetCopyToBackbuffer
members.signature: public void CanvasItemSetCopyToBackbuffer(RID item, bool enabled, Rect2 rect)
members.summary: Sets the [CanvasItem] to copy a rect to the backbuffer.

members.name: CanvasItemSetCustomRect
members.signature: public void CanvasItemSetCustomRect(RID item, bool useCustomRect, Rect2 rect)
members.summary: If [param use_custom_rect] is [code]true[/code], sets the custom visibility rectangle (used for culling) to [param rect] for the canvas item specified by [param item]. Setting a custom visibility rect can reduce CPU load when drawing lots of 2D instances. If [param use_custom_rect] is [code]false[/code], automatically computes a visibility rectangle based on the canvas item's draw commands.

members.name: CanvasItemSetDefaultTextureFilter
members.signature: public void CanvasItemSetDefaultTextureFilter(RID item, int filter)
members.summary: Sets the default texture filter mode for the canvas item specified by the [param item] RID. Equivalent to [member CanvasItem.texture_filter].

members.name: CanvasItemSetDefaultTextureRepeat
members.signature: public void CanvasItemSetDefaultTextureRepeat(RID item, int repeat)
members.summary: Sets the default texture repeat mode for the canvas item specified by the [param item] RID. Equivalent to [member CanvasItem.texture_repeat].

members.name: CanvasItemSetDistanceFieldMode
members.signature: public void CanvasItemSetDistanceFieldMode(RID item, bool enabled)
members.summary: If [param enabled] is [code]true[/code], enables multichannel signed distance field rendering mode for the canvas item specified by the [param item] RID. This is meant to be used for font rendering, or with specially generated images using [url=https://github.com/Chlumsky/msdfgen]msdfgen[/url].

members.name: CanvasItemSetDrawBehindParent
members.signature: public void CanvasItemSetDrawBehindParent(RID item, bool enabled)
members.summary: If [param enabled] is [code]true[/code], draws the canvas item specified by the [param item] RID behind its parent. Equivalent to [member CanvasItem.show_behind_parent].

members.name: CanvasItemSetDrawIndex
members.signature: public void CanvasItemSetDrawIndex(RID item, int index)
members.summary: Sets the index for the [CanvasItem].

members.name: CanvasItemSetInstanceShaderParameter
members.signature: public void CanvasItemSetInstanceShaderParameter(RID instance, StringName parameter, Variant value)
members.summary: Sets the per-instance shader uniform on the specified canvas item instance. Equivalent to [method CanvasItem.set_instance_shader_parameter].

members.name: CanvasItemSetInterpolated
members.signature: public void CanvasItemSetInterpolated(RID item, bool interpolated)
members.summary: If [param interpolated] is [code]true[/code], turns on physics interpolation for the canvas item.

members.name: CanvasItemSetLightMask
members.signature: public void CanvasItemSetLightMask(RID item, int mask)
members.summary: Sets the light [param mask] for the canvas item specified by the [param item] RID. Equivalent to [member CanvasItem.light_mask].

members.name: CanvasItemSetMaterial
members.signature: public void CanvasItemSetMaterial(RID item, RID material)
members.summary: Sets a new [param material] to the canvas item specified by the [param item] RID. Equivalent to [member CanvasItem.material].

members.name: CanvasItemSetModulate
members.signature: public void CanvasItemSetModulate(RID item, Color color)
members.summary: Multiplies the color of the canvas item specified by the [param item] RID, while affecting its children. See also [method canvas_item_set_self_modulate]. Equivalent to [member CanvasItem.modulate].

members.name: CanvasItemSetParent
members.signature: public void CanvasItemSetParent(RID item, RID parent)
members.summary: Sets a parent [CanvasItem] to the [CanvasItem]. The item will inherit transform, modulation and visibility from its parent, like [CanvasItem] nodes in the scene tree.

members.name: CanvasItemSetSelfModulate
members.signature: public void CanvasItemSetSelfModulate(RID item, Color color)
members.summary: Multiplies the color of the canvas item specified by the [param item] RID, without affecting its children. See also [method canvas_item_set_modulate]. Equivalent to [member CanvasItem.self_modulate].

members.name: CanvasItemSetSortChildrenByY
members.signature: public void CanvasItemSetSortChildrenByY(RID item, bool enabled)
members.summary: If [param enabled] is [code]true[/code], child nodes with the lowest Y position are drawn before those with a higher Y position. Y-sorting only affects children that inherit from the canvas item specified by the [param item] RID, not the canvas item itself. Equivalent to [member CanvasItem.y_sort_enabled].

members.name: CanvasItemSetTransform
members.signature: public void CanvasItemSetTransform(RID item, Transform2D transform)
members.summary: Sets the [param transform] of the canvas item specified by the [param item] RID. This affects where and how the item will be drawn. Child canvas items' transforms are multiplied by their parent's transform. Equivalent to [member Node2D.transform].

members.name: CanvasItemSetUseParentMaterial
members.signature: public void CanvasItemSetUseParentMaterial(RID item, bool enabled)
members.summary: Sets if the [CanvasItem] uses its parent's material.

members.name: CanvasItemSetVisibilityLayer
members.signature: public void CanvasItemSetVisibilityLayer(RID item, int visibilityLayer)
members.summary: Sets the rendering visibility layer associated with this [CanvasItem]. Only [Viewport] nodes with a matching rendering mask will render this [CanvasItem].

members.name: CanvasItemSetVisibilityNotifier
members.signature: public void CanvasItemSetVisibilityNotifier(RID item, bool enable, Rect2 area, Callable enterCallable, Callable exitCallable)
members.summary: Sets the given [CanvasItem] as visibility notifier. [param area] defines the area of detecting visibility. [param enter_callable] is called when the [CanvasItem] enters the screen, [param exit_callable] is called when the [CanvasItem] exits the screen. If [param enable] is [code]false[/code], the item will no longer function as notifier. This method can be used to manually mimic [VisibleOnScreenNotifier2D].

members.name: CanvasItemSetVisible
members.signature: public void CanvasItemSetVisible(RID item, bool visible)
members.summary: Sets the visibility of the [CanvasItem].

members.name: CanvasItemSetZAsRelativeToParent
members.signature: public void CanvasItemSetZAsRelativeToParent(RID item, bool enabled)
members.summary: If this is enabled, the Z index of the parent will be added to the children's Z index.

members.name: CanvasItemSetZIndex
members.signature: public void CanvasItemSetZIndex(RID item, int zIndex)
members.summary: Sets the [CanvasItem]'s Z index, i.e. its draw order (lower indexes are drawn first).

members.name: CanvasItemTransformPhysicsInterpolation
members.signature: public void CanvasItemTransformPhysicsInterpolation(RID item, Transform2D transform)
members.summary: Transforms both the current and previous stored transform for a canvas item. This allows transforming a canvas item without creating a "glitch" in the interpolation, which is particularly useful for large worlds utilizing a shifting origin.

members.name: CanvasLightAttachToCanvas
members.signature: public void CanvasLightAttachToCanvas(RID light, RID canvas)
members.summary: Attaches the canvas light to the canvas. Removes it from its previous canvas.

members.name: CanvasLightCreate
members.signature: public RID CanvasLightCreate()
members.summary: Creates a canvas light and adds it to the RenderingServer. It can be accessed with the RID that is returned. This RID will be used in all [code]canvas_light_*[/code] RenderingServer functions. Once finished with your RID, you will want to free the RID using the RenderingServer's [method free_rid] method. [b]Note:[/b] The equivalent node is [Light2D].

members.name: CanvasLightOccluderAttachToCanvas
members.signature: public void CanvasLightOccluderAttachToCanvas(RID occluder, RID canvas)
members.summary: Attaches a light occluder to the canvas. Removes it from its previous canvas.

members.name: CanvasLightOccluderCreate
members.signature: public RID CanvasLightOccluderCreate()
members.summary: Creates a light occluder and adds it to the RenderingServer. It can be accessed with the RID that is returned. This RID will be used in all [code]canvas_light_occluder_*[/code] RenderingServer functions. Once finished with your RID, you will want to free the RID using the RenderingServer's [method free_rid] method. [b]Note:[/b] The equivalent node is [LightOccluder2D].

members.name: CanvasLightOccluderResetPhysicsInterpolation
members.signature: public void CanvasLightOccluderResetPhysicsInterpolation(RID occluder)
members.summary: Prevents physics interpolation for the current physics tick. This is useful when moving an occluder to a new location, to give an instantaneous change rather than interpolation from the previous location.

members.name: CanvasLightOccluderSetAsSdfCollision
members.signature: public void CanvasLightOccluderSetAsSdfCollision(RID occluder, bool enable)
members.summary: Enables or disables using the light occluder as a signed distance field for 2D particle collision.

members.name: CanvasLightOccluderSetEnabled
members.signature: public void CanvasLightOccluderSetEnabled(RID occluder, bool enabled)
members.summary: Enables or disables light occluder.

members.name: CanvasLightOccluderSetInterpolated
members.signature: public void CanvasLightOccluderSetInterpolated(RID occluder, bool interpolated)
members.summary: If [param interpolated] is [code]true[/code], turns on physics interpolation for the light occluder.

members.name: CanvasLightOccluderSetLightMask
members.signature: public void CanvasLightOccluderSetLightMask(RID occluder, int mask)
members.summary: The light mask. See [LightOccluder2D] for more information on light masks.

members.name: CanvasLightOccluderSetPolygon
members.signature: public void CanvasLightOccluderSetPolygon(RID occluder, RID polygon)
members.summary: Sets a light occluder's polygon.

members.name: CanvasLightOccluderSetTransform
members.signature: public void CanvasLightOccluderSetTransform(RID occluder, Transform2D transform)
members.summary: Sets a light occluder's [Transform2D].

members.name: CanvasLightOccluderTransformPhysicsInterpolation
members.signature: public void CanvasLightOccluderTransformPhysicsInterpolation(RID occluder, Transform2D transform)
members.summary: Transforms both the current and previous stored transform for a light occluder. This allows transforming an occluder without creating a "glitch" in the interpolation, which is particularly useful for large worlds utilizing a shifting origin.

members.name: CanvasLightResetPhysicsInterpolation
members.signature: public void CanvasLightResetPhysicsInterpolation(RID light)
members.summary: Prevents physics interpolation for the current physics tick. This is useful when moving a canvas item to a new location, to give an instantaneous change rather than interpolation from the previous location.

members.name: CanvasLightSetBlendMode
members.signature: public void CanvasLightSetBlendMode(RID light, int mode)
members.summary: Sets the blend mode for the given canvas light to [param mode]. Equivalent to [member Light2D.blend_mode].

members.name: CanvasLightSetColor
members.signature: public void CanvasLightSetColor(RID light, Color color)
members.summary: Sets the color for a light.

members.name: CanvasLightSetEnabled
members.signature: public void CanvasLightSetEnabled(RID light, bool enabled)
members.summary: Enables or disables a canvas light.

members.name: CanvasLightSetEnergy
members.signature: public void CanvasLightSetEnergy(RID light, float energy)

members.summary: Creates a new light occluder polygon and adds it to the RenderingServer. It can be accessed with the RID that is returned. This RID will be used in all [code]canvas_occluder_polygon_*[/code] RenderingServer functions. Once finished with your RID, you will want to free the RID using the RenderingServer's [method free_rid] method. [b]Note:[/b] The equivalent resource is [OccluderPolygon2D].

members.signature: public void CanvasOccluderPolygonSetCullMode(RID occluderPolygon, int mode)

members.name: CanvasSetDisableScale
members.signature: public void CanvasSetDisableScale(bool disable)
members.summary: If [param disable] is [code]true[/code], makes 2D rendering ignore the canvas scale defined for each canvas layer. This affects [CanvasLayer]s with the [member CanvasLayer.follow_viewport_enabled] property set to [code]true[/code]. In the editor, this is set to [code]true[/code] by default, and set to [code]false[/code] when [b]View > Preview Canvas Scale[/b] is enabled at the top of the 2D editor viewport. [b]Note:[/b] Setting this to [code]true[/code] does not impact the behavior of [member CanvasLayer.scale], [member Node2D.scale], or [member Control.scale].

members.name: CanvasSetItemMirroring
members.signature: public void CanvasSetItemMirroring(RID canvas, RID item, Vector2 mirroring)
members.summary: A copy of the canvas item will be drawn with a local offset of the [param mirroring]. [b]Note:[/b] This is equivalent to calling [method canvas_set_item_repeat] like [code]canvas_set_item_repeat(item, mirroring, 1)[/code], with an additional check ensuring [param canvas] is a parent of [param item].

members.name: CanvasSetItemRepeat
members.signature: public void CanvasSetItemRepeat(RID item, Vector2 repeatSize, int repeatTimes)
members.summary: A copy of the canvas item will be drawn with a local offset of the [param repeat_size] by the number of times of the [param repeat_times]. As the [param repeat_times] increases, the copies will spread away from the origin texture.

members.summary: Sets the [member ProjectSettings.rendering/2d/shadow_atlas/size] to use for [Light2D] shadow rendering (in pixels). The value is rounded up to the nearest power of 2.

See also [method texture_2d_create]. [b]Note:[/b] The equivalent resource is [CanvasTexture] and is only meant to be used in 2D rendering, not 3D.

To place in a scene, attach this decal to an instance using [method instance_set_base] using the returned RID. [b]Note:[/b] The equivalent node is [Decal].

members.name: EnvironmentBakePanorama
members.signature: public Image EnvironmentBakePanorama(RID environment, bool bakeIrradiance, Vector2i size)
members.summary: Generates and returns an [Image] containing the radiance map for the specified [param environment] RID's sky. This supports built-in sky material and custom sky shaders. If [param bake_irradiance] is [code]true[/code], the irradiance map is saved instead of the radiance map. The radiance map is used to render reflected light, while the irradiance map is used to render ambient light. See also [method sky_bake_panorama]. [b]Note:[/b] The image is saved using linear encoding without any tonemapping performed, which means it will look too dark if viewed directly in an image editor. [b]Note:[/b] [param size] should be a 2:1 aspect ratio for the generated panorama to have square pixels. For radiance maps, there is no point in using a height greater than [member Sky.radiance_size], as it won't increase detail. Irradiance maps only contain low-frequency data, so there is usually no point in going past a size of 128×64 pixels when saving an irradiance map.

members.name: EnvironmentCreate
members.signature: public RID EnvironmentCreate()
members.summary: Creates an environment and adds it to the RenderingServer. It can be accessed with the RID that is returned. This RID will be used in all [code]environment_*[/code] RenderingServer functions. Once finished with your RID, you will want to free the RID using the RenderingServer's [method free_rid] method. [b]Note:[/b] The equivalent resource is [Environment].

members.name: EnvironmentGlowSetUseBicubicUpscale
members.signature: public void EnvironmentGlowSetUseBicubicUpscale(bool enable)
members.summary: If [param enable] is [code]true[/code], enables bicubic upscaling for glow which improves quality at the cost of performance. Equivalent to [member ProjectSettings.rendering/environment/glow/upscale_mode]. [b]Note:[/b] This setting is only effective when using the Forward+ or Mobile rendering methods, as Compatibility uses a different glow implementation.

members.name: ForceSync
members.signature: public void ForceSync()
members.summary: Forces a synchronization between the CPU and GPU, which may be required in certain cases. Only call this when needed, as CPU-GPU synchronization has a performance cost.

members.name: FreeRid
members.signature: public void FreeRid(RID rid)
members.summary: Tries to free an object in the RenderingServer. To avoid memory leaks, this should be called after using an object as memory management does not occur automatically when using RenderingServer directly.

members.name: GetCurrentRenderingDriverName
members.signature: public String GetCurrentRenderingDriverName()
members.summary: Returns the name of the current rendering driver. This can be [code]vulkan[/code], [code]d3d12[/code], [code]metal[/code], [code]opengl3[/code], [code]opengl3_es[/code], or [code]opengl3_angle[/code]. See also [method get_current_rendering_method]. When [member ProjectSettings.rendering/renderer/rendering_method] is [code]forward_plus[/code] or [code]mobile[/code], the rendering driver is determined by [member ProjectSettings.rendering/rendering_device/driver]. When [member ProjectSettings.rendering/renderer/rendering_method] is [code]gl_compatibility[/code], the rendering driver is determined by [member ProjectSettings.rendering/gl_compatibility/driver]. The rendering driver is also determined by the [code]--rendering-driver[/code] command line argument that overrides this project setting, or an automatic fallback that is applied depending on the hardware.

members.name: GetCurrentRenderingMethod
members.signature: public String GetCurrentRenderingMethod()
members.summary: Returns the name of the current rendering method. This can be [code]forward_plus[/code], [code]mobile[/code], or [code]gl_compatibility[/code]. See also [method get_current_rendering_driver_name]. The rendering method is determined by [member ProjectSettings.rendering/renderer/rendering_method], the [code]--rendering-method[/code] command line argument that overrides this project setting, or an automatic fallback that is applied depending on the hardware.

members.name: GetDefaultClearColor
members.signature: public Color GetDefaultClearColor()
members.summary: Returns the default clear color which is used when a specific clear color has not been selected. See also [method set_default_clear_color].

members.name: GetFrameSetupTimeCpu
members.signature: public float GetFrameSetupTimeCpu()
members.summary: Returns the time taken to setup rendering on the CPU in milliseconds. This value is shared across all viewports and does [i]not[/i] require [method viewport_set_measure_render_time] to be enabled on a viewport to be queried. See also [method viewport_get_measured_render_time_cpu].

members.name: GetRenderingDevice
members.signature: public RenderingDevice GetRenderingDevice()
members.summary: Returns the global RenderingDevice. [b]Note:[/b] When using the OpenGL rendering driver or when running in headless mode, this function always returns [code]null[/code].

members.name: GetRenderingInfo
members.signature: public int GetRenderingInfo(int info)
members.summary: Returns a statistic about the rendering engine which can be used for performance profiling. See also [method viewport_get_render_info], which returns information specific to a viewport. [b]Note:[/b] Only 3D rendering is currently taken into account by some of these values, such as the number of draw calls. [b]Note:[/b] Rendering information is not available until at least 2 frames have been rendered by the engine. If rendering information is not available, [method get_rendering_info] returns [code]0[/code]. To print rendering information in [code]_ready()[/code] successfully, use the following: [codeblock] func _ready(): for _i in 2: await get_tree().process_frame print(RenderingServer.get_rendering_info(RENDERING_INFO_TOTAL_DRAW_CALLS_IN_FRAME)) [/codeblock]

members.name: GetShaderParameterList
members.signature: public Dictionary[] GetShaderParameterList(RID shader)
members.summary: Returns the parameters of a shader.

members.name: GetTestCube
members.signature: public RID GetTestCube()
members.summary: Returns the RID of the test cube. This mesh will be created and returned on the first call to [method get_test_cube], then it will be cached for subsequent calls. See also [method make_sphere_mesh].

members.name: GetTestTexture
members.signature: public RID GetTestTexture()
members.summary: Returns the RID of a 256×256 texture with a testing pattern on it (in [constant Image.FORMAT_RGB8] format). This texture will be created and returned on the first call to [method get_test_texture], then it will be cached for subsequent calls. See also [method get_white_texture]. [b]Example:[/b] Get the test texture and apply it to a [Sprite2D] node: [codeblock] var texture_rid = RenderingServer.get_test_texture() var texture = ImageTexture.create_from_image(RenderingServer.texture_2d_get(texture_rid)) $Sprite2D.texture = texture [/codeblock]

members.name: GetVideoAdapterApiVersion
members.signature: public String GetVideoAdapterApiVersion()
members.summary: Returns the version of the graphics video adapter [i]currently in use[/i] (e.g. "1.2.189" for Vulkan, "3.3.0 NVIDIA 510.60.02" for OpenGL). This version may be different from the actual latest version supported by the hardware, as Godot may not always request the latest version. See also [method OS.get_video_adapter_driver_info]. [b]Note:[/b] When running a headless or server binary, this function returns an empty string.

members.name: GetVideoAdapterName
members.signature: public String GetVideoAdapterName()
members.summary: Returns the name of the video adapter (e.g. "GeForce GTX 1080/PCIe/SSE2"). [b]Note:[/b] When running a headless or server binary, this function returns an empty string. [b]Note:[/b] On the web platform, some browsers such as Firefox may report a different, fixed GPU name such as "GeForce GTX 980" (regardless of the user's actual GPU model). This is done to make fingerprinting more difficult.

members.name: GetVideoAdapterType
members.signature: public int GetVideoAdapterType()
members.summary: Returns the type of the video adapter. Since dedicated graphics cards from a given generation will [i]usually[/i] be significantly faster than integrated graphics made in the same generation, the device type can be used as a basis for automatic graphics settings adjustment. However, this is not always true, so make sure to provide users with a way to manually override graphics settings. [b]Note:[/b] When using the OpenGL rendering driver or when running in headless mode, this function always returns [constant RenderingDevice.DEVICE_TYPE_OTHER].

members.name: GetVideoAdapterVendor
members.signature: public String GetVideoAdapterVendor()
members.summary: Returns the vendor of the video adapter (e.g. "NVIDIA Corporation"). [b]Note:[/b] When running a headless or server binary, this function returns an empty string.

members.name: GetWhiteTexture
members.signature: public RID GetWhiteTexture()
members.summary: Returns the ID of a 4×4 white texture (in [constant Image.FORMAT_RGB8] format). This texture will be created and returned on the first call to [method get_white_texture], then it will be cached for subsequent calls. See also [method get_test_texture]. [b]Example:[/b] Get the white texture and apply it to a [Sprite2D] node: [codeblock] var texture_rid = RenderingServer.get_white_texture() var texture = ImageTexture.create_from_image(RenderingServer.texture_2d_get(texture_rid)) $Sprite2D.texture = texture [/codeblock]

members.name: GiSetUseHalfResolution
members.signature: public void GiSetUseHalfResolution(bool halfResolution)
members.summary: If [param half_resolution] is [code]true[/code], renders [VoxelGI] and SDFGI ([member Environment.sdfgi_enabled]) buffers at halved resolution on each axis (e.g. 960×540 when the viewport size is 1920×1080). This improves performance significantly when VoxelGI or SDFGI is enabled, at the cost of artifacts that may be visible on polygon edges. The loss in quality becomes less noticeable as the viewport resolution increases. [LightmapGI] rendering is not affected by this setting. Equivalent to [member ProjectSettings.rendering/global_illumination/gi/use_half_resolution].

members.name: GlobalShaderParameterAdd
members.signature: public void GlobalShaderParameterAdd(StringName name, int type, Variant defaultValue)
members.summary: Creates a new global shader uniform. [b]Note:[/b] Global shader parameter names are case-sensitive.

members.name: GlobalShaderParameterGet
members.signature: public Variant GlobalShaderParameterGet(StringName name)
members.summary: Returns the value of the global shader uniform specified by [param name]. [b]Note:[/b] [method global_shader_parameter_get] has a large performance penalty as the rendering thread needs to synchronize with the calling thread, which is slow. Do not use this method during gameplay to avoid stuttering. If you need to read values in a script after setting them, consider creating an autoload where you store the values you need to query at the same time you're setting them as global parameters.

members.name: GlobalShaderParameterGetList
members.signature: public StringName[] GlobalShaderParameterGetList()
members.summary: Returns the list of global shader uniform names. [b]Note:[/b] [method global_shader_parameter_get] has a large performance penalty as the rendering thread needs to synchronize with the calling thread, which is slow. Do not use this method during gameplay to avoid stuttering. If you need to read values in a script after setting them, consider creating an autoload where you store the values you need to query at the same time you're setting them as global parameters.

members.name: GlobalShaderParameterGetType
members.signature: public int GlobalShaderParameterGetType(StringName name)
members.summary: Returns the type associated to the global shader uniform specified by [param name]. [b]Note:[/b] [method global_shader_parameter_get] has a large performance penalty as the rendering thread needs to synchronize with the calling thread, which is slow. Do not use this method during gameplay to avoid stuttering. If you need to read values in a script after setting them, consider creating an autoload where you store the values you need to query at the same time you're setting them as global parameters.

members.name: GlobalShaderParameterRemove
members.signature: public void GlobalShaderParameterRemove(StringName name)
members.summary: Removes the global shader uniform specified by [param name].

members.name: GlobalShaderParameterSet
members.signature: public void GlobalShaderParameterSet(StringName name, Variant value)
members.summary: Sets the global shader uniform [param name] to [param value].

members.name: GlobalShaderParameterSetOverride
members.signature: public void GlobalShaderParameterSetOverride(StringName name, Variant value)
members.summary: Overrides the global shader uniform [param name] with [param value]. Equivalent to the [ShaderGlobalsOverride] node.

members.name: HasChanged
members.signature: public bool HasChanged()
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if changes have been made to the RenderingServer's data. [method force_draw] is usually called if this happens.

members.name: HasFeature
members.signature: public bool HasFeature(int feature)
members.summary: This method does nothing and always returns [code]false[/code].

members.name: HasOsFeature
members.signature: public bool HasOsFeature(String feature)
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if the OS supports a certain [param feature]. Features might be [code]s3tc[/code], [code]etc[/code], and [code]etc2[/code].

members.name: InstanceAttachObjectInstanceId
members.signature: public void InstanceAttachObjectInstanceId(RID instance, int id)
members.summary: Attaches a unique Object ID to instance. Object ID must be attached to instance for proper culling with [method instances_cull_aabb], [method instances_cull_convex], and [method instances_cull_ray].

members.name: InstanceAttachSkeleton
members.signature: public void InstanceAttachSkeleton(RID instance, RID skeleton)
members.summary: Attaches a skeleton to an instance. Removes the previous skeleton from the instance.

members.name: InstanceCreate
members.signature: public RID InstanceCreate()
members.summary: Creates a visual instance and adds it to the RenderingServer. It can be accessed with the RID that is returned. This RID will be used in all [code]instance_*[/code] RenderingServer functions. Once finished with your RID, you will want to free the RID using the RenderingServer's [method free_rid] method. An instance is a way of placing a 3D object in the scenario. Objects like particles, meshes, reflection probes and decals need to be associated with an instance to be visible in the scenario using [method instance_set_base]. [b]Note:[/b] The equivalent node is [VisualInstance3D].

members.name: InstanceCreate2
members.signature: public RID InstanceCreate2(RID @base, RID scenario)
members.summary: Creates a visual instance, adds it to the RenderingServer, and sets both base and scenario. It can be accessed with the RID that is returned. This RID will be used in all [code]instance_*[/code] RenderingServer functions. Once finished with your RID, you will want to free the RID using the RenderingServer's [method free_rid] method. This is a shorthand for using [method instance_create] and setting the base and scenario manually.

members.name: InstanceGeometryGetShaderParameter
members.signature: public Variant InstanceGeometryGetShaderParameter(RID instance, StringName parameter)
members.summary: Returns the value of the per-instance shader uniform from the specified 3D geometry instance. Equivalent to [method GeometryInstance3D.get_instance_shader_parameter]. [b]Note:[/b] Per-instance shader parameter names are case-sensitive.

members.name: InstanceGeometryGetShaderParameterDefaultValue
members.signature: public Variant InstanceGeometryGetShaderParameterDefaultValue(RID instance, StringName parameter)
members.summary: Returns the default value of the per-instance shader uniform from the specified 3D geometry instance. Equivalent to [method GeometryInstance3D.get_instance_shader_parameter].

members.name: InstanceGeometryGetShaderParameterList
members.signature: public Dictionary[] InstanceGeometryGetShaderParameterList(RID instance)
members.summary: Returns a dictionary of per-instance shader uniform names of the per-instance shader uniform from the specified 3D geometry instance. The returned dictionary is in PropertyInfo format, with the keys [code]name[/code], [code]class_name[/code], [code]type[/code], [code]hint[/code], [code]hint_string[/code] and [code]usage[/code]. Equivalent to [method GeometryInstance3D.get_instance_shader_parameter].

members.name: InstanceGeometrySetCastShadowsSetting
members.signature: public void InstanceGeometrySetCastShadowsSetting(RID instance, int shadowCastingSetting)
members.summary: Sets the shadow casting setting. Equivalent to [member GeometryInstance3D.cast_shadow].

members.name: InstanceGeometrySetFlag
members.signature: public void InstanceGeometrySetFlag(RID instance, int flag, bool enabled)
members.summary: Sets the [param flag] for a given [param instance] to [param enabled].

members.name: InstanceGeometrySetLightmap
members.signature: public void InstanceGeometrySetLightmap(RID instance, RID lightmap, Rect2 lightmapUvScale, int lightmapSlice)
members.summary: Sets the lightmap GI instance to use for the specified 3D geometry instance. The lightmap UV scale for the specified instance (equivalent to [member GeometryInstance3D.gi_lightmap_scale]) and lightmap atlas slice must also be specified.

members.name: InstanceGeometrySetLodBias
members.signature: public void InstanceGeometrySetLodBias(RID instance, float lodBias)
members.summary: Sets the level of detail bias to use when rendering the specified 3D geometry instance. Higher values result in higher detail from further away. Equivalent to [member GeometryInstance3D.lod_bias].

members.name: InstanceGeometrySetMaterialOverlay
members.signature: public void InstanceGeometrySetMaterialOverlay(RID instance, RID material)
members.summary: Sets a material that will be rendered for all surfaces on top of active materials for the mesh associated with this instance. Equivalent to [member GeometryInstance3D.material_overlay].

members.name: InstanceGeometrySetMaterialOverride
members.signature: public void InstanceGeometrySetMaterialOverride(RID instance, RID material)
members.summary: Sets a material that will override the material for all surfaces on the mesh associated with this instance. Equivalent to [member GeometryInstance3D.material_override].

members.name: InstanceGeometrySetShaderParameter
members.signature: public void InstanceGeometrySetShaderParameter(RID instance, StringName parameter, Variant value)
members.summary: Sets the per-instance shader uniform on the specified 3D geometry instance. Equivalent to [method GeometryInstance3D.set_instance_shader_parameter].

members.name: InstanceGeometrySetTransparency
members.signature: public void InstanceGeometrySetTransparency(RID instance, float transparency)
members.summary: Sets the transparency for the given geometry instance. Equivalent to [member GeometryInstance3D.transparency]. A transparency of [code]0.0[/code] is fully opaque, while [code]1.0[/code] is fully transparent. Values greater than [code]0.0[/code] (exclusive) will force the geometry's materials to go through the transparent pipeline, which is slower to render and can exhibit rendering issues due to incorrect transparency sorting. However, unlike using a transparent material, setting [param transparency] to a value greater than [code]0.0[/code] (exclusive) will [i]not[/i] disable shadow rendering. In spatial shaders, [code]1.0 - transparency[/code] is set as the default value of the [code]ALPHA[/code] built-in. [b]Note:[/b] [param transparency] is clamped between [code]0.0[/code] and [code]1.0[/code], so this property cannot be used to make transparent materials more opaque than they originally are.

members.name: InstanceGeometrySetVisibilityRange
members.signature: public void InstanceGeometrySetVisibilityRange(RID instance, float min, float max, float minMargin, float maxMargin, int fadeMode)
members.summary: Sets the visibility range values for the given geometry instance. Equivalent to [member GeometryInstance3D.visibility_range_begin] and related properties.

members.name: InstanceSetBase
members.signature: public void InstanceSetBase(RID instance, RID @base)
members.summary: Sets the base of the instance. A base can be any of the 3D objects that are created in the RenderingServer that can be displayed. For example, any of the light types, mesh, multimesh, particle system, reflection probe, decal, lightmap, voxel GI and visibility notifiers are all types that can be set as the base of an instance in order to be displayed in the scenario.

members.name: InstanceSetBlendShapeWeight
members.signature: public void InstanceSetBlendShapeWeight(RID instance, int shape, float weight)
members.summary: Sets the weight for a given blend shape associated with this instance.

members.name: InstanceSetCustomAabb
members.signature: public void InstanceSetCustomAabb(RID instance, AABB aabb)
members.summary: Sets a custom AABB to use when culling objects from the view frustum. Equivalent to setting [member GeometryInstance3D.custom_aabb].

members.name: InstanceSetExtraVisibilityMargin
members.signature: public void InstanceSetExtraVisibilityMargin(RID instance, float margin)
members.summary: Sets a margin to increase the size of the AABB when culling objects from the view frustum. This allows you to avoid culling objects that fall outside the view frustum. Equivalent to [member GeometryInstance3D.extra_cull_margin].

members.name: InstanceSetIgnoreCulling
members.signature: public void InstanceSetIgnoreCulling(RID instance, bool enabled)
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], ignores all culling on the specified 3D geometry instance, including frustum culling, occlusion culling, and layer culling. This is not the same as [member GeometryInstance3D.ignore_occlusion_culling], which only ignores occlusion culling but leaves frustum and layer culling intact.

members.name: InstanceSetLayerMask
members.signature: public void InstanceSetLayerMask(RID instance, int mask)
members.summary: Sets the render layers that this instance will be drawn to. Equivalent to [member VisualInstance3D.layers].

members.name: InstanceSetPivotData
members.signature: public void InstanceSetPivotData(RID instance, float sortingOffset, bool useAabbCenter)
members.summary: Sets the sorting offset and switches between using the bounding box or instance origin for depth sorting.

members.name: InstanceSetScenario
members.signature: public void InstanceSetScenario(RID instance, RID scenario)
members.summary: Sets the scenario that the instance is in. The scenario is the 3D world that the objects will be displayed in.

members.name: InstanceSetSurfaceOverrideMaterial
members.signature: public void InstanceSetSurfaceOverrideMaterial(RID instance, int surface, RID material)
members.summary: Sets the override material of a specific surface. Equivalent to [method MeshInstance3D.set_surface_override_material].

members.name: InstanceSetTransform
members.signature: public void InstanceSetTransform(RID instance, Transform3D transform)
members.summary: Sets the world space transform of the instance. Equivalent to [member Node3D.global_transform].

members.name: InstanceSetVisibilityParent
members.signature: public void InstanceSetVisibilityParent(RID instance, RID parent)
members.summary: Sets the visibility parent for the given instance. Equivalent to [member Node3D.visibility_parent].

members.name: InstanceSetVisible
members.signature: public void InstanceSetVisible(RID instance, bool visible)
members.summary: Sets whether an instance is drawn or not. Equivalent to [member Node3D.visible].

members.name: InstanceTeleport
members.signature: public void InstanceTeleport(RID instance)
members.summary: Resets motion vectors and other interpolated values. Use this [i]after[/i] teleporting a mesh from one position to another to avoid ghosting artifacts.

members.name: InstancesCullAabb
members.signature: public PackedInt64Array InstancesCullAabb(AABB aabb, RID scenario)
members.summary: Returns an array of object IDs intersecting with the provided AABB. Only 3D nodes that inherit from [VisualInstance3D] are considered, such as [MeshInstance3D] or [DirectionalLight3D]. Use [method @GlobalScope.instance_from_id] to obtain the actual nodes. A scenario RID must be provided, which is available in the [World3D] you want to query. This forces an update for all resources queued to update. [b]Warning:[/b] This function is primarily intended for editor usage. For in-game use cases, prefer physics collision.

members.name: InstancesCullConvex
members.signature: public PackedInt64Array InstancesCullConvex(Plane[] convex, RID scenario)
members.summary: Returns an array of object IDs intersecting with the provided convex shape. Only 3D nodes that inherit from [VisualInstance3D] are considered, such as [MeshInstance3D] or [DirectionalLight3D]. Use [method @GlobalScope.instance_from_id] to obtain the actual nodes. A scenario RID must be provided, which is available in the [World3D] you want to query. This forces an update for all resources queued to update. [b]Warning:[/b] This function is primarily intended for editor usage. For in-game use cases, prefer physics collision.

members.name: InstancesCullRay
members.signature: public PackedInt64Array InstancesCullRay(Vector3 from, Vector3 to, RID scenario)
members.summary: Returns an array of object IDs intersecting with the provided 3D ray. Only 3D nodes that inherit from [VisualInstance3D] are considered, such as [MeshInstance3D] or [DirectionalLight3D]. Use [method @GlobalScope.instance_from_id] to obtain the actual nodes. A scenario RID must be provided, which is available in the [World3D] you want to query. This forces an update for all resources queued to update. [b]Warning:[/b] This function is primarily intended for editor usage. For in-game use cases, prefer physics collision.

members.name: IsOnRenderThread
members.signature: public bool IsOnRenderThread()
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if our code is currently executing on the rendering thread.

members.name: LightAreaSetNormalizeEnergy
members.signature: public void LightAreaSetNormalizeEnergy(RID light, bool enable)
members.summary: Defines whether the energy of an [AreaLight3D] is normalized (divided) by its area. If set to [code]true[/code], changing the size does not affect the total energy output. Equivalent to [member AreaLight3D.area_normalize_energy].

members.name: LightAreaSetSize
members.signature: public void LightAreaSetSize(RID light, Vector2 size)
members.summary: Sets the extents (width and height) in meters for this area light. Equivalent to [member AreaLight3D.area_size].

members.name: LightDirectionalSetBlendSplits
members.signature: public void LightDirectionalSetBlendSplits(RID light, bool enable)
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], this directional light will blend between shadow map splits resulting in a smoother transition between them. Equivalent to [member DirectionalLight3D.directional_shadow_blend_splits].

members.name: LightDirectionalSetShadowMode
members.signature: public void LightDirectionalSetShadowMode(RID light, int mode)
members.summary: Sets the shadow mode for this directional light. Equivalent to [member DirectionalLight3D.directional_shadow_mode].

members.name: LightDirectionalSetSkyMode
members.signature: public void LightDirectionalSetSkyMode(RID light, int mode)
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], this light will not be used for anything except sky shaders. Use this for lights that impact your sky shader that you may want to hide from affecting the rest of the scene. For example, you may want to enable this when the sun in your sky shader falls below the horizon.

members.name: LightOmniSetShadowMode
members.signature: public void LightOmniSetShadowMode(RID light, int mode)
members.summary: Sets whether to use a dual paraboloid or a cubemap for the shadow map. Dual paraboloid is faster but may suffer from artifacts. Equivalent to [member OmniLight3D.omni_shadow_mode].

members.name: LightProjectorsSetFilter
members.signature: public void LightProjectorsSetFilter(int filter)
members.summary: Sets the texture filter mode to use when rendering light projectors. This parameter is global and cannot be set on a per-light basis.

members.name: LightSetBakeMode
members.signature: public void LightSetBakeMode(RID light, int bakeMode)
members.summary: Sets the bake mode to use for the specified 3D light. Equivalent to [member Light3D.light_bake_mode].

members.name: LightSetColor
members.signature: public void LightSetColor(RID light, Color color)
members.summary: Sets the color of the light. Equivalent to [member Light3D.light_color].

members.name: LightSetCullMask
members.signature: public void LightSetCullMask(RID light, int mask)
members.summary: Sets the cull mask for this 3D light. Lights only affect objects in the selected layers. Equivalent to [member Light3D.light_cull_mask].

members.name: LightSetDistanceFade
members.signature: public void LightSetDistanceFade(RID decal, bool enabled, float begin, float shadow, float length)
members.summary: Sets the distance fade for this 3D light. This acts as a form of level of detail (LOD) and can be used to improve performance. Equivalent to [member Light3D.distance_fade_enabled], [member Light3D.distance_fade_begin], [member Light3D.distance_fade_shadow], and [member Light3D.distance_fade_length].

members.name: LightSetMaxSdfgiCascade
members.signature: public void LightSetMaxSdfgiCascade(RID light, int cascade)
members.summary: Sets the maximum SDFGI cascade in which the 3D light's indirect lighting is rendered. Higher values allow the light to be rendered in SDFGI further away from the camera.

members.name: LightSetNegative
members.signature: public void LightSetNegative(RID light, bool enable)
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the 3D light will subtract light instead of adding light. Equivalent to [member Light3D.light_negative].

members.name: LightSetParam
members.signature: public void LightSetParam(RID light, int param, float value)
members.summary: Sets the specified 3D light parameter. Equivalent to [method Light3D.set_param].

members.name: LightSetProjector
members.signature: public void LightSetProjector(RID light, RID texture)
members.summary: Sets the projector texture to use for the specified 3D light. Equivalent to [member Light3D.light_projector].

members.name: LightSetReverseCullFaceMode
members.signature: public void LightSetReverseCullFaceMode(RID light, bool enabled)
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], reverses the backface culling of the mesh. This can be useful when you have a flat mesh that has a light behind it. If you need to cast a shadow on both sides of the mesh, set the mesh to use double-sided shadows with [method instance_geometry_set_cast_shadows_setting]. Equivalent to [member Light3D.shadow_reverse_cull_face].

members.name: LightSetShadow
members.signature: public void LightSetShadow(RID light, bool enabled)
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], light will cast shadows. Equivalent to [member Light3D.shadow_enabled].

members.name: LightSetShadowCasterMask
members.signature: public void LightSetShadowCasterMask(RID light, int mask)
members.summary: Sets the shadow caster mask for this 3D light. Shadows will only be cast using objects in the selected layers. Equivalent to [member Light3D.shadow_caster_mask].

members.name: LightmapCreate
members.signature: public RID LightmapCreate()
members.summary: Creates a new lightmap global illumination instance and adds it to the RenderingServer. It can be accessed with the RID that is returned. This RID will be used in all [code]lightmap_*[/code] RenderingServer functions. Once finished with your RID, you will want to free the RID using the RenderingServer's [method free_rid] method. [b]Note:[/b] The equivalent node is [LightmapGI].

members.name: LightmapGetProbeCaptureBspTree
members.signature: public PackedInt32Array LightmapGetProbeCaptureBspTree(RID lightmap)
members.summary: Returns the BSP tree data used for accelerating probe lookups. The BSP data is structured as a series of six signed 32-bit values per BSP node in this order: [code]float plane_x[/code], [code]float plane_y[/code], [code]float plane_z[/code], [code]float plane_distance[/code], [code]int32_t over[/code], [code]int32_t under[/code]. An empty leaf is denoted by the value [code]-2147483648[/code] (the minimum 32-bit signed integer). See also [method lightmap_set_probe_capture_data].

members.name: LightmapGetProbeCapturePoints
members.signature: public PackedVector3Array LightmapGetProbeCapturePoints(RID lightmap)
members.summary: Returns the [i]local space[/i] positions of each lightmap probe capture point. Keep in mind the lightmap instance may have a non-zero transform, which will affect the position of the probe capture points. See also [method lightmap_set_probe_capture_data].

members.name: LightmapGetProbeCaptureSh
members.signature: public PackedColorArray LightmapGetProbeCaptureSh(RID lightmap)
members.summary: Returns the L0, L1, and L2 [url=https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Spherical_harmonics]spherical harmonics[/url] data for each lightmap probe capture point. This is specified as 9 [Color] values per probe, which means the size of the returned data is always 9 times the number of probe points. See also [method lightmap_set_probe_capture_data].

members.name: LightmapGetProbeCaptureTetrahedra
members.signature: public PackedInt32Array LightmapGetProbeCaptureTetrahedra(RID lightmap)
members.summary: Returns the tetrahedralization data used for interpolating between lightmap probe capture points. Each tetrahedron is specified as a series of 4 numbers, each being an index into the probe capture points array returned by [method lightmap_get_probe_capture_points]. See also [method lightmap_set_probe_capture_data].

members.name: LightmapSetBakedExposureNormalization
members.signature: public void LightmapSetBakedExposureNormalization(RID lightmap, float bakedExposure)
members.summary: Used to inform the renderer what exposure normalization value was used while baking the lightmap. This value will be used and modulated at run time to ensure that the lightmap maintains a consistent level of exposure even if the scene-wide exposure normalization is changed at run time. For more information see [method camera_attributes_set_exposure].

members.name: LightmapSetProbeBounds
members.signature: public void LightmapSetProbeBounds(RID lightmap, AABB bounds)
members.summary: Sets the bounds that this lightmap instance should visually affect, both in terms of static lightmap baking and probe-based global illumination.

members.name: LightmapSetProbeCaptureData
members.signature: public void LightmapSetProbeCaptureData(RID lightmap, PackedVector3Array points, PackedColorArray pointSh, PackedInt32Array tetrahedra, PackedInt32Array bspTree)
members.summary: Sets the probe capture data for the given lightmap instance. See [method lightmap_get_probe_capture_points], [method lightmap_get_probe_capture_sh], [method lightmap_get_probe_capture_tetrahedra], and [method lightmap_get_probe_capture_bsp_tree] for the expected data formats.

members.name: LightmapSetProbeCaptureUpdateSpeed
members.signature: public void LightmapSetProbeCaptureUpdateSpeed(float speed)
members.summary: The framerate-independent update speed when representing dynamic object lighting from [LightmapProbe]s. Higher values make dynamic object lighting update faster. Higher values can prevent fast-moving objects from having "outdated" indirect lighting displayed on them, at the cost of possible flickering when an object moves from a bright area to a shaded area. See also [member ProjectSettings.rendering/lightmapping/probe_capture/update_speed].

members.name: LightmapSetProbeInterior
members.signature: public void LightmapSetProbeInterior(RID lightmap, bool interior)
members.summary: Sets whether the lightmap instance should be considered as interior (when [param interior] is [code]true[/code]). If the lightmap is marked as interior, environment lighting is ignored when baking lightmaps.

members.name: LightmapSetTextures
members.signature: public void LightmapSetTextures(RID lightmap, RID light, bool usesSh)
members.summary: Set the textures on the given [param lightmap] GI instance to the texture array pointed to by the [param light] RID. If the lightmap texture was baked with [member LightmapGI.directional] set to [code]true[/code], then [param uses_sh] must also be [code]true[/code].

members.name: LightmapsSetBicubicFilter
members.signature: public void LightmapsSetBicubicFilter(bool enable)
members.summary: Toggles whether a bicubic filter should be used when lightmaps are sampled. This smoothens their appearance at a performance cost.

members.name: MakeSphereMesh
members.signature: public RID MakeSphereMesh(int latitudes, int longitudes, float radius)
members.summary: Returns a mesh of a sphere with the given number of horizontal subdivisions, vertical subdivisions and radius. See also [method get_test_cube].

members.name: MaterialCreate
members.signature: public RID MaterialCreate()
members.summary: Creates an empty material and adds it to the RenderingServer. It can be accessed with the RID that is returned. This RID will be used in all [code]material_*[/code] RenderingServer functions. Once finished with your RID, you will want to free the RID using the RenderingServer's [method free_rid] method. [b]Note:[/b] The equivalent resource is [Material].

members.name: MaterialGetParam
members.signature: public Variant MaterialGetParam(RID material, StringName parameter)
members.summary: Returns the value of a certain material's parameter.

members.name: MaterialSetNextPass
members.signature: public void MaterialSetNextPass(RID material, RID nextMaterial)
members.summary: Sets an object's next material.

members.name: MaterialSetParam
members.signature: public void MaterialSetParam(RID material, StringName parameter, Variant value)
members.summary: Sets a material's parameter.

members.name: MaterialSetRenderPriority
members.signature: public void MaterialSetRenderPriority(RID material, int priority)
members.summary: Sets a material's render priority.

members.name: MaterialSetShader
members.signature: public void MaterialSetShader(RID shaderMaterial, RID shader)
members.summary: Sets a shader material's shader.

members.name: MaterialSetUseDebanding
members.signature: public void MaterialSetUseDebanding(bool enable)
members.summary: When using the Mobile renderer, [method material_set_use_debanding] can be used to enable or disable the debanding feature of 3D materials ([BaseMaterial3D] and [ShaderMaterial]). [method material_set_use_debanding] has no effect when using the Compatibility or Forward+ renderer. In Forward+, [Viewport] debanding can be used instead. See also [member ProjectSettings.rendering/anti_aliasing/quality/use_debanding] and [method RenderingServer.viewport_set_use_debanding].

members.name: MeshAddSurface
members.signature: public void MeshAddSurface(RID mesh, Dictionary surface)
members.summary: Creates a new surface on the given [param mesh]. Equivalent to [method mesh_add_surface_from_arrays], but takes a single [Dictionary] argument instead of separate arguments. The dictionary must follow this structure: [codeblock] { # Required: "primitive": RenderingServer.PrimitiveType, "format": RenderingServer.ArrayFormat, "vertex_data": PackedByteArray, "vertex_count": int, "aabb": AABB, # Optional: "attribute_data": PackedByteArray, "skin_data": PackedByteArray, "index_data": PackedByteArray, "index_count": int, # Required if `index_data` is specified. "uv_scale": Vector4, "lods": [ # Both values are required for each LOD level. { "edge_length": float, "index_data": PackedByteArray, }, ], "bone_aabbs": Array[AABB], "blend_shape_data": PackedByteArray, "material": Material, } [/codeblock] See also [method mesh_get_surface], which returns data in the same structure defined above.

members.name: MeshAddSurfaceFromArrays
members.signature: public void MeshAddSurfaceFromArrays(RID mesh, int primitive, Array arrays, Array blendShapes, Dictionary lods, int compressFormat)
members.summary: Creates a new surface on the given [param mesh]. [method mesh_get_surface_count] will become the surface index for this new surface. Surfaces are created to be rendered using a [param primitive], which may be any of the values defined in [enum Mesh.PrimitiveType]. The [param arrays] argument is an array of arrays. Each of the [constant Mesh.ARRAY_MAX] elements contains an array with some of the mesh data for this surface as described by the corresponding member of [enum Mesh.ArrayType] or [code]null[/code] if it is not used by the surface. For example, [code]arrays[0][/code] is the array of vertices. That first vertex sub-array is always required; the others are optional. Adding an index array puts this surface into "index mode" where the vertex and other arrays become the sources of data and the index array defines the vertex order. All sub-arrays must have the same length as the vertex array (or be an exact multiple of the vertex array's length, when multiple elements of a sub-array correspond to a single vertex) or be empty, except for [constant Mesh.ARRAY_INDEX] if it is used. The [param blend_shapes] argument is an array of vertex data for each blend shape. Each element is an array of the same structure as [param arrays], but [constant Mesh.ARRAY_VERTEX], [constant Mesh.ARRAY_NORMAL], and [constant Mesh.ARRAY_TANGENT] are set if and only if they are set in [param arrays] and all other entries are [code]null[/code]. The [param lods] argument is a dictionary with [float] keys and [PackedInt32Array] values. Each entry in the dictionary represents an LOD level of the surface, where the value is the [constant Mesh.ARRAY_INDEX] array to use for the LOD level and the key is roughly proportional to the distance at which the LOD stats being used. I.e., increasing the key of an LOD also increases the distance that the objects has to be from the camera before the LOD is used. The [param compress_format] argument is the bitwise OR of, as required: One value of [enum ArrayFormat] left shifted by [code]ARRAY_FORMAT_CUSTOMn_SHIFT[/code] for each custom channel in use, [constant ARRAY_FLAG_USE_DYNAMIC_UPDATE], [constant ARRAY_FLAG_USE_8_BONE_WEIGHTS], or [constant ARRAY_FLAG_USES_EMPTY_VERTEX_ARRAY]. See [method ArrayMesh.add_surface_from_arrays] and [method ImporterMesh.add_surface] for higher-level equivalents of this method. [b]Note:[/b] When using indices, it is recommended to only use points, lines, or triangles.

members.name: MeshClear
members.signature: public void MeshClear(RID mesh)
members.summary: Removes all surfaces from a mesh.

members.name: MeshCreate
members.signature: public RID MeshCreate()
members.summary: Creates a new mesh and adds it to the RenderingServer. It can be accessed with the RID that is returned. This RID will be used in all [code]mesh_*[/code] RenderingServer functions. Once finished with your RID, you will want to free the RID using the RenderingServer's [method free_rid] method. To place in a scene, attach this mesh to an instance using [method instance_set_base] using the returned RID. [b]Note:[/b] The equivalent resource is [Mesh].

members.name: MeshCreateFromSurfaces
members.signature: public RID MeshCreateFromSurfaces(Dictionary[] surfaces, int blendShapeCount)
members.summary: Creates a new mesh with predefined surfaces for it and adds the mesh to the RenderingServer. It can be accessed with the RID that is returned. This RID will be used in all [code]mesh_*[/code] RenderingServer functions. This method is more efficient for creating meshes with multiple surfaces compared to creating an empty mesh with [method mesh_create] and adding surfaces one by one with [method mesh_add_surface]. Each element in the [param surfaces] array must follow the same structure as described in [method mesh_add_surface]. The [param blend_shape_count] parameter must match the blend shape data defined in all surfaces. Once finished with your RID, you will want to free the RID using the RenderingServer's [method free_rid] method. To place in a scene, attach this mesh to an instance using [method instance_set_base] using the returned RID. [b]Note:[/b] The equivalent resource is [Mesh].

members.name: MeshGetBlendShapeCount
members.signature: public int MeshGetBlendShapeCount(RID mesh)
members.summary: Returns a mesh's blend shape count.

members.name: MeshGetBlendShapeMode
members.signature: public int MeshGetBlendShapeMode(RID mesh)
members.summary: Returns a mesh's blend shape mode.

members.name: MeshGetCustomAabb
members.signature: public AABB MeshGetCustomAabb(RID mesh)
members.summary: Returns a mesh's custom aabb.

members.name: MeshGetSurface
members.signature: public Dictionary MeshGetSurface(RID mesh, int surface)
members.summary: Returns a mesh's surface as a dictionary following the same structure as described in [method mesh_add_surface].

members.name: MeshGetSurfaceCount
members.signature: public int MeshGetSurfaceCount(RID mesh)
