members.signature: public void MeshSetCustomAabb(RID mesh, AABB aabb)
members.summary: Sets a mesh's custom aabb.

members.name: MeshSetShadowMesh
members.signature: public void MeshSetShadowMesh(RID mesh, RID shadowMesh)
members.summary: Sets an optional second mesh which can be used for rendering shadows and the depth prepass. Can be used to increase performance by supplying a mesh with fused vertices and only vertex position data (without normals, UVs, colors, etc.). [b]Note:[/b] This mesh must have exactly the same vertex positions as the source mesh (including the source mesh's LODs, if present). If vertex positions differ, then the mesh will not draw correctly.

members.name: MeshSurfaceGetArrays
members.signature: public Array MeshSurfaceGetArrays(RID mesh, int surface)
members.summary: Returns a mesh's surface's buffer arrays.

members.name: MeshSurfaceGetBlendShapeArrays
members.signature: public Array[] MeshSurfaceGetBlendShapeArrays(RID mesh, int surface)
members.summary: Returns a mesh's surface's arrays for blend shapes.

members.name: MeshSurfaceGetFormatAttributeStride
members.signature: public int MeshSurfaceGetFormatAttributeStride(int format, int vertexCount)
members.summary: Returns the stride of the attribute buffer for a mesh with given [param format].

members.name: MeshSurfaceGetFormatIndexStride
members.signature: public int MeshSurfaceGetFormatIndexStride(int format, int vertexCount)
members.summary: Returns the stride of the index buffer for a mesh with the given [param format].

members.name: MeshSurfaceGetFormatNormalTangentStride
members.signature: public int MeshSurfaceGetFormatNormalTangentStride(int format, int vertexCount)
members.summary: Returns the stride of the combined normals and tangents for a mesh with given [param format]. Note importantly that, while normals and tangents are in the vertex buffer with vertices, they are only interleaved with each other and so have a different stride than vertex positions.

members.name: MeshSurfaceGetFormatOffset
members.signature: public int MeshSurfaceGetFormatOffset(int format, int vertexCount, int arrayIndex)
members.summary: Returns the offset of a given attribute by [param array_index] in the start of its respective buffer.

members.name: MeshSurfaceGetFormatSkinStride
members.signature: public int MeshSurfaceGetFormatSkinStride(int format, int vertexCount)
members.summary: Returns the stride of the skin buffer for a mesh with given [param format].

members.name: MeshSurfaceGetFormatVertexStride
members.signature: public int MeshSurfaceGetFormatVertexStride(int format, int vertexCount)
members.summary: Returns the stride of the vertex positions for a mesh with given [param format]. Note importantly that vertex positions are stored consecutively and are not interleaved with the other attributes in the vertex buffer (normals and tangents).

members.name: MeshSurfaceGetMaterial
members.signature: public RID MeshSurfaceGetMaterial(RID mesh, int surface)
members.summary: Returns a mesh's surface's material.

members.name: MeshSurfaceRemove
members.signature: public void MeshSurfaceRemove(RID mesh, int surface)
members.summary: Removes the surface at the given index from the Mesh, shifting surfaces with higher index down by one.

members.name: MeshSurfaceSetMaterial
members.signature: public void MeshSurfaceSetMaterial(RID mesh, int surface, RID material)
members.summary: Sets a mesh's surface's material.

members.name: MeshSurfaceUpdateAttributeRegion
members.signature: public void MeshSurfaceUpdateAttributeRegion(RID mesh, int surface, int offset, PackedByteArray data)
members.summary: Updates the attribute buffer of the mesh surface with the given [param data]. The expected data per attribute is 8 or 12 bytes (4 bytes per float, 2 floats per [Vector2], and 3 floats per [Vector3]) depending on if the mesh is using [Vector2] or [Vector3] vertices. This value can be determined with [method mesh_surface_get_format_attribute_stride] instead. The starting point of the updates can be changed with [param offset]. The value of [param offset] should be a multiple of 12 bytes in most cases to align to each attribute. A [PackedVector3Array] of attribute locations can be converted into a [PackedByteArray] using [method PackedVector3Array.to_byte_array] for use in [param data].

members.name: MeshSurfaceUpdateIndexRegion
members.signature: public void MeshSurfaceUpdateIndexRegion(RID mesh, int surface, int offset, PackedByteArray data)
members.summary: Updates the index buffer of the mesh surface with the given [param data]. The expected data are 16 or 32-bit unsigned integers, which can be determined with [method mesh_surface_get_format_index_stride].

members.name: MeshSurfaceUpdateSkinRegion
members.signature: public void MeshSurfaceUpdateSkinRegion(RID mesh, int surface, int offset, PackedByteArray data)
members.summary: Updates the skin buffer of the mesh surface with the given [param data]. The expected data per skin is 8 or 12 bytes (4 bytes per float, 2 floats per [Vector2], and 3 floats per [Vector3]) depending on if the mesh is using [Vector2] or [Vector3] vertices. This value can be determined with [method mesh_surface_get_format_skin_stride] instead. The starting point of the updates can be changed with [param offset]. The value of [param offset] should be a multiple of 12 bytes in most cases to align to each skin. A [PackedVector3Array] of skin locations can be converted into a [PackedByteArray] using [method PackedVector3Array.to_byte_array] for use in [param data].

members.name: MeshSurfaceUpdateVertexRegion
members.signature: public void MeshSurfaceUpdateVertexRegion(RID mesh, int surface, int offset, PackedByteArray data)
members.summary: Updates the vertex buffer of the mesh surface with the given [param data]. The expected data per vertex is 8 or 12 bytes (4 bytes per float, 2 floats per [Vector2], and 3 floats per [Vector3]) depending on if the mesh is using [Vector2] or [Vector3] vertices. This value can be determined with [method mesh_surface_get_format_vertex_stride] instead. The starting point of the updates can be changed with [param offset]. The value of [param offset] should be a multiple of 12 bytes in most cases to align to each vertex. A [PackedVector3Array] of vertex locations can be converted into a [PackedByteArray] using [method PackedVector3Array.to_byte_array] for use in [param data].

members.name: MultimeshAllocateData
members.signature: public void MultimeshAllocateData(RID multimesh, int instances, int transformFormat, bool colorFormat, bool customDataFormat, bool useIndirect)
members.summary: Sets up the multimesh using the specified data. The number of instances is set by [param instances]. The format of the instance transforms is set by [param transform_format], which should be set according to whether the multimesh is meant to be rendered in 2D or 3D. If [param color_format] is [code]true[/code], each instance will have a color associated with it. If [param custom_data_format] is [code]true[/code], each instance will have a custom data vector associated with it. If [param use_indirect] is [code]true[/code], an indirect command buffer will be created for this multimesh, allowing the instance count to be modified directly on the GPU. See also [method multimesh_get_command_buffer_rd_rid].

members.name: MultimeshCreate
members.signature: public RID MultimeshCreate()
members.summary: Creates a new multimesh on the RenderingServer and returns an [RID] handle. This RID will be used in all [code]multimesh_*[/code] RenderingServer functions. Once finished with your RID, you will want to free the RID using the RenderingServer's [method free_rid] method. To place in a scene, attach this multimesh to an instance using [method instance_set_base] using the returned RID. [b]Note:[/b] The equivalent resource is [MultiMesh].

members.name: MultimeshGetAabb
members.signature: public AABB MultimeshGetAabb(RID multimesh)
members.summary: Calculates and returns the axis-aligned bounding box that encloses all instances within the multimesh.

members.name: MultimeshGetBuffer
members.signature: public PackedFloat32Array MultimeshGetBuffer(RID multimesh)
members.summary: Returns the MultiMesh data (such as instance transforms, colors, etc.). See [method multimesh_set_buffer] for details on the returned data. [b]Note:[/b] If the buffer is in the engine's internal cache, it will have to be fetched from GPU memory and possibly decompressed. This means [method multimesh_get_buffer] is potentially a slow operation and should be avoided whenever possible.

members.name: MultimeshGetBufferRdRid
members.signature: public RID MultimeshGetBufferRdRid(RID multimesh)
members.summary: Returns the [RenderingDevice] [RID] handle of the [MultiMesh], which can be used as any other buffer on the Rendering Device.

members.name: MultimeshGetCommandBufferRdRid
members.signature: public RID MultimeshGetCommandBufferRdRid(RID multimesh)
members.summary: Returns the [RenderingDevice] [RID] handle of the [MultiMesh] command buffer. This [RID] is only valid if [code]use_indirect[/code] is set to [code]true[/code] when allocating data through [method multimesh_allocate_data]. It can be used to directly modify the instance count via buffer. The data structure is dependent on both how many surfaces the mesh contains and whether it is indexed or not, the buffer has 5 integers in it, with the last unused if the mesh is not indexed. Each of the values in the buffer correspond to these options: [codeblock lang=text] Indexed: 0 - indexCount; 1 - instanceCount; 2 - firstIndex; 3 - vertexOffset; 4 - firstInstance; Non-indexed: 0 - vertexCount; 1 - instanceCount; 2 - firstVertex; 3 - firstInstance; 4 - unused; [/codeblock]

members.name: MultimeshGetCustomAabb
members.signature: public AABB MultimeshGetCustomAabb(RID multimesh)
members.summary: Returns the custom AABB defined for this MultiMesh resource.

members.name: MultimeshGetInstanceCount
members.signature: public int MultimeshGetInstanceCount(RID multimesh)
members.summary: Returns the number of instances allocated for this multimesh.

members.name: MultimeshGetMesh
members.signature: public RID MultimeshGetMesh(RID multimesh)
members.summary: Returns the RID of the mesh that will be used in drawing this multimesh.

members.name: MultimeshGetVisibleInstances

members.summary: Returns the number of visible instances for this multimesh.

members.signature: public Color MultimeshInstanceGetColor(RID multimesh, int index)
members.summary: Returns the color by which the specified instance will be modulated.

members.name: MultimeshInstanceGetCustomData
members.signature: public Color MultimeshInstanceGetCustomData(RID multimesh, int index)
members.summary: Returns the custom data associated with the specified instance.

members.name: MultimeshInstanceGetTransform
members.signature: public Transform3D MultimeshInstanceGetTransform(RID multimesh, int index)
members.summary: Returns the [Transform3D] of the specified instance.

members.signature: public Transform2D MultimeshInstanceGetTransform2d(RID multimesh, int index)
members.summary: Returns the [Transform2D] of the specified instance. For use when the multimesh is set to use 2D transforms.

members.name: MultimeshInstanceResetPhysicsInterpolation
members.signature: public void MultimeshInstanceResetPhysicsInterpolation(RID multimesh, int index)
members.summary: Prevents physics interpolation for the specified instance during the current physics tick. This is useful when moving an instance to a new location, to give an instantaneous change rather than interpolation from the previous location.

members.signature: public void MultimeshInstanceSetColor(RID multimesh, int index, Color color)

members.signature: public void MultimeshInstanceSetCustomData(RID multimesh, int index, Color customData)
members.summary: Sets the custom data for this instance. Custom data is passed as a [Color], but is interpreted as a [code]vec4[/code] in the shader. Equivalent to [method MultiMesh.set_instance_custom_data].

members.name: MultimeshInstanceSetTransform
members.signature: public void MultimeshInstanceSetTransform(RID multimesh, int index, Transform3D transform)
members.summary: Sets the [Transform3D] for this instance. Equivalent to [method MultiMesh.set_instance_transform].

members.name: MultimeshInstanceSetTransform2d
members.signature: public void MultimeshInstanceSetTransform2d(RID multimesh, int index, Transform2D transform)
members.summary: Sets the [Transform2D] for this instance. For use when multimesh is used in 2D. Equivalent to [method MultiMesh.set_instance_transform_2d].

members.name: MultimeshInstancesResetPhysicsInterpolation
members.signature: public void MultimeshInstancesResetPhysicsInterpolation(RID multimesh)
members.summary: Prevents physics interpolation for all instances during the current physics tick. This is useful when moving all instances to new locations, to give instantaneous changes rather than interpolation from the previous locations.

members.name: MultimeshSetBuffer
members.signature: public void MultimeshSetBuffer(RID multimesh, PackedFloat32Array buffer)
members.summary: Set the entire data to use for drawing the [param multimesh] at once to [param buffer] (such as instance transforms and colors). [param buffer]'s size must match the number of instances multiplied by the per-instance data size (which depends on the enabled MultiMesh fields). Otherwise, an error message is printed and nothing is rendered. See also [method multimesh_get_buffer]. The per-instance data size and expected data order is: [codeblock lang=text] 2D: - Position: 8 floats (8 floats for Transform2D) - Position + Vertex color: 12 floats (8 floats for Transform2D, 4 floats for Color) - Position + Custom data: 12 floats (8 floats for Transform2D, 4 floats of custom data) - Position + Vertex color + Custom data: 16 floats (8 floats for Transform2D, 4 floats for Color, 4 floats of custom data) 3D: - Position: 12 floats (12 floats for Transform3D) - Position + Vertex color: 16 floats (12 floats for Transform3D, 4 floats for Color) - Position + Custom data: 16 floats (12 floats for Transform3D, 4 floats of custom data) - Position + Vertex color + Custom data: 20 floats (12 floats for Transform3D, 4 floats for Color, 4 floats of custom data) [/codeblock] Instance transforms are in row-major order. Specifically: - For [Transform2D] the float-order is: [code](x.x, y.x, padding_float, origin.x, x.y, y.y, padding_float, origin.y)[/code]. - For [Transform3D] the float-order is: [code](basis.x.x, basis.y.x, basis.z.x, origin.x, basis.x.y, basis.y.y, basis.z.y, origin.y, basis.x.z, basis.y.z, basis.z.z, origin.z)[/code].

members.name: MultimeshSetBufferInterpolated
members.signature: public void MultimeshSetBufferInterpolated(RID multimesh, PackedFloat32Array buffer, PackedFloat32Array bufferPrevious)
members.summary: Alternative version of [method multimesh_set_buffer] for use with physics interpolation. Takes both an array of current data and an array of data for the previous physics tick.

members.name: MultimeshSetCustomAabb
members.signature: public void MultimeshSetCustomAabb(RID multimesh, AABB aabb)
members.summary: Sets the custom AABB for this MultiMesh resource.

members.signature: public void MultimeshSetMesh(RID multimesh, RID mesh)
members.summary: Sets the mesh to be drawn by the multimesh. Equivalent to [member MultiMesh.mesh].

members.name: MultimeshSetPhysicsInterpolated
members.signature: public void MultimeshSetPhysicsInterpolated(RID multimesh, bool interpolated)
members.summary: Turns on and off physics interpolation for this MultiMesh resource.

members.name: MultimeshSetPhysicsInterpolationQuality
members.signature: public void MultimeshSetPhysicsInterpolationQuality(RID multimesh, int quality)
members.summary: Sets the physics interpolation quality for the [MultiMesh]. A value of [constant MULTIMESH_INTERP_QUALITY_FAST] gives fast but low quality interpolation, a value of [constant MULTIMESH_INTERP_QUALITY_HIGH] gives slower but higher quality interpolation.

[b]Note:[/b] The equivalent resource is [Occluder3D] (not to be confused with the [OccluderInstance3D] node).

members.summary: Creates a new omni light and adds it to the RenderingServer. It can be accessed with the RID that is returned. This RID can be used in most [code]light_*[/code] RenderingServer functions. Once finished with your RID, you will want to free the RID using the RenderingServer's [method free_rid] method. To place in a scene, attach this omni light to an instance using [method instance_set_base] using the returned RID. [b]Note:[/b] The equivalent node is [OmniLight3D].

members.name: ParticlesCollisionCreate
members.signature: public RID ParticlesCollisionCreate()
members.summary: Creates a new 3D GPU particle collision or attractor and adds it to the RenderingServer. It can be accessed with the RID that is returned. This RID can be used in most [code]particles_collision_*[/code] RenderingServer functions. [b]Note:[/b] The equivalent nodes are [GPUParticlesCollision3D] and [GPUParticlesAttractor3D].

members.name: ParticlesCollisionHeightFieldUpdate
members.signature: public void ParticlesCollisionHeightFieldUpdate(RID particlesCollision)
members.summary: Requests an update for the 3D GPU particle collision heightfield. This may be automatically called by the 3D GPU particle collision heightfield depending on its [member GPUParticlesCollisionHeightField3D.update_mode].

members.name: ParticlesCollisionSetAttractorAttenuation
members.signature: public void ParticlesCollisionSetAttractorAttenuation(RID particlesCollision, float curve)
members.summary: Sets the attenuation [param curve] for the 3D GPU particles attractor specified by the [param particles_collision] RID. Only used for attractors, not colliders. Equivalent to [member GPUParticlesAttractor3D.attenuation].

members.name: ParticlesCollisionSetAttractorDirectionality
members.signature: public void ParticlesCollisionSetAttractorDirectionality(RID particlesCollision, float amount)
members.summary: Sets the directionality [param amount] for the 3D GPU particles attractor specified by the [param particles_collision] RID. Only used for attractors, not colliders. Equivalent to [member GPUParticlesAttractor3D.directionality].

members.name: ParticlesCollisionSetAttractorStrength
members.signature: public void ParticlesCollisionSetAttractorStrength(RID particlesCollision, float strength)
members.summary: Sets the [param strength] for the 3D GPU particles attractor specified by the [param particles_collision] RID. Only used for attractors, not colliders. Equivalent to [member GPUParticlesAttractor3D.strength].

members.name: ParticlesCollisionSetBoxExtents
members.signature: public void ParticlesCollisionSetBoxExtents(RID particlesCollision, Vector3 extents)
members.summary: Sets the [param extents] for the 3D GPU particles collision by the [param particles_collision] RID. Equivalent to [member GPUParticlesCollisionBox3D.size], [member GPUParticlesCollisionSDF3D.size], [member GPUParticlesCollisionHeightField3D.size], [member GPUParticlesAttractorBox3D.size] or [member GPUParticlesAttractorVectorField3D.size] depending on the [param particles_collision] type.

members.name: ParticlesCollisionSetCollisionType
members.signature: public void ParticlesCollisionSetCollisionType(RID particlesCollision, int type)
members.summary: Sets the collision or attractor shape [param type] for the 3D GPU particles collision or attractor specified by the [param particles_collision] RID.

members.name: ParticlesCollisionSetCullMask
members.signature: public void ParticlesCollisionSetCullMask(RID particlesCollision, int mask)
members.summary: Sets the cull [param mask] for the 3D GPU particles collision or attractor specified by the [param particles_collision] RID. Equivalent to [member GPUParticlesCollision3D.cull_mask] or [member GPUParticlesAttractor3D.cull_mask] depending on the [param particles_collision] type.

members.name: ParticlesCollisionSetFieldTexture
members.signature: public void ParticlesCollisionSetFieldTexture(RID particlesCollision, RID texture)
members.summary: Sets the signed distance field [param texture] for the 3D GPU particles collision specified by the [param particles_collision] RID. Equivalent to [member GPUParticlesCollisionSDF3D.texture] or [member GPUParticlesAttractorVectorField3D.texture] depending on the [param particles_collision] type.

members.name: ParticlesCollisionSetHeightFieldMask
members.signature: public void ParticlesCollisionSetHeightFieldMask(RID particlesCollision, int mask)
members.summary: Sets the heightfield [param mask] for the 3D GPU particles heightfield collision specified by the [param particles_collision] RID. Equivalent to [member GPUParticlesCollisionHeightField3D.heightfield_mask].

members.name: ParticlesCollisionSetHeightFieldResolution
members.signature: public void ParticlesCollisionSetHeightFieldResolution(RID particlesCollision, int resolution)
members.summary: Sets the heightmap [param resolution] for the 3D GPU particles heightfield collision specified by the [param particles_collision] RID. Equivalent to [member GPUParticlesCollisionHeightField3D.resolution].

members.name: ParticlesCollisionSetSphereRadius
members.signature: public void ParticlesCollisionSetSphereRadius(RID particlesCollision, float radius)
members.summary: Sets the [param radius] for the 3D GPU particles sphere collision or attractor specified by the [param particles_collision] RID. Equivalent to [member GPUParticlesCollisionSphere3D.radius] or [member GPUParticlesAttractorSphere3D.radius] depending on the [param particles_collision] type.

members.name: ParticlesCreate
members.signature: public RID ParticlesCreate()
members.summary: Creates a GPU-based particle system and adds it to the RenderingServer. It can be accessed with the RID that is returned. This RID will be used in all [code]particles_*[/code] RenderingServer functions. Once finished with your RID, you will want to free the RID using the RenderingServer's [method free_rid] method. To place in a scene, attach these particles to an instance using [method instance_set_base] using the returned RID. [b]Note:[/b] The equivalent nodes are [GPUParticles2D] and [GPUParticles3D]. [b]Note:[/b] All [code]particles_*[/code] methods only apply to GPU-based particles, not CPU-based particles. [CPUParticles2D] and [CPUParticles3D] do not have equivalent RenderingServer functions available, as these use [MultiMeshInstance2D] and [MultiMeshInstance3D] under the hood (see [code]multimesh_*[/code] methods).

members.name: ParticlesEmit
members.signature: public void ParticlesEmit(RID particles, Transform3D transform, Vector3 velocity, Color color, Color custom, int emitFlags)
members.summary: Manually emits particles from the [param particles] instance.

members.name: ParticlesGetCurrentAabb
members.signature: public AABB ParticlesGetCurrentAabb(RID particles)
members.summary: Calculates and returns the axis-aligned bounding box that contains all the particles. Equivalent to [method GPUParticles3D.capture_aabb].

members.name: ParticlesGetEmitting
members.signature: public bool ParticlesGetEmitting(RID particles)
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if particles are currently set to emitting.

members.name: ParticlesIsInactive
members.signature: public bool ParticlesIsInactive(RID particles)
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if particles are not emitting and particles are set to inactive.

members.name: ParticlesRequestProcess
members.signature: public void ParticlesRequestProcess(RID particles)
members.summary: Add particle system to list of particle systems that need to be updated. Update will take place on the next frame, or on the next call to [method instances_cull_aabb], [method instances_cull_convex], or [method instances_cull_ray].

members.name: ParticlesRequestProcessTime
members.signature: public void ParticlesRequestProcessTime(RID particles, float processTime, float processTimeResidual)
members.summary: Requests the particles to process for extra process time during a single frame. [param process_time] defines the time that the particles will process while emitting is on. [param process_time_residual] defines the time that particles will process with emitting turned off for the simulation. When combined with the particles' speed scale set to [code]0.0[/code], this is useful to be able to seek a particle system timeline.

members.name: ParticlesRestart
members.signature: public void ParticlesRestart(RID particles)
members.summary: Reset the particles on the next update. Equivalent to [method GPUParticles3D.restart].

members.name: ParticlesSetAmount
members.signature: public void ParticlesSetAmount(RID particles, int amount)
members.summary: Sets the number of particles to be drawn and allocates the memory for them. Equivalent to [member GPUParticles3D.amount].

members.name: ParticlesSetAmountRatio
members.signature: public void ParticlesSetAmountRatio(RID particles, float ratio)
members.summary: Sets the amount ratio for particles to be emitted. Equivalent to [member GPUParticles3D.amount_ratio].

members.name: ParticlesSetCollisionBaseSize
members.signature: public void ParticlesSetCollisionBaseSize(RID particles, float size)
members.summary: Sets the base size for particle collision. Equivalent to [member GPUParticles3D.collision_base_size].

members.name: ParticlesSetCustomAabb
members.signature: public void ParticlesSetCustomAabb(RID particles, AABB aabb)
members.summary: Sets a custom axis-aligned bounding box for the particle system. Equivalent to [member GPUParticles3D.visibility_aabb].

members.name: ParticlesSetDrawOrder
members.signature: public void ParticlesSetDrawOrder(RID particles, int order)
members.summary: Sets the draw order of the particles. Equivalent to [member GPUParticles3D.draw_order].

members.name: ParticlesSetDrawPassMesh
members.signature: public void ParticlesSetDrawPassMesh(RID particles, int pass, RID mesh)
members.summary: Sets the mesh to be used for the specified draw pass. Equivalent to [member GPUParticles3D.draw_pass_1], [member GPUParticles3D.draw_pass_2], [member GPUParticles3D.draw_pass_3], and [member GPUParticles3D.draw_pass_4].

members.name: ParticlesSetDrawPasses
members.signature: public void ParticlesSetDrawPasses(RID particles, int count)
members.summary: Sets the number of draw passes to use. Equivalent to [member GPUParticles3D.draw_passes].

members.name: ParticlesSetEmissionTransform
members.signature: public void ParticlesSetEmissionTransform(RID particles, Transform3D transform)
members.summary: Sets the [Transform3D] that will be used by the particles when they first emit.

members.name: ParticlesSetEmitterVelocity
members.signature: public void ParticlesSetEmitterVelocity(RID particles, Vector3 velocity)
members.summary: Sets the velocity of a particle node, that will be used by [member ParticleProcessMaterial.inherit_velocity_ratio].

members.name: ParticlesSetEmitting
members.signature: public void ParticlesSetEmitting(RID particles, bool emitting)
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], particles will emit over time. Setting to [code]false[/code] does not reset the particles, but only stops their emission. Equivalent to [member GPUParticles3D.emitting].

members.name: ParticlesSetExplosivenessRatio
members.signature: public void ParticlesSetExplosivenessRatio(RID particles, float ratio)
members.summary: Sets the explosiveness ratio. Equivalent to [member GPUParticles3D.explosiveness].

members.name: ParticlesSetFixedFps
members.signature: public void ParticlesSetFixedFps(RID particles, int fps)
members.summary: Sets the frame rate that the particle system rendering will be fixed to. Equivalent to [member GPUParticles3D.fixed_fps].

members.name: ParticlesSetFractionalDelta
members.signature: public void ParticlesSetFractionalDelta(RID particles, bool enable)
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], uses fractional delta which smooths the movement of the particles. Equivalent to [member GPUParticles3D.fract_delta].

members.name: ParticlesSetInterpToEnd
members.signature: public void ParticlesSetInterpToEnd(RID particles, float factor)
members.summary: Sets the value that informs a [ParticleProcessMaterial] to rush all particles towards the end of their lifetime.

members.name: ParticlesSetInterpolate
members.signature: public void ParticlesSetInterpolate(RID particles, bool enable)
members.summary: Sets whether particles should use interpolation between fixed steps. Equivalent to [member GPUParticles3D.interpolate].

members.name: ParticlesSetLifetime
members.signature: public void ParticlesSetLifetime(RID particles, float lifetime)
members.summary: Sets the lifetime of each particle in the system. Equivalent to [member GPUParticles3D.lifetime].

members.name: ParticlesSetMode
members.signature: public void ParticlesSetMode(RID particles, int mode)
members.summary: Sets whether the GPU particles specified by the [param particles] RID should be rendered in 2D or 3D according to [param mode].

members.name: ParticlesSetOneShot
members.signature: public void ParticlesSetOneShot(RID particles, bool oneShot)
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], particles will emit once and then stop. Equivalent to [member GPUParticles3D.one_shot].

members.name: ParticlesSetPreProcessTime
members.signature: public void ParticlesSetPreProcessTime(RID particles, float time)
members.summary: Sets the preprocess time for the particles' animation. This lets you delay starting an animation until after the particles have begun emitting. Equivalent to [member GPUParticles3D.preprocess].

members.name: ParticlesSetProcessMaterial
members.signature: public void ParticlesSetProcessMaterial(RID particles, RID material)
members.summary: Sets the material for processing the particles. [b]Note:[/b] This is not the material used to draw the materials. Equivalent to [member GPUParticles3D.process_material].

members.name: ParticlesSetRandomnessRatio
members.signature: public void ParticlesSetRandomnessRatio(RID particles, float ratio)
members.summary: Sets the emission randomness ratio. This randomizes the emission of particles within their phase. Equivalent to [member GPUParticles3D.randomness].

members.name: ParticlesSetSpeedScale
members.signature: public void ParticlesSetSpeedScale(RID particles, float scale)
members.summary: Sets the speed scale of the particle system. Equivalent to [member GPUParticles3D.speed_scale].

members.name: ParticlesSetSubemitter
members.signature: public void ParticlesSetSubemitter(RID particles, RID subemitterParticles)
members.summary: Sets the subemitter particles for the particle system. Equivalent to [member GPUParticles3D.sub_emitter].

members.name: ParticlesSetTrailBindPoses
members.signature: public void ParticlesSetTrailBindPoses(RID particles, Transform3D[] bindPoses)
members.summary: Sets the trail bind poses for the particle system. This specified as an array of [Transform3D]s representing the bind pose for each draw pass. See [member GPUParticles3D.draw_skin], [method Skin.get_bind_count], and [method Skin.get_bind_pose]. Set the value for each draw pass to [constant Transform3D.IDENTITY] to use the default behavior, which is what built-in trails use ([RibbonTrailMesh] and [TubeTrailMesh]).

members.name: ParticlesSetTrails
members.signature: public void ParticlesSetTrails(RID particles, bool enable, float lengthSec)
members.summary: If [param enable] is [code]true[/code], enables trails for the [param particles] with the specified [param length_sec] in seconds. Equivalent to [member GPUParticles3D.trail_enabled] and [member GPUParticles3D.trail_lifetime].

members.name: ParticlesSetTransformAlign
members.signature: public void ParticlesSetTransformAlign(RID particles, int align)
members.summary: Sets the transform alignment for the particle system. Equivalent to [member GPUParticles3D.transform_align].

members.name: ParticlesSetTransformAlignAxis
members.signature: public void ParticlesSetTransformAlignAxis(RID particles, int rotationAxis)
members.summary: Sets which axis to use for transform alignment.

members.name: ParticlesSetTransformAlignChannelFilter
members.signature: public void ParticlesSetTransformAlignChannelFilter(RID particles, int channelFilter)
members.summary: When using Z-Billboarding, which CUSTOM channel to read from.

members.name: ParticlesSetUseLocalCoordinates
members.signature: public void ParticlesSetUseLocalCoordinates(RID particles, bool enable)
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], particles use local coordinates. If [code]false[/code] they use global coordinates. Equivalent to [member GPUParticles3D.local_coords].

members.name: PositionalSoftShadowFilterSetQuality
members.signature: public void PositionalSoftShadowFilterSetQuality(int quality)
members.summary: Sets the filter quality for omni and spot light shadows in 3D. See also [member ProjectSettings.rendering/lights_and_shadows/positional_shadow/soft_shadow_filter_quality]. This parameter is global and cannot be set on a per-viewport basis.

members.name: ReflectionProbeCreate
members.signature: public RID ReflectionProbeCreate()
members.summary: Creates a reflection probe and adds it to the RenderingServer. It can be accessed with the RID that is returned. This RID will be used in all [code]reflection_probe_*[/code] RenderingServer functions. Once finished with your RID, you will want to free the RID using the RenderingServer's [method free_rid] method. To place in a scene, attach this reflection probe to an instance using [method instance_set_base] using the returned RID. [b]Note:[/b] The equivalent node is [ReflectionProbe].

members.name: ReflectionProbeSetAmbientColor
members.signature: public void ReflectionProbeSetAmbientColor(RID probe, Color color)
members.summary: Sets the reflection probe's custom ambient light color. Equivalent to [member ReflectionProbe.ambient_color].

members.name: ReflectionProbeSetAmbientEnergy
members.signature: public void ReflectionProbeSetAmbientEnergy(RID probe, float energy)
members.summary: Sets the reflection probe's custom ambient light energy. Equivalent to [member ReflectionProbe.ambient_color_energy].

members.name: ReflectionProbeSetAmbientMode
members.signature: public void ReflectionProbeSetAmbientMode(RID probe, int mode)
members.summary: Sets the reflection probe's ambient light mode. Equivalent to [member ReflectionProbe.ambient_mode].

members.name: ReflectionProbeSetAsInterior
members.signature: public void ReflectionProbeSetAsInterior(RID probe, bool enable)
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], reflections will ignore sky contribution. Equivalent to [member ReflectionProbe.interior].

members.name: ReflectionProbeSetBlendDistance
members.signature: public void ReflectionProbeSetBlendDistance(RID probe, float blendDistance)
members.summary: Sets the distance in meters over which a probe blends into the scene.

members.name: ReflectionProbeSetCullMask
members.signature: public void ReflectionProbeSetCullMask(RID probe, int layers)
members.summary: Sets the render cull mask for this reflection probe. Only instances with a matching layer will be reflected by this probe. Equivalent to [member ReflectionProbe.cull_mask].

members.name: ReflectionProbeSetEnableBoxProjection
members.signature: public void ReflectionProbeSetEnableBoxProjection(RID probe, bool enable)
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], uses box projection. This can make reflections look more correct in certain situations. Equivalent to [member ReflectionProbe.box_projection].

members.name: ReflectionProbeSetEnableShadows
members.signature: public void ReflectionProbeSetEnableShadows(RID probe, bool enable)
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], computes shadows in the reflection probe. This makes the reflection much slower to compute. Equivalent to [member ReflectionProbe.enable_shadows].

members.name: ReflectionProbeSetIntensity
members.signature: public void ReflectionProbeSetIntensity(RID probe, float intensity)
members.summary: Sets the intensity of the reflection probe. Intensity modulates the strength of the reflection. Equivalent to [member ReflectionProbe.intensity].

members.name: ReflectionProbeSetMaxDistance
members.signature: public void ReflectionProbeSetMaxDistance(RID probe, float distance)
members.summary: Sets the max distance away from the probe an object can be before it is culled. Equivalent to [member ReflectionProbe.max_distance].

members.name: ReflectionProbeSetMeshLodThreshold
members.signature: public void ReflectionProbeSetMeshLodThreshold(RID probe, float pixels)
members.summary: Sets the mesh level of detail to use in the reflection probe rendering. Higher values will use less detailed versions of meshes that have LOD variations generated, which can improve performance. Equivalent to [member ReflectionProbe.mesh_lod_threshold].

members.name: ReflectionProbeSetOriginOffset
members.signature: public void ReflectionProbeSetOriginOffset(RID probe, Vector3 offset)
members.summary: Sets the origin offset to be used when this reflection probe is in box project mode. Equivalent to [member ReflectionProbe.origin_offset].

members.name: ReflectionProbeSetReflectionMask
members.signature: public void ReflectionProbeSetReflectionMask(RID probe, int layers)
members.summary: Sets the render reflection mask for this reflection probe. Only instances with a matching layer will have reflections applied from this probe. Equivalent to [member ReflectionProbe.reflection_mask].

members.name: ReflectionProbeSetResolution
members.signature: public void ReflectionProbeSetResolution(RID probe, int resolution)
members.summary: Deprecated. This method does nothing.

members.name: ReflectionProbeSetSize
members.signature: public void ReflectionProbeSetSize(RID probe, Vector3 size)
members.summary: Sets the size of the area that the reflection probe will capture. Equivalent to [member ReflectionProbe.size].

members.name: ReflectionProbeSetUpdateMode
members.signature: public void ReflectionProbeSetUpdateMode(RID probe, int mode)
members.summary: Sets how often the reflection probe updates. Can either be once or every frame.

members.name: RequestFrameDrawnCallback
members.signature: public void RequestFrameDrawnCallback(Callable callable)
members.summary: Schedules a callback to the given callable after a frame has been drawn.

members.name: ScenarioCreate
members.signature: public RID ScenarioCreate()
members.summary: Creates a scenario and adds it to the RenderingServer. It can be accessed with the RID that is returned. This RID will be used in all [code]scenario_*[/code] RenderingServer functions. Once finished with your RID, you will want to free the RID using the RenderingServer's [method free_rid] method. The scenario is the 3D world that all the visual instances exist in.

members.name: ScenarioSetCameraAttributes
members.signature: public void ScenarioSetCameraAttributes(RID scenario, RID effects)
members.summary: Sets the camera attributes ([param effects]) that will be used with this scenario. See also [CameraAttributes].

members.name: ScenarioSetCompositor
members.signature: public void ScenarioSetCompositor(RID scenario, RID compositor)
members.summary: Sets the compositor ([param compositor]) that will be used with this scenario. See also [Compositor].

members.name: ScenarioSetEnvironment
members.signature: public void ScenarioSetEnvironment(RID scenario, RID environment)
members.summary: Sets the environment that will be used with this scenario. See also [Environment].

members.name: ScenarioSetFallbackEnvironment
members.signature: public void ScenarioSetFallbackEnvironment(RID scenario, RID environment)
members.summary: Sets the fallback environment to be used by this scenario. The fallback environment is used if no environment is set. Internally, this is used by the editor to provide a default environment.

members.name: ScreenSpaceRoughnessLimiterSetActive
members.signature: public void ScreenSpaceRoughnessLimiterSetActive(bool enable, float amount, float limit)
members.summary: Sets the screen-space roughness limiter parameters, such as whether it should be enabled and its thresholds. Equivalent to [member ProjectSettings.rendering/anti_aliasing/screen_space_roughness_limiter/enabled], [member ProjectSettings.rendering/anti_aliasing/screen_space_roughness_limiter/amount] and [member ProjectSettings.rendering/anti_aliasing/screen_space_roughness_limiter/limit].

members.name: SetBootImage
members.signature: public void SetBootImage(Image image, Color color, bool scale, bool useFilter)
members.summary: Sets a boot image. The [param color] defines the background color. The value of [param scale] indicates if the image will be scaled to fit the screen size. If [param use_filter] is [code]true[/code], the image will be scaled with linear interpolation. If [param use_filter] is [code]false[/code], the image will be scaled with nearest-neighbor interpolation.

members.name: SetBootImageWithStretch
members.signature: public void SetBootImageWithStretch(Image image, Color color, int stretchMode, bool useFilter)
members.summary: Sets a boot image. The [param color] defines the background color. The value of [param stretch_mode] indicates how the image will be stretched (see [enum SplashStretchMode] for possible values). If [param use_filter] is [code]true[/code], the image will be scaled with linear interpolation. If [param use_filter] is [code]false[/code], the image will be scaled with nearest-neighbor interpolation.

members.name: SetDebugGenerateWireframes
members.signature: public void SetDebugGenerateWireframes(bool generate)
members.summary: If [param generate] is [code]true[/code], generates debug wireframes for all meshes that are loaded when using the Compatibility renderer. By default, the engine does not generate debug wireframes at runtime, since they slow down loading of assets and take up VRAM. [b]Note:[/b] You must call this method before loading any meshes when using the Compatibility renderer. Otherwise, wireframes will not be used.

members.name: SetDefaultClearColor
members.signature: public void SetDefaultClearColor(Color color)
members.summary: Sets the default clear color which is used when a specific clear color has not been selected. See also [method get_default_clear_color].

members.name: ShaderCreate
members.signature: public RID ShaderCreate()
members.summary: Creates an empty shader and adds it to the RenderingServer. It can be accessed with the RID that is returned. This RID will be used in all [code]shader_*[/code] RenderingServer functions. Once finished with your RID, you will want to free the RID using the RenderingServer's [method free_rid] method. [b]Note:[/b] The equivalent resource is [Shader].

members.name: ShaderGetCode
members.signature: public String ShaderGetCode(RID shader)
members.summary: Returns a shader's source code as a string.

members.name: ShaderGetDefaultTextureParameter
members.signature: public RID ShaderGetDefaultTextureParameter(RID shader, StringName name, int index)
members.summary: Returns a default texture from a shader searched by name. [b]Note:[/b] If the sampler array is used use [param index] to access the specified texture.

members.name: ShaderGetParameterDefault
members.signature: public Variant ShaderGetParameterDefault(RID shader, StringName name)
members.summary: Returns the default value for the specified shader uniform. This is usually the value written in the shader source code.

members.name: ShaderSetCode
members.signature: public void ShaderSetCode(RID shader, String code)
members.summary: Sets the shader's source code (which triggers recompilation after being changed).

members.name: ShaderSetDefaultTextureParameter
members.signature: public void ShaderSetDefaultTextureParameter(RID shader, StringName name, RID texture, int index)
members.summary: Sets a shader's default texture. Overwrites the texture given by name. [b]Note:[/b] If the sampler array is used use [param index] to access the specified texture.

members.name: ShaderSetPathHint
members.signature: public void ShaderSetPathHint(RID shader, String path)
members.summary: Sets the path hint for the specified shader. This should generally match the [Shader] resource's [member Resource.resource_path].

members.summary: Allocates data for this skeleton using the number of bones specified in [param bones]. If [param is_2d_skeleton] is [code]true[/code], the skeleton will be treated as a 2D skeleton instead of a 3D skeleton. See also [method skeleton_get_bone_count].

members.name: SkeletonBoneGetTransform
members.signature: public Transform3D SkeletonBoneGetTransform(RID skeleton, int bone)
members.summary: Returns the [Transform3D] set for a specific bone of this skeleton.

members.name: SkeletonBoneGetTransform2d
