members.summary: Returns the [Transform2D] set for a specific bone of this skeleton.

members.name: SkeletonBoneSetTransform
members.signature: public void SkeletonBoneSetTransform(RID skeleton, int bone, Transform3D transform)
members.summary: Sets the [Transform3D] for a specific bone of this skeleton.

members.name: SkeletonBoneSetTransform2d
members.signature: public void SkeletonBoneSetTransform2d(RID skeleton, int bone, Transform2D transform)
members.summary: Sets the [Transform2D] for a specific bone of this skeleton.

members.name: SkeletonCreate
members.signature: public RID SkeletonCreate()
members.summary: Creates a skeleton and adds it to the RenderingServer. It can be accessed with the RID that is returned. This RID will be used in all [code]skeleton_*[/code] RenderingServer functions. Once finished with your RID, you will want to free the RID using the RenderingServer's [method free_rid] method.

members.name: SkeletonGetBoneCount
members.signature: public int SkeletonGetBoneCount(RID skeleton)
members.summary: Returns the number of bones allocated for this skeleton. See also [method skeleton_allocate_data].

members.name: SkeletonSetBaseTransform2d
members.signature: public void SkeletonSetBaseTransform2d(RID skeleton, Transform2D baseTransform)
members.summary: Sets the base [Transform2D] to use for the specified skeleton.

members.name: SkyBakePanorama
members.signature: public Image SkyBakePanorama(RID sky, float energy, bool bakeIrradiance, Vector2i size)
members.summary: Generates and returns an [Image] containing the radiance map for the specified [param sky] RID. This supports built-in sky material and custom sky shaders. If [param bake_irradiance] is [code]true[/code], the irradiance map is saved instead of the radiance map. The radiance map is used to render reflected light, while the irradiance map is used to render ambient light. See also [method environment_bake_panorama]. [b]Note:[/b] The image is saved using linear encoding without any tonemapping performed, which means it will look too dark if viewed directly in an image editor. [param energy] values above [code]1.0[/code] can be used to brighten the resulting image. [b]Note:[/b] [param size] should be a 2:1 aspect ratio for the generated panorama to have square pixels. For radiance maps, there is no point in using a height greater than [member Sky.radiance_size], as it won't increase detail. Irradiance maps only contain low-frequency data, so there is usually no point in going past a size of 128×64 pixels when saving an irradiance map.

members.name: SkyCreate
members.signature: public RID SkyCreate()
members.summary: Creates an empty sky and adds it to the RenderingServer. It can be accessed with the RID that is returned. This RID will be used in all [code]sky_*[/code] RenderingServer functions. Once finished with your RID, you will want to free the RID using the RenderingServer's [method free_rid] method.

members.name: SkySetMaterial
members.signature: public void SkySetMaterial(RID sky, RID material)
members.summary: Sets the material that the sky uses to render the background, ambient and reflection maps.

members.name: SkySetMode
members.signature: public void SkySetMode(RID sky, int mode)
members.summary: Sets the process [param mode] of the sky specified by the [param sky] RID. Equivalent to [member Sky.process_mode].

members.name: SkySetRadianceSize
members.signature: public void SkySetRadianceSize(RID sky, int radianceSize)
members.summary: Sets the [param radiance_size] of the sky specified by the [param sky] RID (in pixels). Equivalent to [member Sky.radiance_size].

members.name: SpotLightCreate
members.signature: public RID SpotLightCreate()
members.summary: Creates a spot light and adds it to the RenderingServer. It can be accessed with the RID that is returned. This RID can be used in most [code]light_*[/code] RenderingServer functions. Once finished with your RID, you will want to free the RID using the RenderingServer's [method free_rid] method. To place in a scene, attach this spot light to an instance using [method instance_set_base] using the returned RID.

members.name: SubSurfaceScatteringSetQuality
members.signature: public void SubSurfaceScatteringSetQuality(int quality)
members.summary: Sets [member ProjectSettings.rendering/environment/subsurface_scattering/subsurface_scattering_quality] to use when rendering materials that have subsurface scattering enabled.

members.name: SubSurfaceScatteringSetScale
members.signature: public void SubSurfaceScatteringSetScale(float scale, float depthScale)
members.summary: Sets the [member ProjectSettings.rendering/environment/subsurface_scattering/subsurface_scattering_scale] and [member ProjectSettings.rendering/environment/subsurface_scattering/subsurface_scattering_depth_scale] to use when rendering materials that have subsurface scattering enabled.

members.name: Texture2dCreate
members.signature: public RID Texture2dCreate(Image image)
members.summary: Creates a 2-dimensional texture and adds it to the RenderingServer. It can be accessed with the RID that is returned. This RID will be used in all [code]texture_2d_*[/code] RenderingServer functions. Once finished with your RID, you will want to free the RID using the RenderingServer's [method free_rid] method. [b]Note:[/b] The equivalent resource is [Texture2D]. [b]Note:[/b] Not to be confused with [method RenderingDevice.texture_create], which creates the graphics API's own texture type as opposed to the Godot-specific [Texture2D] resource.

members.name: Texture2dGet
members.signature: public Image Texture2dGet(RID texture)
members.summary: Returns an [Image] instance from the given [param texture] [RID]. [b]Example:[/b] Get the test texture from [method get_test_texture] and apply it to a [Sprite2D] node: [codeblock] var texture_rid = RenderingServer.get_test_texture() var texture = ImageTexture.create_from_image(RenderingServer.texture_2d_get(texture_rid)) $Sprite2D.texture = texture [/codeblock]

members.name: Texture2dLayerGet
members.signature: public Image Texture2dLayerGet(RID texture, int layer)
members.summary: Returns an [Image] instance from the given [param texture] [RID] and [param layer].

members.name: Texture2dLayeredCreate
members.signature: public RID Texture2dLayeredCreate(Image[] layers, int layeredType)
members.summary: Creates a 2-dimensional layered texture and adds it to the RenderingServer. It can be accessed with the RID that is returned. This RID will be used in all [code]texture_2d_layered_*[/code] RenderingServer functions. Once finished with your RID, you will want to free the RID using the RenderingServer's [method free_rid] method. [b]Note:[/b] The equivalent resource is [TextureLayered].

members.name: Texture2dLayeredPlaceholderCreate
members.signature: public RID Texture2dLayeredPlaceholderCreate(int layeredType)
members.summary: Creates a placeholder for a 2-dimensional layered texture and adds it to the RenderingServer. It can be accessed with the RID that is returned. This RID will be used in all [code]texture_2d_layered_*[/code] RenderingServer functions, although it does nothing when used. See also [method texture_2d_placeholder_create]. [b]Note:[/b] The equivalent resource is [PlaceholderTextureLayered].

members.name: Texture2dPlaceholderCreate
members.signature: public RID Texture2dPlaceholderCreate()
members.summary: Creates a placeholder for a 2-dimensional layered texture and adds it to the RenderingServer. It can be accessed with the RID that is returned. This RID will be used in all [code]texture_2d_layered_*[/code] RenderingServer functions, although it does nothing when used. See also [method texture_2d_layered_placeholder_create]. Once finished with your RID, you will want to free the RID using the RenderingServer's [method free_rid] method. [b]Note:[/b] The equivalent resource is [PlaceholderTexture2D].

members.name: Texture2dUpdate
members.signature: public void Texture2dUpdate(RID texture, Image image, int layer)
members.summary: Updates the texture specified by the [param texture] [RID] with the data in [param image]. A [param layer] must also be specified, which should be [code]0[/code] when updating a single-layer texture ([Texture2D]). [b]Note:[/b] The [param image] must have the same width, height and format as the current [param texture] data. Otherwise, an error will be printed and the original texture won't be modified. If you need to use different width, height or format, use [method texture_replace] instead.

members.name: Texture3dCreate
members.signature: public RID Texture3dCreate(int format, int width, int height, int depth, bool mipmaps, Image[] data)
members.summary: [b]Note:[/b] The equivalent resource is [Texture3D].

members.name: Texture3dGet
members.signature: public Image[] Texture3dGet(RID texture)
members.summary: Returns 3D texture data as an array of [Image]s for the specified texture [RID].

members.name: Texture3dPlaceholderCreate
members.signature: public RID Texture3dPlaceholderCreate()
members.summary: Creates a placeholder for a 3-dimensional texture and adds it to the RenderingServer. It can be accessed with the RID that is returned. This RID will be used in all [code]texture_3d_*[/code] RenderingServer functions, although it does nothing when used. Once finished with your RID, you will want to free the RID using the RenderingServer's [method free_rid] method. [b]Note:[/b] The equivalent resource is [PlaceholderTexture3D].

members.name: Texture3dUpdate
members.signature: public void Texture3dUpdate(RID texture, Image[] data)
members.summary: Updates the texture specified by the [param texture] [RID]'s data with the data in [param data]. All the texture's layers must be replaced at once. [b]Note:[/b] The [param texture] must have the same width, height, depth and format as the current texture data. Otherwise, an error will be printed and the original texture won't be modified. If you need to use different width, height, depth or format, use [method texture_replace] instead.

members.name: TextureCreateFromNativeHandle
members.signature: public RID TextureCreateFromNativeHandle(int type, int format, int nativeHandle, int width, int height, int depth, int layers, int layeredType)
members.summary: Creates a texture based on a native handle that was created outside of Godot's renderer. [b]Note:[/b] If using only the rendering device renderer, it's recommend to use [method RenderingDevice.texture_create_from_extension] together with [method RenderingServer.texture_rd_create], rather than this method. This way, the texture's format and usage can be controlled more effectively.

members.name: TextureDrawableBlitRect
members.signature: public void TextureDrawableBlitRect(RID[] textures, Rect2i rect, RID material, Color modulate, RID[] sourceTextures, int toMipmap)
members.summary: Draws to [param rect] on up to 4 given Drawable [param textures], using a TextureBlit Shader from [param material]. [param modulate] and up to 4 [param source_textures] are uniforms for the Shader to process with. [param to_mipmap] can specify to perform this draw to a lower mipmap level. [b]Note:[/b] All [param textures] must be the same size and format.

members.name: TextureDrawableCreate
members.signature: public RID TextureDrawableCreate(int width, int height, int format, Color color, bool withMipmaps)
members.summary: Creates a 2-dimensional texture and adds it to the RenderingServer. It can be accessed with the RID that is returned. This RID will be used in all [code]texture_drawable*[/code] RenderingServer functions. Once finished with your RID, you will want to free the RID using the RenderingServer's [method free_rid] method. [b]Note:[/b] The equivalent resource is [DrawableTexture2D].

members.name: TextureDrawableGenerateMipmaps
members.signature: public void TextureDrawableGenerateMipmaps(RID texture)
members.summary: Calculates new MipMaps for the given Drawable [param texture].

members.name: TextureDrawableGetDefaultMaterial
members.signature: public RID TextureDrawableGetDefaultMaterial()
members.summary: Returns a ShaderMaterial with the default texture_blit Shader.

members.name: TextureGetFormat
members.signature: public int TextureGetFormat(RID texture)
members.summary: Returns the format for the texture.

members.name: TextureGetNativeHandle
members.signature: public int TextureGetNativeHandle(RID texture, bool srgb)
members.summary: Returns the internal graphics handle for this texture object. For use when communicating with third-party APIs mostly with GDExtension. [param srgb] should be [code]true[/code] when the texture uses nonlinear sRGB encoding and [code]false[/code] when the texture uses linear encoding. [b]Note:[/b] This function returns a [code]uint64_t[/code] which internally maps to a [code]GLuint[/code] (OpenGL) or [code]VkImage[/code] (Vulkan).

members.name: TextureGetPath
members.signature: public String TextureGetPath(RID texture)
members.summary: Returns the resource path (starting with [code]res://[/code] or [code]uid://[/code]) for the specified texture RID. Returns an empty [String] if the resource is built-in. See also [method texture_set_path].

members.name: TextureGetRdTexture
members.signature: public RID TextureGetRdTexture(RID texture, bool srgb)
members.summary: Returns a texture [RID] that can be used with [RenderingDevice]. [param srgb] should be [code]true[/code] when the texture uses nonlinear sRGB encoding and [code]false[/code] when the texture uses linear encoding.

members.name: TextureProxyCreate
members.signature: public RID TextureProxyCreate(RID @base)
members.summary: This method does nothing and always returns an invalid [RID].

members.name: TextureProxyUpdate
members.signature: public void TextureProxyUpdate(RID texture, RID proxyTo)
members.summary: This method does nothing.

members.name: TextureRdCreate
members.signature: public RID TextureRdCreate(RID rdTexture, int layerType)
members.summary: Creates a new texture object based on a texture created directly on the [RenderingDevice]. If the texture contains layers, [param layer_type] is used to define the layer type. Once finished with your RID, you will want to free the RID using the RenderingServer's [method free_rid] method. [b]Note:[/b] The RenderingServer's [method free_rid] won't free the underlying [param rd_texture], you will want to free the [param rd_texture] using [method RenderingDevice.free_rid].

members.name: TextureReplace
members.signature: public void TextureReplace(RID texture, RID byTexture)
members.summary: Replaces [param texture]'s texture data by the texture specified by the [param by_texture] RID, without changing [param texture]'s RID.

members.name: TextureSetForceRedrawIfVisible
members.signature: public void TextureSetForceRedrawIfVisible(RID texture, bool enable)
members.summary: Sets whether the texture RID should force redrawing when it's visible on screen when [member OS.low_processor_usage_mode] is [code]true[/code]. This is used by [AnimatedTexture] to force redrawing.

members.name: TextureSetPath
members.signature: public void TextureSetPath(RID texture, String path)
members.summary: Sets the resource path for this texture RID. See also [method texture_get_path]. [b]Note:[/b] This is purely a hint and does not cause the texture to be automatically saved when set to a [code]res://[/code] path.

members.name: TextureSetSizeOverride
members.signature: public void TextureSetSizeOverride(RID texture, int width, int height)
members.summary: Sets the size at which the texture should be [i]displayed[/i] in 2D, ignoring its original size. This does not rescale the texture data itself, only how it is drawn in 2D. Set [param width] and [param height] to 0 to disable the size override.

members.name: ViewportAttachCamera
members.signature: public void ViewportAttachCamera(RID viewport, RID camera)
members.summary: Sets a viewport's camera.

members.name: ViewportAttachCanvas
members.signature: public void ViewportAttachCanvas(RID viewport, RID canvas)
members.summary: Sets a viewport's canvas.

members.name: ViewportAttachToScreen
members.signature: public void ViewportAttachToScreen(RID viewport, Rect2 rect, int screen)
members.summary: Copies the viewport to a region of the screen specified by [param rect]. If [method viewport_set_render_direct_to_screen] is [code]true[/code], then the viewport does not use a framebuffer and the contents of the viewport are rendered directly to screen. However, note that the root viewport is drawn last, therefore it will draw over the screen. Accordingly, you must set the root viewport to an area that does not cover the area that you have attached this viewport to. For example, you can set the root viewport to not render at all with the following code: [codeblocks] [gdscript] func _ready(): RenderingServer.viewport_attach_to_screen(get_viewport().get_viewport_rid(), Rect2()) RenderingServer.viewport_attach_to_screen($Viewport.get_viewport_rid(), Rect2(0, 0, 600, 600)) [/gdscript] [/codeblocks] Using this can result in significant optimization, especially on lower-end devices. However, it comes at the cost of having to manage your viewports manually. For further optimization, see [method viewport_set_render_direct_to_screen].

members.name: ViewportCreate
members.signature: public RID ViewportCreate()
members.summary: Creates an empty viewport and adds it to the RenderingServer. It can be accessed with the RID that is returned. This RID will be used in all [code]viewport_*[/code] RenderingServer functions. Once finished with your RID, you will want to free the RID using the RenderingServer's [method free_rid] method. [b]Note:[/b] The equivalent node is [Viewport].

members.name: ViewportGetMeasuredRenderTimeCpu
members.signature: public float ViewportGetMeasuredRenderTimeCpu(RID viewport)
members.summary: Returns the CPU time taken to render the last frame in milliseconds. This [i]only[/i] includes time spent in rendering-related operations; scripts' [code]_process[/code] functions and other engine subsystems are not included in this readout. To get a complete readout of CPU time spent to render the scene, sum the render times of all viewports that are drawn every frame plus [method get_frame_setup_time_cpu]. Unlike [method Engine.get_frames_per_second], this method will accurately reflect CPU utilization even if framerate is capped via V-Sync or [member Engine.max_fps]. See also [method viewport_get_measured_render_time_gpu]. [b]Note:[/b] Requires measurements to be enabled on the specified [param viewport] using [method viewport_set_measure_render_time]. Otherwise, this method returns [code]0.0[/code].

members.name: ViewportGetMeasuredRenderTimeGpu
members.signature: public float ViewportGetMeasuredRenderTimeGpu(RID viewport)
members.summary: Returns the GPU time taken to render the last frame in milliseconds. To get a complete readout of GPU time spent to render the scene, sum the render times of all viewports that are drawn every frame. Unlike [method Engine.get_frames_per_second], this method accurately reflects GPU utilization even if framerate is capped via V-Sync or [member Engine.max_fps]. See also [method viewport_get_measured_render_time_cpu]. [b]Note:[/b] Requires measurements to be enabled on the specified [param viewport] using [method viewport_set_measure_render_time]. Otherwise, this method returns [code]0.0[/code]. [b]Note:[/b] When GPU utilization is low enough during a certain period of time, GPUs will decrease their power state (which in turn decreases core and memory clock speeds). This can cause the reported GPU time to increase if GPU utilization is kept low enough by a framerate cap (compared to what it would be at the GPU's highest power state). Keep this in mind when benchmarking using [method viewport_get_measured_render_time_gpu]. This behavior can be overridden in the graphics driver settings at the cost of higher power usage.

members.name: ViewportGetRenderInfo
members.signature: public int ViewportGetRenderInfo(RID viewport, int type, int info)
members.summary: Returns a statistic about the rendering engine which can be used for performance profiling. This is separated into render pass [param type]s, each of them having the same [param info]s you can query (different passes will return different values). See also [method get_rendering_info], which returns global information across all viewports. [b]Note:[/b] Viewport rendering information is not available until at least 2 frames have been rendered by the engine. If rendering information is not available, [method viewport_get_render_info] returns [code]0[/code]. To print rendering information in [code]_ready()[/code] successfully, use the following: [codeblock] func _ready(): for _i in 2: await get_tree().process_frame print( RenderingServer.viewport_get_render_info(get_viewport().get_viewport_rid(), RenderingServer.VIEWPORT_RENDER_INFO_TYPE_VISIBLE, RenderingServer.VIEWPORT_RENDER_INFO_DRAW_CALLS_IN_FRAME) ) [/codeblock]

members.name: ViewportGetRenderTarget
members.signature: public RID ViewportGetRenderTarget(RID viewport)
members.summary: Returns the render target for the viewport.

members.name: ViewportGetTexture
members.signature: public RID ViewportGetTexture(RID viewport)
members.summary: Returns the viewport's last rendered frame.

members.name: ViewportGetUpdateMode
members.signature: public int ViewportGetUpdateMode(RID viewport)
members.summary: Returns the viewport's update mode. [b]Warning:[/b] Calling this from any thread other than the rendering thread will be detrimental to performance.

members.name: ViewportRemoveCanvas
members.signature: public void ViewportRemoveCanvas(RID viewport, RID canvas)
members.summary: Detaches a viewport from a canvas.

members.name: ViewportSetActive
members.signature: public void ViewportSetActive(RID viewport, bool active)
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], sets the viewport active, else sets it inactive.

members.name: ViewportSetAnisotropicFilteringLevel
members.signature: public void ViewportSetAnisotropicFilteringLevel(RID viewport, int anisotropicFilteringLevel)
members.summary: Sets the maximum number of samples to take when using anisotropic filtering on textures (as a power of two). A higher sample count will result in sharper textures at oblique angles, but is more expensive to compute. A value of [code]0[/code] forcibly disables anisotropic filtering, even on materials where it is enabled. The anisotropic filtering level also affects decals and light projectors if they are configured to use anisotropic filtering. See [member ProjectSettings.rendering/textures/decals/filter] and [member ProjectSettings.rendering/textures/light_projectors/filter]. [b]Note:[/b] In 3D, for this setting to have an effect, set [member BaseMaterial3D.texture_filter] to [constant BaseMaterial3D.TEXTURE_FILTER_LINEAR_WITH_MIPMAPS_ANISOTROPIC] or [constant BaseMaterial3D.TEXTURE_FILTER_NEAREST_WITH_MIPMAPS_ANISOTROPIC] on materials. [b]Note:[/b] In 2D, for this setting to have an effect, set [member CanvasItem.texture_filter] to [constant CanvasItem.TEXTURE_FILTER_LINEAR_WITH_MIPMAPS_ANISOTROPIC] or [constant CanvasItem.TEXTURE_FILTER_NEAREST_WITH_MIPMAPS_ANISOTROPIC] on the [CanvasItem] node displaying the texture (or in [CanvasTexture]). However, anisotropic filtering is rarely useful in 2D, so only enable it for textures in 2D if it makes a meaningful visual difference.

members.name: ViewportSetCanvasCullMask
members.signature: public void ViewportSetCanvasCullMask(RID viewport, int canvasCullMask)
members.summary: Sets the rendering mask associated with this [Viewport]. Only [CanvasItem] nodes with a matching rendering visibility layer will be rendered by this [Viewport].

members.name: ViewportSetCanvasStacking
members.signature: public void ViewportSetCanvasStacking(RID viewport, RID canvas, int layer, int sublayer)
members.summary: Sets the stacking order for a viewport's canvas. [param layer] is the actual canvas layer, while [param sublayer] specifies the stacking order of the canvas among those in the same layer. [b]Note:[/b] [param layer] should be between [constant CANVAS_LAYER_MIN] and [constant CANVAS_LAYER_MAX] (inclusive). Any other value will wrap around.

members.name: ViewportSetCanvasTransform
members.signature: public void ViewportSetCanvasTransform(RID viewport, RID canvas, Transform2D offset)
members.summary: Sets the transformation of a viewport's canvas.

members.name: ViewportSetClearMode
members.signature: public void ViewportSetClearMode(RID viewport, int clearMode)
members.summary: Sets the clear mode of a viewport.

members.name: ViewportSetDebugDraw
members.signature: public void ViewportSetDebugDraw(RID viewport, int draw)
members.summary: Sets the debug draw mode of a viewport.

members.name: ViewportSetDefaultCanvasItemTextureFilter
members.signature: public void ViewportSetDefaultCanvasItemTextureFilter(RID viewport, int filter)
members.summary: Sets the default texture filtering mode for the specified [param viewport] RID.

members.name: ViewportSetDefaultCanvasItemTextureRepeat
members.signature: public void ViewportSetDefaultCanvasItemTextureRepeat(RID viewport, int repeat)
members.summary: Sets the default texture repeat mode for the specified [param viewport] RID.

members.name: ViewportSetDisable2d
members.signature: public void ViewportSetDisable2d(RID viewport, bool disable)
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the viewport's canvas (i.e. 2D and GUI elements) is not rendered.

members.name: ViewportSetDisable3d
members.signature: public void ViewportSetDisable3d(RID viewport, bool disable)
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the viewport's 3D elements are not rendered.

members.name: ViewportSetEnvironmentMode
members.signature: public void ViewportSetEnvironmentMode(RID viewport, int mode)
members.summary: Sets the viewport's environment mode which allows enabling or disabling rendering of 3D environment over 2D canvas. When disabled, 2D will not be affected by the environment. When enabled, 2D will be affected by the environment if the environment background mode is [constant ENV_BG_CANVAS]. The default behavior is to inherit the setting from the viewport's parent. If the topmost parent is also set to [constant VIEWPORT_ENVIRONMENT_INHERIT], then the behavior will be the same as if it was set to [constant VIEWPORT_ENVIRONMENT_ENABLED].

members.name: ViewportSetFsrSharpness
members.signature: public void ViewportSetFsrSharpness(RID viewport, float sharpness)
members.summary: Determines how sharp the upscaled image will be when using the FSR upscaling mode. Sharpness halves with every whole number. Values go from 0.0 (sharpest) to 2.0. Values above 2.0 won't make a visible difference.

members.name: ViewportSetGlobalCanvasTransform
members.signature: public void ViewportSetGlobalCanvasTransform(RID viewport, Transform2D transform)
members.summary: Sets the viewport's global transformation matrix.

members.name: ViewportSetMeasureRenderTime
members.signature: public void ViewportSetMeasureRenderTime(RID viewport, bool enable)
members.summary: Sets the measurement for the given [param viewport] RID (obtained using [method Viewport.get_viewport_rid]). Once enabled, [method viewport_get_measured_render_time_cpu] and [method viewport_get_measured_render_time_gpu] will return values greater than [code]0.0[/code] when queried with the given [param viewport].

members.name: ViewportSetMsaa2d
members.signature: public void ViewportSetMsaa2d(RID viewport, int msaa)
members.summary: Sets the multisample antialiasing mode for 2D/Canvas on the specified [param viewport] RID. Equivalent to [member ProjectSettings.rendering/anti_aliasing/quality/msaa_2d] or [member Viewport.msaa_2d].

members.name: ViewportSetMsaa3d
members.signature: public void ViewportSetMsaa3d(RID viewport, int msaa)
members.summary: Sets the multisample antialiasing mode for 3D on the specified [param viewport] RID. Equivalent to [member ProjectSettings.rendering/anti_aliasing/quality/msaa_3d] or [member Viewport.msaa_3d].

members.name: ViewportSetOcclusionCullingBuildQuality
members.signature: public void ViewportSetOcclusionCullingBuildQuality(int quality)
members.summary: Sets the [member ProjectSettings.rendering/occlusion_culling/bvh_build_quality] to use for occlusion culling. This parameter is global and cannot be set on a per-viewport basis.

members.name: ViewportSetOcclusionRaysPerThread
members.signature: public void ViewportSetOcclusionRaysPerThread(int raysPerThread)
members.summary: Sets the [member ProjectSettings.rendering/occlusion_culling/occlusion_rays_per_thread] to use for occlusion culling. This parameter is global and cannot be set on a per-viewport basis.

members.name: ViewportSetParentViewport
members.signature: public void ViewportSetParentViewport(RID viewport, RID parentViewport)
members.summary: Sets the viewport's parent to the viewport specified by the [param parent_viewport] RID.

members.name: ViewportSetPositionalShadowAtlasQuadrantSubdivision
members.signature: public void ViewportSetPositionalShadowAtlasQuadrantSubdivision(RID viewport, int quadrant, int subdivision)
members.summary: Sets the number of subdivisions to use in the specified shadow atlas [param quadrant] for omni and spot shadows. See also [method Viewport.set_positional_shadow_atlas_quadrant_subdiv].

members.name: ViewportSetPositionalShadowAtlasSize
members.signature: public void ViewportSetPositionalShadowAtlasSize(RID viewport, int size, bool use16Bits)
members.summary: Sets the [param size] of the shadow atlas's images (used for omni and spot lights) on the viewport specified by the [param viewport] RID. The value is rounded up to the nearest power of 2. If [param use_16_bits] is [code]true[/code], use 16 bits for the omni/spot shadow depth map. Enabling this results in shadows having less precision and may result in shadow acne, but can lead to performance improvements on some devices. [b]Note:[/b] If this is set to [code]0[/code], no positional shadows will be visible at all. This can improve performance significantly on low-end systems by reducing both the CPU and GPU load (as fewer draw calls are needed to draw the scene without shadows).

members.name: ViewportSetRenderDirectToScreen
members.signature: public void ViewportSetRenderDirectToScreen(RID viewport, bool enabled)
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], render the contents of the viewport directly to screen. This allows a low-level optimization where you can skip drawing a viewport to the root viewport. While this optimization can result in a significant increase in speed (especially on older devices), it comes at a cost of usability. When this is enabled, you cannot read from the viewport or from the screen_texture. You also lose the benefit of certain window settings, such as the various stretch modes. Another consequence to be aware of is that in 2D the rendering happens in window coordinates, so if you have a viewport that is double the size of the window, and you set this, then only the portion that fits within the window will be drawn, no automatic scaling is possible, even if your game scene is significantly larger than the window size.

members.name: ViewportSetScaling3dMode
members.signature: public void ViewportSetScaling3dMode(RID viewport, int scaling3dMode)
members.summary: Sets the 3D resolution scaling mode. Bilinear scaling renders at different resolution to either undersample or supersample the viewport. FidelityFX Super Resolution 1.0, abbreviated to FSR, is an upscaling technology that produces high quality images at fast framerates by using a spatially aware upscaling algorithm. FSR is slightly more expensive than bilinear, but it produces significantly higher image quality. FSR should be used where possible.

members.name: ViewportSetScaling3dScale
members.signature: public void ViewportSetScaling3dScale(RID viewport, float scale)
members.summary: Scales the 3D render buffer based on the viewport size uses an image filter specified in [enum ViewportScaling3DMode] to scale the output image to the full viewport size. Values lower than [code]1.0[/code] can be used to speed up 3D rendering at the cost of quality (undersampling). Values greater than [code]1.0[/code] are only valid for bilinear mode and can be used to improve 3D rendering quality at a high performance cost (supersampling). See also [enum ViewportMSAA] for multi-sample antialiasing, which is significantly cheaper but only smoothens the edges of polygons. When using FSR upscaling, AMD recommends exposing the following values as preset options to users "Ultra Quality: 0.77", "Quality: 0.67", "Balanced: 0.59", "Performance: 0.5" instead of exposing the entire scale.

members.name: ViewportSetScenario
members.signature: public void ViewportSetScenario(RID viewport, RID scenario)
members.summary: Sets a viewport's scenario. The scenario contains information about environment information, reflection atlas, etc.

members.name: ViewportSetScreenSpaceAa
members.signature: public void ViewportSetScreenSpaceAa(RID viewport, int mode)
members.summary: Sets the viewport's screen-space antialiasing mode. Equivalent to [member ProjectSettings.rendering/anti_aliasing/quality/screen_space_aa] or [member Viewport.screen_space_aa].

members.name: ViewportSetSdfOversizeAndScale
members.signature: public void ViewportSetSdfOversizeAndScale(RID viewport, int oversize, int scale)
members.summary: Sets the viewport's 2D signed distance field [member ProjectSettings.rendering/2d/sdf/oversize] and [member ProjectSettings.rendering/2d/sdf/scale]. This is used when sampling the signed distance field in [CanvasItem] shaders as well as [GPUParticles2D] collision. This is [i]not[/i] used by SDFGI in 3D rendering.

members.name: ViewportSetSize
members.signature: public void ViewportSetSize(RID viewport, int width, int height, int viewCount)
members.summary: Sets the viewport's [param width] and [param height] in pixels. Optionally the [param view_count] can be set to increase the number of view layers for stereo rendering.

members.name: ViewportSetSnap2dTransformsToPixel
members.signature: public void ViewportSetSnap2dTransformsToPixel(RID viewport, bool enabled)
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], canvas item transforms (i.e. origin position) are snapped to the nearest pixel when rendering. This can lead to a crisper appearance at the cost of less smooth movement, especially when [Camera2D] smoothing is enabled. Equivalent to [member ProjectSettings.rendering/2d/snap/snap_2d_transforms_to_pixel].

members.name: ViewportSetSnap2dVerticesToPixel
members.signature: public void ViewportSetSnap2dVerticesToPixel(RID viewport, bool enabled)
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], canvas item vertices (i.e. polygon points) are snapped to the nearest pixel when rendering. This can lead to a crisper appearance at the cost of less smooth movement, especially when [Camera2D] smoothing is enabled. Equivalent to [member ProjectSettings.rendering/2d/snap/snap_2d_vertices_to_pixel].

members.name: ViewportSetTextureMipmapBias
members.signature: public void ViewportSetTextureMipmapBias(RID viewport, float mipmapBias)
members.summary: Affects the final texture sharpness by reading from a lower or higher mipmap (also called "texture LOD bias"). Negative values make mipmapped textures sharper but grainier when viewed at a distance, while positive values make mipmapped textures blurrier (even when up close). To get sharper textures at a distance without introducing too much graininess, set this between [code]-0.75[/code] and [code]0.0[/code]. Enabling temporal antialiasing ([member ProjectSettings.rendering/anti_aliasing/quality/use_taa]) can help reduce the graininess visible when using negative mipmap bias. [b]Note:[/b] When the 3D scaling mode is set to FSR 1.0, this value is used to adjust the automatic mipmap bias which is calculated internally based on the scale factor. The formula for this is [code]-log2(1.0 / scale) + mipmap_bias[/code]. [b]Note:[/b] This method is only supported in the Forward+ and Mobile renderers, not Compatibility. In Compatibility, this method is always treated as if [param mipmap_bias] was set to [code]0.0[/code].

members.name: ViewportSetTransparentBackground
members.signature: public void ViewportSetTransparentBackground(RID viewport, bool enabled)
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the viewport renders its background as transparent.

members.name: ViewportSetUpdateMode
members.signature: public void ViewportSetUpdateMode(RID viewport, int updateMode)
members.summary: Sets when the viewport should be updated.

members.name: ViewportSetUseDebanding
members.signature: public void ViewportSetUseDebanding(RID viewport, bool enable)
members.summary: Equivalent to [member Viewport.use_debanding]. See also [member ProjectSettings.rendering/anti_aliasing/quality/use_debanding].

members.name: ViewportSetUseHdr2d
members.signature: public void ViewportSetUseHdr2d(RID viewport, bool enabled)
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], 2D rendering will use a high dynamic range (HDR) [code]RGBA16[/code] format framebuffer. Additionally, 2D rendering will be performed on linear values and will be converted using the appropriate transfer function immediately before blitting to the screen (if the Viewport is attached to the screen). Practically speaking, this means that the end result of the Viewport will not be clamped to the [code]0-1[/code] range and can be used in 3D rendering without color encoding adjustments. This allows 2D rendering to take advantage of effects requiring high dynamic range (e.g. 2D glow) as well as substantially improves the appearance of effects requiring highly detailed gradients. This setting has the same effect as [member Viewport.use_hdr_2d].

members.name: ViewportSetUseOcclusionCulling
members.signature: public void ViewportSetUseOcclusionCulling(RID viewport, bool enable)
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], enables occlusion culling on the specified viewport. Equivalent to [member ProjectSettings.rendering/occlusion_culling/use_occlusion_culling].

members.name: ViewportSetUseTaa
members.signature: public void ViewportSetUseTaa(RID viewport, bool enable)
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], use temporal antialiasing. Equivalent to [member ProjectSettings.rendering/anti_aliasing/quality/use_taa] or [member Viewport.use_taa].

members.name: ViewportSetUseXr
members.signature: public void ViewportSetUseXr(RID viewport, bool useXr)
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the viewport uses augmented or virtual reality technologies. See [XRInterface].

members.name: ViewportSetVrsMode
members.signature: public void ViewportSetVrsMode(RID viewport, int mode)
members.summary: Sets the Variable Rate Shading (VRS) mode for the viewport. If the GPU does not support VRS, this property is ignored. Equivalent to [member ProjectSettings.rendering/vrs/mode].

members.name: ViewportSetVrsTexture
members.signature: public void ViewportSetVrsTexture(RID viewport, RID texture)
members.summary: The texture to use when the VRS mode is set to [constant RenderingServer.VIEWPORT_VRS_TEXTURE]. Equivalent to [member ProjectSettings.rendering/vrs/texture].

members.name: ViewportSetVrsUpdateMode
members.signature: public void ViewportSetVrsUpdateMode(RID viewport, int mode)
members.summary: Sets the update mode for Variable Rate Shading (VRS) for the viewport. VRS requires the input texture to be converted to the format usable by the VRS method supported by the hardware. The update mode defines how often this happens. If the GPU does not support VRS, or VRS is not enabled, this property is ignored. If set to [constant RenderingServer.VIEWPORT_VRS_UPDATE_ONCE], the input texture is copied once and the mode is changed to [constant RenderingServer.VIEWPORT_VRS_UPDATE_DISABLED].

members.name: VisibilityNotifierCreate
members.signature: public RID VisibilityNotifierCreate()
members.summary: Creates a new 3D visibility notifier object and adds it to the RenderingServer. It can be accessed with the RID that is returned. This RID will be used in all [code]visibility_notifier_*[/code] RenderingServer functions. Once finished with your RID, you will want to free the RID using the RenderingServer's [method free_rid] method. To place in a scene, attach this notifier to an instance using [method instance_set_base] using the returned RID. [b]Note:[/b] The equivalent node is [VisibleOnScreenNotifier3D].

members.name: VisibilityNotifierSetAabb
members.signature: public void VisibilityNotifierSetAabb(RID notifier, AABB aabb)
members.summary: Sets the AABB of the specified visibility notifier.

members.name: VisibilityNotifierSetCallbacks
members.signature: public void VisibilityNotifierSetCallbacks(RID notifier, Callable enterCallable, Callable exitCallable)
members.summary: Sets the methods to be called when the notifier enters or exits the view.

members.name: VoxelGiAllocateData
members.signature: public void VoxelGiAllocateData(RID voxelGi, Transform3D toCellXform, AABB aabb, Vector3i octreeSize, PackedByteArray octreeCells, PackedByteArray dataCells, PackedByteArray distanceField, PackedInt32Array levelCounts)
members.summary: Allocates and initializes the voxel GI data for the specified [param voxel_gi] RID. [param octree_cells] must be a multiple of 32. [param octree_cells] must be double the size of [param data_cells]. The allocated data can be retrieved later using the various [code]voxel_gi_get_*[/code] methods.

members.name: VoxelGiCreate
members.signature: public RID VoxelGiCreate()
members.summary: Creates a new voxel-based global illumination object and adds it to the RenderingServer. It can be accessed with the RID that is returned. This RID will be used in all [code]voxel_gi_*[/code] RenderingServer functions. Once finished with your RID, you will want to free the RID using the RenderingServer's [method free_rid] method. [b]Note:[/b] The equivalent node is [VoxelGI].

members.name: VoxelGiGetDataCells
members.signature: public PackedByteArray VoxelGiGetDataCells(RID voxelGi)
members.summary: Returns the data cells for the specified voxel GI data instance. See also [method voxel_gi_allocate_data].

members.name: VoxelGiGetDistanceField
members.signature: public PackedByteArray VoxelGiGetDistanceField(RID voxelGi)
members.summary: Returns the distance field data for the specified voxel GI data instance. See also [method voxel_gi_allocate_data].

members.name: VoxelGiGetLevelCounts
members.signature: public PackedInt32Array VoxelGiGetLevelCounts(RID voxelGi)
members.summary: Returns the level counts for the specified voxel GI data instance. See also [method voxel_gi_allocate_data].

members.name: VoxelGiGetOctreeCells
members.signature: public PackedByteArray VoxelGiGetOctreeCells(RID voxelGi)
members.summary: Returns the octree cell data for the specified voxel GI data instance. See also [method voxel_gi_allocate_data].

members.name: VoxelGiGetOctreeSize
members.signature: public Vector3i VoxelGiGetOctreeSize(RID voxelGi)
members.summary: Returns the octree size for the specified voxel GI data instance, which corresponds to the number of subdivisions per axis. This can be viewed in the editor by hovering the [b]Bake VoxelGI[/b] button at the top of the 3D editor viewport when a [VoxelGI] node is selected and looking at the [b]Subdivisions[/b] field in the tooltip.

members.name: VoxelGiGetToCellXform
members.signature: public Transform3D VoxelGiGetToCellXform(RID voxelGi)
members.summary: Returns the transform to cell space for the specified voxel GI data instance. See also [method voxel_gi_allocate_data].

members.name: VoxelGiSetBakedExposureNormalization
members.signature: public void VoxelGiSetBakedExposureNormalization(RID voxelGi, float bakedExposure)
members.summary: Used to inform the renderer what exposure normalization value was used while baking the voxel gi. This value will be used and modulated at run time to ensure that the voxel gi maintains a consistent level of exposure even if the scene-wide exposure normalization is changed at run time. For more information see [method camera_attributes_set_exposure].

members.name: VoxelGiSetBias
members.signature: public void VoxelGiSetBias(RID voxelGi, float bias)
members.summary: Sets the [member VoxelGIData.bias] value to use on the specified [param voxel_gi]'s [RID].

members.name: VoxelGiSetDynamicRange
members.signature: public void VoxelGiSetDynamicRange(RID voxelGi, float range)
members.summary: Sets the [member VoxelGIData.dynamic_range] value to use on the specified [param voxel_gi]'s [RID].

members.name: VoxelGiSetEnergy
members.signature: public void VoxelGiSetEnergy(RID voxelGi, float energy)
members.summary: Sets the [member VoxelGIData.energy] value to use on the specified [param voxel_gi]'s [RID].

members.name: VoxelGiSetInterior
members.signature: public void VoxelGiSetInterior(RID voxelGi, bool enable)
members.summary: Sets the [member VoxelGIData.interior] value to use on the specified [param voxel_gi]'s [RID].

members.summary: Sets the [member VoxelGIData.propagation] value to use on the specified [param voxel_gi]'s [RID].

members.name: RenderLoopEnabled
members.signature: public bool RenderLoopEnabled { get; set; }
members.summary: If [code]false[/code], disables rendering completely, but the engine logic is still being processed. You can call [method force_draw] to draw a frame even with rendering disabled.

signals.name: FramePostDraw
signals.summary: Emitted at the end of the frame, after the RenderingServer has finished updating all the Viewports.

signals.name: FramePreDraw
signals.summary: Emitted at the beginning of the frame, before the RenderingServer updates all the Viewports.

values.name: ARRAY_CUSTOM_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum ArrayCustomFormat] enum.

values.name: ARRAY_CUSTOM_RGBA8_SNORM
values.summary: Custom data array contains 8-bit-per-channel red/green/blue/alpha color data. Values are normalized, signed floating-point in the [code][-1.0, 1.0][/code] range.

values.name: ARRAY_CUSTOM_RGBA8_UNORM
values.summary: Custom data array contains 8-bit-per-channel red/green/blue/alpha color data. Values are normalized, unsigned floating-point in the [code][0.0, 1.0][/code] range.

values.name: ARRAY_CUSTOM_RGBA_FLOAT
values.summary: Custom data array contains 32-bit-per-channel red/green/blue/alpha color data. Values are floating-point in single precision.

values.name: ARRAY_CUSTOM_RGBA_HALF
values.summary: Custom data array contains 16-bit-per-channel red/green/blue/alpha color data. Values are floating-point in half precision.

values.name: ARRAY_CUSTOM_RGB_FLOAT
values.summary: Custom data array contains 32-bit-per-channel red/green/blue color data. Values are floating-point in single precision.

values.name: ARRAY_CUSTOM_RG_FLOAT
values.summary: Custom data array contains 32-bit-per-channel red/green color data. Values are floating-point in single precision.

values.name: ARRAY_CUSTOM_RG_HALF
values.summary: Custom data array contains 16-bit-per-channel red/green color data. Values are floating-point in half precision.

values.name: ARRAY_CUSTOM_R_FLOAT
values.summary: Custom data array contains 32-bit-per-channel red color data. Values are floating-point in single precision.

values.name: ARRAY_COMPRESS_FLAGS_BASE
values.summary: Shift of first compress flag. Compress flags should be passed to [method ArrayMesh.add_surface_from_arrays] and [method SurfaceTool.commit].

values.name: ARRAY_FLAG_COMPRESS_ATTRIBUTES
values.summary: Flag used to mark that a mesh is using compressed attributes (vertices, normals, tangents, UVs). When this form of compression is enabled, vertex positions will be packed into an RGBA16UNORM attribute and scaled in the vertex shader. The normal and tangent will be packed into an RG16UNORM representing an axis, and a 16-bit float stored in the A-channel of the vertex. UVs will use 16-bit normalized floats instead of full 32-bit signed floats. When using this compression mode you must use either vertices, normals, and tangents or only vertices. You cannot use normals without tangents. Importers will automatically enable this compression if they can.

values.name: ARRAY_FLAG_FORMAT_CURRENT_VERSION
values.summary: Flag used to record the current version that the engine expects. Currently this is the same as [constant ARRAY_FLAG_FORMAT_VERSION_2].

values.name: ARRAY_FLAG_FORMAT_VERSION_1
values.summary: Flag used to record the format used by prior mesh versions before the introduction of a version.

values.name: ARRAY_FLAG_FORMAT_VERSION_2
values.summary: Flag used to record the second iteration of the mesh version flag. The primary difference between this and [constant ARRAY_FLAG_FORMAT_VERSION_1] is that this version supports [constant ARRAY_FLAG_COMPRESS_ATTRIBUTES] and in this version vertex positions are de-interleaved from normals and tangents.

values.name: ARRAY_FLAG_FORMAT_VERSION_BASE
values.summary: Flag used to mark the start of the bits used to store the mesh version.

values.name: ARRAY_FLAG_FORMAT_VERSION_MASK
values.summary: Flag used to isolate the bits used for mesh version after using [constant ARRAY_FLAG_FORMAT_VERSION_SHIFT] to shift them into place.

values.name: ARRAY_FLAG_FORMAT_VERSION_SHIFT
values.summary: Flag used to shift a mesh format int to bring the version into the lowest digits.

values.name: ARRAY_FLAG_USES_EMPTY_VERTEX_ARRAY
values.summary: Flag used to mark that the mesh does not have a vertex array and instead will infer vertex positions in the shader using indices and other information.

values.name: ARRAY_FLAG_USE_2D_VERTICES
values.summary: Flag used to mark that the array contains 2D vertices.

values.name: ARRAY_FLAG_USE_8_BONE_WEIGHTS
values.summary: Flag used to mark that the array uses 8 bone weights instead of 4.

values.name: ARRAY_FLAG_USE_DYNAMIC_UPDATE
values.summary: Flag used to mark that the mesh data will use [code]GL_DYNAMIC_DRAW[/code] on GLES. Unused on Vulkan.

values.name: ARRAY_FORMAT_BLEND_SHAPE_MASK
values.summary: Mask of mesh channels permitted in blend shapes.

values.name: ARRAY_FORMAT_BONES
values.summary: Flag used to mark a bone information array.

values.name: ARRAY_FORMAT_COLOR
values.summary: Flag used to mark a vertex color array.

values.name: ARRAY_FORMAT_CUSTOM0
values.summary: Flag used to mark an array of custom per-vertex data for the first set of custom data.

values.name: ARRAY_FORMAT_CUSTOM0_SHIFT
values.summary: Amount to shift [enum ArrayCustomFormat] for custom channel index 0.

values.name: ARRAY_FORMAT_CUSTOM1
values.summary: Flag used to mark an array of custom per-vertex data for the second set of custom data.

values.name: ARRAY_FORMAT_CUSTOM1_SHIFT
values.summary: Amount to shift [enum ArrayCustomFormat] for custom channel index 1.

values.name: ARRAY_FORMAT_CUSTOM2
values.summary: Flag used to mark an array of custom per-vertex data for the third set of custom data.

values.name: ARRAY_FORMAT_CUSTOM2_SHIFT
values.summary: Amount to shift [enum ArrayCustomFormat] for custom channel index 2.

values.name: ARRAY_FORMAT_CUSTOM3
values.summary: Flag used to mark an array of custom per-vertex data for the fourth set of custom data.

values.name: ARRAY_FORMAT_CUSTOM3_SHIFT
values.summary: Amount to shift [enum ArrayCustomFormat] for custom channel index 3.

values.name: ARRAY_FORMAT_CUSTOM_BASE
values.summary: Shift of first custom channel.

values.name: ARRAY_FORMAT_CUSTOM_BITS
values.summary: Number of format bits per custom channel. See [enum ArrayCustomFormat].

values.name: ARRAY_FORMAT_CUSTOM_MASK
values.summary: Mask of custom format bits per custom channel. Must be shifted by one of the SHIFT constants. See [enum ArrayCustomFormat].

values.name: ARRAY_FORMAT_INDEX
values.summary: Flag used to mark an index array.

values.name: ARRAY_FORMAT_NORMAL
values.summary: Flag used to mark a normal array.

values.name: ARRAY_FORMAT_TANGENT
values.summary: Flag used to mark a tangent array.

values.name: ARRAY_FORMAT_TEX_UV
values.summary: Flag used to mark a UV coordinates array.

values.name: ARRAY_FORMAT_TEX_UV2
values.summary: Flag used to mark a UV coordinates array for the second UV coordinates.

values.name: ARRAY_FORMAT_VERTEX
values.summary: Flag used to mark a vertex position array.

values.name: ARRAY_FORMAT_WEIGHTS
values.summary: Flag used to mark a weights array.

values.name: ARRAY_BONES
values.summary: Array contains bone information.

values.name: ARRAY_COLOR
values.summary: Array is a vertex color array.

values.name: ARRAY_CUSTOM0
values.summary: Array is a custom data array for the first set of custom data.

values.name: ARRAY_CUSTOM1
values.summary: Array is a custom data array for the second set of custom data.

values.name: ARRAY_CUSTOM2
values.summary: Array is a custom data array for the third set of custom data.

values.name: ARRAY_CUSTOM3
values.summary: Array is a custom data array for the fourth set of custom data.

values.name: ARRAY_INDEX
values.summary: Array is an index array.

values.name: ARRAY_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum ArrayType] enum.

values.name: ARRAY_NORMAL
values.summary: Array is a normal array.

values.name: ARRAY_TANGENT
values.summary: Array is a tangent array.

values.name: ARRAY_TEX_UV
values.summary: Array is a UV coordinates array.

values.name: ARRAY_TEX_UV2
values.summary: Array is a UV coordinates array for the second set of UV coordinates.

values.name: ARRAY_VERTEX
values.summary: Array is a vertex position array.

values.name: ARRAY_WEIGHTS
values.summary: Array is weight information.

values.name: BAKE_CHANNEL_ALBEDO_ALPHA
values.summary: Index of [Image] in array of [Image]s returned by [method bake_render_uv2]. Image uses [constant Image.FORMAT_RGBA8] and contains albedo color in the [code].rgb[/code] channels and alpha in the [code].a[/code] channel.

values.name: BAKE_CHANNEL_EMISSION
values.summary: Index of [Image] in array of [Image]s returned by [method bake_render_uv2]. Image uses [constant Image.FORMAT_RGBAH] and contains emission color in the [code].rgb[/code] channels and nothing in the [code].a[/code] channel.

values.name: BAKE_CHANNEL_NORMAL
values.summary: Index of [Image] in array of [Image]s returned by [method bake_render_uv2]. Image uses [constant Image.FORMAT_RGBA8] and contains the per-pixel normal of the object in the [code].rgb[/code] channels and nothing in the [code].a[/code] channel. The per-pixel normal is encoded as [code]normal * 0.5 + 0.5[/code].

values.name: BAKE_CHANNEL_ORM
values.summary: Index of [Image] in array of [Image]s returned by [method bake_render_uv2]. Image uses [constant Image.FORMAT_RGBA8] and contains ambient occlusion (from material and decals only) in the [code].r[/code] channel, roughness in the [code].g[/code] channel, metallic in the [code].b[/code] channel and sub surface scattering amount in the [code].a[/code] channel.

values.name: BLEND_SHAPE_MODE_NORMALIZED
values.summary: Blend shapes are normalized.

values.name: BLEND_SHAPE_MODE_RELATIVE
values.summary: Blend shapes are relative to base weight.

values.name: CANVAS_GROUP_MODE_CLIP_AND_DRAW
values.summary: Parent is used for clipping child, but parent is also drawn underneath child as normal before clipping child to its visible area.

values.name: CANVAS_GROUP_MODE_CLIP_ONLY
values.summary: Parent is used for the purposes of clipping only. Child is clipped to the parent's visible area, parent is not drawn.

values.name: CANVAS_GROUP_MODE_DISABLED
values.summary: Child draws over parent and is not clipped.

values.name: CANVAS_ITEM_TEXTURE_FILTER_DEFAULT
values.summary: Uses the default filter mode for this [Viewport].

values.name: CANVAS_ITEM_TEXTURE_FILTER_LINEAR
values.summary: The texture filter blends between the nearest 4 pixels. This makes the texture look smooth from up close, and grainy from a distance (due to mipmaps not being sampled).

values.name: CANVAS_ITEM_TEXTURE_FILTER_LINEAR_WITH_MIPMAPS
values.summary: The texture filter blends between the nearest 4 pixels and between the nearest 2 mipmaps (or uses the nearest mipmap if [member ProjectSettings.rendering/textures/default_filters/use_nearest_mipmap_filter] is [code]true[/code]). This makes the texture look smooth from up close, and smooth from a distance. Use this for non-pixel art textures that may be viewed at a low scale (e.g. due to [Camera2D] zoom or sprite scaling), as mipmaps are important to smooth out pixels that are smaller than on-screen pixels.

values.name: CANVAS_ITEM_TEXTURE_FILTER_LINEAR_WITH_MIPMAPS_ANISOTROPIC
values.summary: The texture filter blends between the nearest 4 pixels and blends between 2 mipmaps (or uses the nearest mipmap if [member ProjectSettings.rendering/textures/default_filters/use_nearest_mipmap_filter] is [code]true[/code]) based on the angle between the surface and the camera view. This makes the texture look smooth from up close, and smooth from a distance. Anisotropic filtering improves texture quality on surfaces that are almost in line with the camera, but is slightly slower. The anisotropic filtering level can be changed by adjusting [member ProjectSettings.rendering/textures/default_filters/anisotropic_filtering_level]. [b]Note:[/b] This texture filter is rarely useful in 2D projects. [constant CANVAS_ITEM_TEXTURE_FILTER_LINEAR_WITH_MIPMAPS] is usually more appropriate in this case.

values.name: CANVAS_ITEM_TEXTURE_FILTER_MAX
values.summary: Max value for [enum CanvasItemTextureFilter] enum.

values.name: CANVAS_ITEM_TEXTURE_FILTER_NEAREST
values.summary: The texture filter reads from the nearest pixel only. This makes the texture look pixelated from up close, and grainy from a distance (due to mipmaps not being sampled).

values.name: CANVAS_ITEM_TEXTURE_FILTER_NEAREST_WITH_MIPMAPS
values.summary: The texture filter reads from the nearest pixel and blends between the nearest 2 mipmaps (or uses the nearest mipmap if [member ProjectSettings.rendering/textures/default_filters/use_nearest_mipmap_filter] is [code]true[/code]). This makes the texture look pixelated from up close, and smooth from a distance. Use this for non-pixel art textures that may be viewed at a low scale (e.g. due to [Camera2D] zoom or sprite scaling), as mipmaps are important to smooth out pixels that are smaller than on-screen pixels.

values.name: CANVAS_ITEM_TEXTURE_FILTER_NEAREST_WITH_MIPMAPS_ANISOTROPIC
values.summary: The texture filter reads from the nearest pixel and blends between 2 mipmaps (or uses the nearest mipmap if [member ProjectSettings.rendering/textures/default_filters/use_nearest_mipmap_filter] is [code]true[/code]) based on the angle between the surface and the camera view. This makes the texture look pixelated from up close, and smooth from a distance. Anisotropic filtering improves texture quality on surfaces that are almost in line with the camera, but is slightly slower. The anisotropic filtering level can be changed by adjusting [member ProjectSettings.rendering/textures/default_filters/anisotropic_filtering_level]. [b]Note:[/b] This texture filter is rarely useful in 2D projects. [constant CANVAS_ITEM_TEXTURE_FILTER_NEAREST_WITH_MIPMAPS] is usually more appropriate in this case.

values.name: CANVAS_ITEM_TEXTURE_REPEAT_DEFAULT
values.summary: Uses the default repeat mode for this [Viewport].

values.name: CANVAS_ITEM_TEXTURE_REPEAT_DISABLED
values.summary: Disables textures repeating. Instead, when reading UVs outside the 0-1 range, the value will be clamped to the edge of the texture, resulting in a stretched out look at the borders of the texture.

values.name: CANVAS_ITEM_TEXTURE_REPEAT_ENABLED
values.summary: Enables the texture to repeat when UV coordinates are outside the 0-1 range. If using one of the linear filtering modes, this can result in artifacts at the edges of a texture when the sampler filters across the edges of the texture.

values.name: CANVAS_ITEM_TEXTURE_REPEAT_MAX
values.summary: Max value for [enum CanvasItemTextureRepeat] enum.

values.name: CANVAS_ITEM_TEXTURE_REPEAT_MIRROR
values.summary: Flip the texture when repeating so that the edge lines up instead of abruptly changing.

values.name: CANVAS_LIGHT_BLEND_MODE_ADD
values.summary: Adds light color additive to the canvas.

values.name: CANVAS_LIGHT_BLEND_MODE_MIX
values.summary: The light adds color depending on transparency.

values.name: CANVAS_LIGHT_BLEND_MODE_SUB
values.summary: Adds light color subtractive to the canvas.

values.name: CANVAS_LIGHT_MODE_DIRECTIONAL
values.summary: 2D directional (sun/moon) light (see [DirectionalLight2D]).

values.name: CANVAS_LIGHT_MODE_POINT
values.summary: 2D point light (see [PointLight2D]).

values.name: CANVAS_LIGHT_FILTER_MAX
values.summary: Max value of the [enum CanvasLightShadowFilter] enum.

values.name: CANVAS_LIGHT_FILTER_NONE
values.summary: Do not apply a filter to canvas light shadows.

values.name: CANVAS_LIGHT_FILTER_PCF13
values.summary: Use PCF13 filtering to filter canvas light shadows.

values.name: CANVAS_LIGHT_FILTER_PCF5
values.summary: Use PCF5 filtering to filter canvas light shadows.

values.name: CANVAS_OCCLUDER_POLYGON_CULL_CLOCKWISE
values.summary: Culling of the canvas occluder is clockwise.

values.name: CANVAS_OCCLUDER_POLYGON_CULL_COUNTER_CLOCKWISE
values.summary: Culling of the canvas occluder is counterclockwise.

values.name: CANVAS_OCCLUDER_POLYGON_CULL_DISABLED
values.summary: Culling of the canvas occluder is disabled.

values.name: CANVAS_TEXTURE_CHANNEL_DIFFUSE
values.summary: Diffuse canvas texture ([member CanvasTexture.diffuse_texture]).

values.name: CANVAS_TEXTURE_CHANNEL_NORMAL
values.summary: Normal map canvas texture ([member CanvasTexture.normal_texture]).

values.name: CANVAS_TEXTURE_CHANNEL_SPECULAR
values.summary: Specular map canvas texture ([member CanvasTexture.specular_texture]).

values.name: COMPOSITOR_EFFECT_CALLBACK_TYPE_POST_OPAQUE
values.summary: The callback is called after our opaque rendering pass, but before our sky is rendered.

values.name: COMPOSITOR_EFFECT_CALLBACK_TYPE_POST_SKY
values.summary: The callback is called after our sky is rendered, but before our back buffers are created (and if enabled, before subsurface scattering and/or screen space reflections).

values.name: COMPOSITOR_EFFECT_CALLBACK_TYPE_POST_TRANSPARENT
values.summary: The callback is called after our transparent rendering pass, but before any built-in post-processing effects and output to our render target.

values.name: COMPOSITOR_EFFECT_CALLBACK_TYPE_PRE_OPAQUE
values.summary: The callback is called before our opaque rendering pass, but after depth prepass (if applicable).

values.name: COMPOSITOR_EFFECT_CALLBACK_TYPE_PRE_TRANSPARENT
values.summary: The callback is called before our transparent rendering pass, but after our sky is rendered and we've created our back buffers.

values.name: COMPOSITOR_EFFECT_FLAG_ACCESS_RESOLVED_COLOR
values.summary: The rendering effect requires the color buffer to be resolved if MSAA is enabled.

values.name: COMPOSITOR_EFFECT_FLAG_ACCESS_RESOLVED_DEPTH
values.summary: The rendering effect requires the depth buffer to be resolved if MSAA is enabled.

values.name: COMPOSITOR_EFFECT_FLAG_NEEDS_MOTION_VECTORS
values.summary: The rendering effect requires motion vectors to be produced.

values.name: COMPOSITOR_EFFECT_FLAG_NEEDS_ROUGHNESS
values.summary: The rendering effect requires normals and roughness g-buffer to be produced (Forward+ only).

values.name: COMPOSITOR_EFFECT_FLAG_NEEDS_SEPARATE_SPECULAR
values.summary: The rendering effect requires specular data to be separated out (Forward+ only).

values.name: CUBEMAP_LAYER_BACK
values.summary: Back face of a [Cubemap].

values.name: CUBEMAP_LAYER_BOTTOM
values.summary: Bottom face of a [Cubemap].

values.name: CUBEMAP_LAYER_FRONT
values.summary: Front face of a [Cubemap].

values.name: CUBEMAP_LAYER_LEFT
values.summary: Left face of a [Cubemap].

values.name: CUBEMAP_LAYER_RIGHT
values.summary: Right face of a [Cubemap].

values.name: CUBEMAP_LAYER_TOP
values.summary: Top face of a [Cubemap].

values.name: DOF_BLUR_QUALITY_HIGH
values.summary: Highest quality DOF blur. Results in the smoothest looking blur by taking the most samples, but is also significantly slower.

values.name: DOF_BLUR_QUALITY_LOW
values.summary: Low quality DOF blur.

values.name: DOF_BLUR_QUALITY_MEDIUM
values.summary: Medium quality DOF blur.

values.name: DOF_BLUR_QUALITY_VERY_LOW
values.summary: Lowest quality DOF blur. This is the fastest setting, but you may be able to see filtering artifacts.

values.name: DOF_BOKEH_BOX
values.summary: Calculate the DOF blur using a box filter. The fastest option, but results in obvious lines in blur pattern.

values.name: DOF_BOKEH_CIRCLE
values.summary: Calculates DOF blur using a circle shaped filter. Best quality and most realistic, but slowest. Use only for areas where a lot of performance can be dedicated to post-processing (e.g. cutscenes).

values.name: DOF_BOKEH_HEXAGON
values.summary: Calculates DOF blur using a hexagon shaped filter.

values.name: DECAL_FILTER_LINEAR
values.summary: Linear filter for decals (use for non-pixel art decals). No mipmaps are used for rendering, which means decals at a distance will look smooth but blurry. This has roughly the same performance cost as using mipmaps.

values.name: DECAL_FILTER_LINEAR_MIPMAPS
values.summary: Linear filter for decals (use for non-pixel art decals). Isotropic mipmaps are used for rendering, which means decals at a distance will look smooth but blurry. This has roughly the same performance cost as not using mipmaps.

values.name: DECAL_FILTER_LINEAR_MIPMAPS_ANISOTROPIC
values.summary: Linear filter for decals (use for non-pixel art decals). Anisotropic mipmaps are used for rendering, which means decals at a distance will look smooth and sharp when viewed from oblique angles. This looks better compared to isotropic mipmaps, but is slower. The level of anisotropic filtering is defined by [member ProjectSettings.rendering/textures/default_filters/anisotropic_filtering_level].

values.name: DECAL_FILTER_NEAREST
values.summary: Nearest-neighbor filter for decals (use for pixel art decals). No mipmaps are used for rendering, which means decals at a distance will look sharp but grainy. This has roughly the same performance cost as using mipmaps.

values.name: DECAL_FILTER_NEAREST_MIPMAPS
values.summary: Nearest-neighbor filter for decals (use for pixel art decals). Isotropic mipmaps are used for rendering, which means decals at a distance will look smooth but blurry. This has roughly the same performance cost as not using mipmaps.

values.name: DECAL_FILTER_NEAREST_MIPMAPS_ANISOTROPIC
values.summary: Nearest-neighbor filter for decals (use for pixel art decals). Anisotropic mipmaps are used for rendering, which means decals at a distance will look smooth and sharp when viewed from oblique angles. This looks better compared to isotropic mipmaps, but is slower. The level of anisotropic filtering is defined by [member ProjectSettings.rendering/textures/default_filters/anisotropic_filtering_level].

values.name: DECAL_TEXTURE_ALBEDO
values.summary: Albedo texture slot in a decal ([member Decal.texture_albedo]).

values.name: DECAL_TEXTURE_EMISSION
values.summary: Emission texture slot in a decal ([member Decal.texture_emission]).

values.name: DECAL_TEXTURE_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum DecalTexture] enum.

values.name: DECAL_TEXTURE_NORMAL
values.summary: Normal map texture slot in a decal ([member Decal.texture_normal]).

values.name: DECAL_TEXTURE_ORM
values.summary: Occlusion/Roughness/Metallic texture slot in a decal ([member Decal.texture_orm]).

values.name: ENV_AMBIENT_SOURCE_BG
values.summary: Gather ambient light from whichever source is specified as the background.

values.name: ENV_AMBIENT_SOURCE_COLOR
values.summary: Specify a specific [Color] for ambient light.

values.name: ENV_AMBIENT_SOURCE_DISABLED
values.summary: Disable ambient light.

values.name: ENV_AMBIENT_SOURCE_SKY
values.summary: Gather ambient light from the [Sky] regardless of what the background is.

values.name: ENV_BG_CAMERA_FEED
values.summary: Displays a camera feed in the background.

values.name: ENV_BG_CANVAS
values.summary: Use a specified canvas layer as the background. This can be useful for instantiating a 2D scene in a 3D world.

values.name: ENV_BG_CLEAR_COLOR
values.summary: Use the clear color as background.

values.name: ENV_BG_COLOR
values.summary: Use a specified color as the background.

values.name: ENV_BG_KEEP
values.summary: Do not clear the background, use whatever was rendered last frame as the background.

values.name: ENV_BG_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum EnvironmentBG] enum.

values.name: ENV_BG_SKY
values.summary: Use a sky resource for the background.

values.name: ENV_FOG_MODE_DEPTH
values.summary: Use a simple fog model defined by start and end positions and a custom curve. While not physically accurate, this model can be useful when you need more artistic control.

values.name: ENV_FOG_MODE_EXPONENTIAL
values.summary: Use a physically-based fog model defined primarily by fog density.

values.name: ENV_GLOW_BLEND_MODE_ADDITIVE
values.summary: Adds the glow effect to the scene.

values.name: ENV_GLOW_BLEND_MODE_MIX
values.summary: Mixes the glow image with the scene image. Best used with [member Environment.glow_bloom] to avoid darkening the scene.

values.name: ENV_GLOW_BLEND_MODE_REPLACE
values.summary: Replaces all pixels' color by the glow effect. This can be used to simulate a full-screen blur effect by tweaking the glow parameters to match the original image's brightness or to preview glow configuration in the editor.

values.name: ENV_GLOW_BLEND_MODE_SCREEN
values.summary: Adds the glow effect to the scene after modifying the glow influence based on the scene value; dark values will be highly influenced by glow and bright values will not be influenced by glow. This approach avoids bright values becoming overly bright from the glow effect. [member Environment.tonemap_white] is used to determine the maximum scene value where the glow should have no influence. When [member Environment.tonemap_mode] is set to [constant Environment.TONE_MAPPER_LINEAR] and [member Viewport.use_hdr_2d] is [code]true[/code], the parent window's [method Window.get_output_max_linear_value] will be used as the maximum scene value.

values.name: ENV_GLOW_BLEND_MODE_SOFTLIGHT
values.summary: Adds the glow effect to the tonemapped image after modifying the glow influence based on the image value; dark values and bright values will not be influenced by glow and mid-range values will be highly influenced by glow. This approach avoids bright values becoming overly bright from the glow effect. The glow will have the largest influence on image values of [code]0.25[/code] and will have no influence when applied to image values greater than [code]1.0[/code]. [b]Note:[/b] This blend mode does not support HDR output because expects a maximum output value of [code]1.0[/code]. It is recommended to use a different blend mode when rendering to an HDR screen.

values.name: ENV_REFLECTION_SOURCE_BG
values.summary: Use the background for reflections.

values.name: ENV_REFLECTION_SOURCE_DISABLED
values.summary: Disable reflections.

values.name: ENV_REFLECTION_SOURCE_SKY
values.summary: Use the [Sky] for reflections regardless of what the background is.

values.name: ENV_SDFGI_CONVERGE_IN_10_FRAMES
values.summary: Configure SDFGI to fully converge over 10 frames.

values.name: ENV_SDFGI_CONVERGE_IN_15_FRAMES
values.summary: Configure SDFGI to fully converge over 15 frames.

values.name: ENV_SDFGI_CONVERGE_IN_20_FRAMES
values.summary: Configure SDFGI to fully converge over 20 frames.

values.name: ENV_SDFGI_CONVERGE_IN_25_FRAMES
values.summary: Configure SDFGI to fully converge over 25 frames.

values.name: ENV_SDFGI_CONVERGE_IN_30_FRAMES
values.summary: Configure SDFGI to fully converge over 30 frames. This is the least responsive, but creates the least noisy result with a given ray count.

values.name: ENV_SDFGI_CONVERGE_IN_5_FRAMES
values.summary: Converge SDFGI over 5 frames. This is the most responsive, but creates the most noisy result with a given ray count.

values.name: ENV_SDFGI_CONVERGE_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum EnvironmentSDFGIFramesToConverge] enum.

values.name: ENV_SDFGI_UPDATE_LIGHT_IN_16_FRAMES
values.summary: Update indirect light from dynamic lights in SDFGI over 16 frames. This is the least responsive, but has the lowest GPU requirements.

values.name: ENV_SDFGI_UPDATE_LIGHT_IN_1_FRAME
values.summary: Update indirect light from dynamic lights in SDFGI over 1 frame. This is the most responsive, but has the highest GPU requirements.

values.name: ENV_SDFGI_UPDATE_LIGHT_IN_2_FRAMES
values.summary: Update indirect light from dynamic lights in SDFGI over 2 frames.

values.name: ENV_SDFGI_UPDATE_LIGHT_IN_4_FRAMES
values.summary: Update indirect light from dynamic lights in SDFGI over 4 frames.

values.name: ENV_SDFGI_UPDATE_LIGHT_IN_8_FRAMES
values.summary: Update indirect light from dynamic lights in SDFGI over 8 frames.

values.name: ENV_SDFGI_UPDATE_LIGHT_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum EnvironmentSDFGIFramesToUpdateLight] enum.

values.name: ENV_SDFGI_RAY_COUNT_128
values.summary: Throw 128 rays per frame when converging SDFGI. This has very high GPU requirements, but creates the least noisy result.

values.name: ENV_SDFGI_RAY_COUNT_16
values.summary: Throw 16 rays per frame when converging SDFGI.

values.name: ENV_SDFGI_RAY_COUNT_32
values.summary: Throw 32 rays per frame when converging SDFGI.

values.name: ENV_SDFGI_RAY_COUNT_4
values.summary: Throw 4 rays per frame when converging SDFGI. This has the lowest GPU requirements, but creates the most noisy result.

values.name: ENV_SDFGI_RAY_COUNT_64
values.summary: Throw 64 rays per frame when converging SDFGI.

values.name: ENV_SDFGI_RAY_COUNT_8
values.summary: Throw 8 rays per frame when converging SDFGI.

values.name: ENV_SDFGI_RAY_COUNT_96
values.summary: Throw 96 rays per frame when converging SDFGI. This has high GPU requirements.

values.name: ENV_SDFGI_RAY_COUNT_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum EnvironmentSDFGIRayCount] enum.

values.name: ENV_SDFGI_Y_SCALE_100_PERCENT
values.summary: Use 100% scale for SDFGI on the Y (vertical) axis. SDFGI cells will be as tall as they are wide. This is usually the best choice for highly vertical scenes. The downside is that light leaking may become more noticeable with thin floors and ceilings.

values.name: ENV_SDFGI_Y_SCALE_50_PERCENT
values.summary: Use 50% scale for SDFGI on the Y (vertical) axis. SDFGI cells will be twice as short as they are wide. This allows providing increased GI detail and reduced light leaking with thin floors and ceilings. This is usually the best choice for scenes that don't feature much verticality.

values.name: ENV_SDFGI_Y_SCALE_75_PERCENT
values.summary: Use 75% scale for SDFGI on the Y (vertical) axis. This is a balance between the 50% and 100% SDFGI Y scales.

values.name: ENV_SSAO_QUALITY_HIGH
values.summary: High quality screen-space ambient occlusion.

values.name: ENV_SSAO_QUALITY_LOW
values.summary: Low quality screen-space ambient occlusion.

values.name: ENV_SSAO_QUALITY_MEDIUM
values.summary: Medium quality screen-space ambient occlusion.

values.name: ENV_SSAO_QUALITY_ULTRA
values.summary: Highest quality screen-space ambient occlusion. Uses the adaptive target setting which can be dynamically adjusted to smoothly balance performance and visual quality.

values.name: ENV_SSAO_QUALITY_VERY_LOW
values.summary: Lowest quality of screen-space ambient occlusion.

values.name: ENV_SSIL_QUALITY_HIGH
values.summary: High quality screen-space indirect lighting.

values.name: ENV_SSIL_QUALITY_LOW
values.summary: Low quality screen-space indirect lighting.

values.name: ENV_SSIL_QUALITY_MEDIUM
values.summary: High quality screen-space indirect lighting.

values.name: ENV_SSIL_QUALITY_ULTRA
values.summary: Highest quality screen-space indirect lighting. Uses the adaptive target setting which can be dynamically adjusted to smoothly balance performance and visual quality.

values.name: ENV_SSIL_QUALITY_VERY_LOW
values.summary: Lowest quality of screen-space indirect lighting.

values.name: ENV_SSR_ROUGHNESS_QUALITY_DISABLED
values.summary: Lowest quality of roughness filter for screen-space reflections. Rough materials will not have blurrier screen-space reflections compared to smooth (non-rough) materials. This is the fastest option.

values.name: ENV_SSR_ROUGHNESS_QUALITY_HIGH
values.summary: High quality of roughness filter for screen-space reflections. This is the slowest option.

values.name: ENV_SSR_ROUGHNESS_QUALITY_LOW
values.summary: Low quality of roughness filter for screen-space reflections.

values.name: ENV_SSR_ROUGHNESS_QUALITY_MEDIUM
values.summary: Medium quality of roughness filter for screen-space reflections.

values.name: ENV_TONE_MAPPER_ACES
values.summary: Uses a high-contrast film-like tonemapping curve and desaturates bright values for a more realistic appearance. Slightly slower than [constant ENV_TONE_MAPPER_FILMIC]. [b]Note:[/b] This tonemapping operator is called "ACES Fitted" in Godot 3.x. [b]Note:[/b] This tonemapper does not support HDR output because it produces output in the SDR range. It is recommended to use a different tonemapper when rendering to an HDR screen.

values.name: ENV_TONE_MAPPER_AGX
values.summary: Uses an adjustable film-like tonemapping curve and desaturates bright values for a more realistic appearance. Better than other tonemappers at maintaining the hue of colors as they become brighter. The slowest tonemapping option.

values.name: ENV_TONE_MAPPER_FILMIC
values.summary: Uses a film-like tonemapping curve to prevent clipping of bright values and provide better contrast than [constant ENV_TONE_MAPPER_REINHARD]. Slightly slower than [constant ENV_TONE_MAPPER_REINHARD]. [b]Note:[/b] This tonemapper does not support HDR output because it produces output in the SDR range. It is recommended to use a different tonemapper when rendering to an HDR screen.

values.name: ENV_TONE_MAPPER_LINEAR
values.summary: Does not modify color data, resulting in a linear tonemapping curve which unnaturally clips bright values, causing bright lighting to look blown out. The simplest and fastest tonemapper.

values.name: ENV_TONE_MAPPER_REINHARD
values.summary: A simple tonemapping curve that rolls off bright values to prevent clipping. This results in an image that can appear dull and low contrast. Slower than [constant ENV_TONE_MAPPER_LINEAR]. [b]Note:[/b] When [member Environment.tonemap_white] is left at the default value of [code]1.0[/code], [constant ENV_TONE_MAPPER_REINHARD] produces an identical image to [constant ENV_TONE_MAPPER_LINEAR].

values.name: FOG_VOLUME_SHAPE_BOX
values.summary: [FogVolume] will be shaped like a box.

values.name: FOG_VOLUME_SHAPE_CONE
values.summary: [FogVolume] will be shaped like a cone pointing upwards (in local coordinates). The cone's angle is set automatically to fill the size. The cone will be adjusted to fit within the size. Rotate the [FogVolume] node to reorient the cone. Non-uniform scaling via size is not supported (scale the [FogVolume] node instead).

values.name: FOG_VOLUME_SHAPE_CYLINDER
values.summary: [FogVolume] will be shaped like an upright cylinder (in local coordinates). Rotate the [FogVolume] node to reorient the cylinder. The cylinder will be adjusted to fit within the size. Non-uniform scaling via size is not supported (scale the [FogVolume] node instead).

values.name: FOG_VOLUME_SHAPE_ELLIPSOID
values.summary: [FogVolume] will be shaped like an ellipsoid (stretched sphere).
