values.name: FOG_VOLUME_SHAPE_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum FogVolumeShape] enum.

values.name: FOG_VOLUME_SHAPE_WORLD
values.summary: [FogVolume] will have no shape, will cover the whole world and will not be culled.

values.name: GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_BOOL
values.summary: Boolean global shader parameter ([code]global uniform bool ...[/code]).

values.name: GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_BVEC2
values.summary: 2-dimensional boolean vector global shader parameter ([code]global uniform bvec2 ...[/code]).

values.name: GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_BVEC3
values.summary: 3-dimensional boolean vector global shader parameter ([code]global uniform bvec3 ...[/code]).

values.name: GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_BVEC4
values.summary: 4-dimensional boolean vector global shader parameter ([code]global uniform bvec4 ...[/code]).

values.name: GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_COLOR
values.summary: Color global shader parameter ([code]global uniform vec4 ...[/code]). Equivalent to [constant GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_VEC4] in shader code, but exposed as a [Color] in the editor UI.

values.name: GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_FLOAT
values.summary: Single-precision floating-point global shader parameter ([code]global uniform float ...[/code]).

values.name: GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_INT
values.summary: Integer global shader parameter ([code]global uniform int ...[/code]).

values.name: GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_IVEC2
values.summary: 2-dimensional integer vector global shader parameter ([code]global uniform ivec2 ...[/code]).

values.name: GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_IVEC3
values.summary: 3-dimensional integer vector global shader parameter ([code]global uniform ivec3 ...[/code]).

values.name: GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_IVEC4
values.summary: 4-dimensional integer vector global shader parameter ([code]global uniform ivec4 ...[/code]).

values.name: GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_MAT2
values.summary: 2×2 matrix global shader parameter ([code]global uniform mat2 ...[/code]). Exposed as a [PackedInt32Array] in the editor UI.

values.name: GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_MAT3
values.summary: 3×3 matrix global shader parameter ([code]global uniform mat3 ...[/code]). Exposed as a [Basis] in the editor UI.

values.name: GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_MAT4
values.summary: 4×4 matrix global shader parameter ([code]global uniform mat4 ...[/code]). Exposed as a [Projection] in the editor UI.

values.name: GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum GlobalShaderParameterType] enum.

values.name: GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_RECT2
values.summary: 2-dimensional floating-point rectangle global shader parameter ([code]global uniform vec4 ...[/code]). Equivalent to [constant GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_VEC4] in shader code, but exposed as a [Rect2] in the editor UI.

values.name: GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_RECT2I
values.summary: 2-dimensional integer rectangle global shader parameter ([code]global uniform ivec4 ...[/code]). Equivalent to [constant GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_IVEC4] in shader code, but exposed as a [Rect2i] in the editor UI.

values.name: GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_SAMPLER2D
values.summary: 2D sampler global shader parameter ([code]global uniform sampler2D ...[/code]). Exposed as a [Texture2D] in the editor UI.

values.name: GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_SAMPLER2DARRAY
values.summary: 2D sampler array global shader parameter ([code]global uniform sampler2DArray ...[/code]). Exposed as a [Texture2DArray] in the editor UI.

values.name: GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_SAMPLER3D
values.summary: 3D sampler global shader parameter ([code]global uniform sampler3D ...[/code]). Exposed as a [Texture3D] in the editor UI.

values.name: GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_SAMPLERCUBE
values.summary: Cubemap sampler global shader parameter ([code]global uniform samplerCube ...[/code]). Exposed as a [Cubemap] in the editor UI.

values.name: GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_SAMPLEREXT
values.summary: External sampler global shader parameter ([code]global uniform samplerExternalOES ...[/code]). Exposed as an [ExternalTexture] in the editor UI.

values.name: GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_TRANSFORM
values.summary: 3-dimensional transform global shader parameter ([code]global uniform mat3x4 ...[/code]). Exposed as a [Transform3D] in the editor UI.

values.name: GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_TRANSFORM_2D
values.summary: 2-dimensional transform global shader parameter ([code]global uniform mat2x3 ...[/code]). Exposed as a [Transform2D] in the editor UI.

values.name: GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_UINT
values.summary: Unsigned integer global shader parameter ([code]global uniform uint ...[/code]).

values.name: GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_UVEC2
values.summary: 2-dimensional unsigned integer vector global shader parameter ([code]global uniform uvec2 ...[/code]).

values.name: GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_UVEC3
values.summary: 3-dimensional unsigned integer vector global shader parameter ([code]global uniform uvec3 ...[/code]).

values.name: GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_UVEC4
values.summary: 4-dimensional unsigned integer vector global shader parameter ([code]global uniform uvec4 ...[/code]).

values.name: GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_VEC2
values.summary: 2-dimensional floating-point vector global shader parameter ([code]global uniform vec2 ...[/code]).

values.name: GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_VEC3
values.summary: 3-dimensional floating-point vector global shader parameter ([code]global uniform vec3 ...[/code]).

values.name: GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_VEC4
values.summary: 4-dimensional floating-point vector global shader parameter ([code]global uniform vec4 ...[/code]).

values.name: INSTANCE_FLAG_DRAW_NEXT_FRAME_IF_VISIBLE
values.summary: When set, manually requests to draw geometry on next frame.

values.name: INSTANCE_FLAG_IGNORE_OCCLUSION_CULLING
values.summary: Always draw, even if the instance would be culled by occlusion culling. Does not affect view frustum culling.

values.name: INSTANCE_FLAG_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum InstanceFlags] enum.

values.name: INSTANCE_FLAG_USE_BAKED_LIGHT
values.summary: Allows the instance to be used in baked lighting.

values.name: INSTANCE_FLAG_USE_DYNAMIC_GI
values.summary: Allows the instance to be used with dynamic global illumination.

values.name: INSTANCE_DECAL
values.summary: The instance is a decal.

values.name: INSTANCE_FOG_VOLUME
values.summary: The instance is a fog volume.

values.name: INSTANCE_GEOMETRY_MASK
values.summary: A combination of the flags of geometry instances (mesh, multimesh, immediate and particles).

values.name: INSTANCE_LIGHT
values.summary: The instance is a light.

values.name: INSTANCE_LIGHTMAP
values.summary: The instance is a lightmap.

values.name: INSTANCE_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum InstanceType] enum.

values.name: INSTANCE_MESH
values.summary: The instance is a mesh.

values.name: INSTANCE_MULTIMESH
values.summary: The instance is a multimesh.

values.name: INSTANCE_NONE
values.summary: The instance does not have a type.

values.name: INSTANCE_OCCLUDER
values.summary: The instance is an occlusion culling occluder.

values.name: INSTANCE_PARTICLES
values.summary: The instance is a particle emitter.

values.name: INSTANCE_PARTICLES_COLLISION
values.summary: The instance is a GPUParticles collision shape.

values.name: INSTANCE_REFLECTION_PROBE
values.summary: The instance is a reflection probe.

values.name: INSTANCE_VISIBLITY_NOTIFIER
values.summary: The instance is a visible on-screen notifier.

values.name: INSTANCE_VOXEL_GI
values.summary: The instance is a VoxelGI.

values.name: LIGHT_BAKE_DISABLED
values.summary: Light is ignored when baking. This is the fastest mode, but the light will be taken into account when baking global illumination. This mode should generally be used for dynamic lights that change quickly, as the effect of global illumination is less noticeable on those lights.

values.name: LIGHT_BAKE_DYNAMIC
values.summary: Light is taken into account in dynamic baking ([VoxelGI] and SDFGI ([member Environment.sdfgi_enabled]) only). The light can be moved around or modified with global illumination updating in real-time. The light's global illumination appearance will be slightly different compared to [constant LIGHT_BAKE_STATIC]. This has a greater performance cost compared to [constant LIGHT_BAKE_STATIC]. When using SDFGI, the update speed of dynamic lights is affected by [member ProjectSettings.rendering/global_illumination/sdfgi/frames_to_update_lights].

values.name: LIGHT_BAKE_STATIC
values.summary: Light is taken into account in static baking ([VoxelGI], [LightmapGI], SDFGI ([member Environment.sdfgi_enabled])). The light can be moved around or modified, but its global illumination will not update in real-time. This is suitable for subtle changes (such as flickering torches), but generally not large changes such as toggling a light on and off.

values.name: LIGHT_DIRECTIONAL_SHADOW_ORTHOGONAL
values.summary: Use orthogonal shadow projection for directional light.

values.name: LIGHT_DIRECTIONAL_SHADOW_PARALLEL_2_SPLITS
values.summary: Use 2 splits for shadow projection when using directional light.

values.name: LIGHT_DIRECTIONAL_SHADOW_PARALLEL_4_SPLITS
values.summary: Use 4 splits for shadow projection when using directional light.

values.name: LIGHT_DIRECTIONAL_SKY_MODE_LIGHT_AND_SKY
values.summary: Use DirectionalLight3D in both sky rendering and scene lighting.

values.name: LIGHT_DIRECTIONAL_SKY_MODE_LIGHT_ONLY
values.summary: Only use DirectionalLight3D in scene lighting.

values.name: LIGHT_DIRECTIONAL_SKY_MODE_SKY_ONLY
values.summary: Only use DirectionalLight3D in sky rendering.

values.name: LIGHT_OMNI_SHADOW_CUBE
values.summary: Use a cubemap shadow map for omni lights. Slower but better quality than dual paraboloid.

values.name: LIGHT_OMNI_SHADOW_DUAL_PARABOLOID
values.summary: Use a dual paraboloid shadow map for omni lights.

values.name: LIGHT_PARAM_ATTENUATION
values.summary: The light's attenuation.

values.name: LIGHT_PARAM_ENERGY
values.summary: The light's energy multiplier.

values.name: LIGHT_PARAM_INDIRECT_ENERGY
values.summary: The light's indirect energy multiplier (final indirect energy is [constant LIGHT_PARAM_ENERGY] * [constant LIGHT_PARAM_INDIRECT_ENERGY]).

values.name: LIGHT_PARAM_INTENSITY
values.summary: Constant representing the intensity of the light, measured in Lumens when dealing with a [SpotLight3D] or [OmniLight3D], or measured in Lux with a [DirectionalLight3D]. Only used when [member ProjectSettings.rendering/lights_and_shadows/use_physical_light_units] is [code]true[/code].

values.name: LIGHT_PARAM_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum LightParam] enum.

values.name: LIGHT_PARAM_RANGE
values.summary: The light's range.

values.name: LIGHT_PARAM_SHADOW_BIAS
values.summary: Bias for the shadow lookup to fix self-shadowing artifacts.

values.name: LIGHT_PARAM_SHADOW_BLUR
values.summary: Blurs the edges of the shadow. Can be used to hide pixel artifacts in low resolution shadow maps. A high value can make shadows appear grainy and can cause other unwanted artifacts. Try to keep as near default as possible.

values.name: LIGHT_PARAM_SHADOW_FADE_START
values.summary: Proportion of shadow max distance where the shadow will start to fade out.

values.name: LIGHT_PARAM_SHADOW_MAX_DISTANCE
values.summary: The maximum distance for shadow splits. Increasing this value will make directional shadows visible from further away, at the cost of lower overall shadow detail and performance (since more objects need to be included in the directional shadow rendering).

values.name: LIGHT_PARAM_SHADOW_NORMAL_BIAS
values.summary: Normal bias used to offset shadow lookup by object normal. Can be used to fix self-shadowing artifacts.

values.name: LIGHT_PARAM_SHADOW_OPACITY
values.summary: The light's shadow opacity. Values lower than [code]1.0[/code] make the light appear through shadows. This can be used to fake global illumination at a low performance cost.

values.name: LIGHT_PARAM_SHADOW_PANCAKE_SIZE
values.summary: Sets the size of the directional shadow pancake. The pancake offsets the start of the shadow's camera frustum to provide a higher effective depth resolution for the shadow. However, a high pancake size can cause artifacts in the shadows of large objects that are close to the edge of the frustum. Reducing the pancake size can help. Setting the size to [code]0[/code] turns off the pancaking effect.

values.name: LIGHT_PARAM_SHADOW_SPLIT_1_OFFSET
values.summary: Proportion of shadow atlas occupied by the first split.

values.name: LIGHT_PARAM_SHADOW_SPLIT_2_OFFSET
values.summary: Proportion of shadow atlas occupied by the second split.

values.name: LIGHT_PARAM_SHADOW_SPLIT_3_OFFSET
values.summary: Proportion of shadow atlas occupied by the third split. The fourth split occupies the rest.

values.name: LIGHT_PARAM_SIZE
values.summary: The size of the light when using spot light or omni light. The angular size of the light when using directional light.

values.name: LIGHT_PARAM_SPECULAR
values.summary: The light's influence on specularity.

values.name: LIGHT_PARAM_SPOT_ANGLE
values.summary: The spotlight's angle.

values.name: LIGHT_PARAM_SPOT_ATTENUATION
values.summary: The spotlight's attenuation.

values.name: LIGHT_PARAM_VOLUMETRIC_FOG_ENERGY
values.summary: The light's volumetric fog energy multiplier (final volumetric fog energy is [constant LIGHT_PARAM_ENERGY] * [constant LIGHT_PARAM_VOLUMETRIC_FOG_ENERGY]).

values.name: LIGHT_PROJECTOR_FILTER_LINEAR
values.summary: Linear filter for light projectors (use for non-pixel art light projectors). No mipmaps are used for rendering, which means light projectors at a distance will look smooth but blurry. This has roughly the same performance cost as using mipmaps.

values.name: LIGHT_PROJECTOR_FILTER_LINEAR_MIPMAPS
values.summary: Linear filter for light projectors (use for non-pixel art light projectors). Isotropic mipmaps are used for rendering, which means light projectors at a distance will look smooth but blurry. This has roughly the same performance cost as not using mipmaps.

values.name: LIGHT_PROJECTOR_FILTER_LINEAR_MIPMAPS_ANISOTROPIC
values.summary: Linear filter for light projectors (use for non-pixel art light projectors). Anisotropic mipmaps are used for rendering, which means light projectors at a distance will look smooth and sharp when viewed from oblique angles. This looks better compared to isotropic mipmaps, but is slower. The level of anisotropic filtering is defined by [member ProjectSettings.rendering/textures/default_filters/anisotropic_filtering_level].

values.name: LIGHT_PROJECTOR_FILTER_NEAREST
values.summary: Nearest-neighbor filter for light projectors (use for pixel art light projectors). No mipmaps are used for rendering, which means light projectors at a distance will look sharp but grainy. This has roughly the same performance cost as using mipmaps.

values.name: LIGHT_PROJECTOR_FILTER_NEAREST_MIPMAPS
values.summary: Nearest-neighbor filter for light projectors (use for pixel art light projectors). Isotropic mipmaps are used for rendering, which means light projectors at a distance will look smooth but blurry. This has roughly the same performance cost as not using mipmaps.

values.name: LIGHT_PROJECTOR_FILTER_NEAREST_MIPMAPS_ANISOTROPIC
values.summary: Nearest-neighbor filter for light projectors (use for pixel art light projectors). Anisotropic mipmaps are used for rendering, which means light projectors at a distance will look smooth and sharp when viewed from oblique angles. This looks better compared to isotropic mipmaps, but is slower. The level of anisotropic filtering is defined by [member ProjectSettings.rendering/textures/default_filters/anisotropic_filtering_level].

values.name: LIGHT_AREA
values.summary: Area light (see [AreaLight3D]).

values.name: LIGHT_DIRECTIONAL
values.summary: Directional (sun/moon) light (see [DirectionalLight3D]).

values.name: LIGHT_OMNI
values.summary: Omni light (see [OmniLight3D]).

values.name: LIGHT_SPOT
values.summary: Spot light (see [SpotLight3D]).

values.name: MULTIMESH_INTERP_QUALITY_FAST
values.summary: MultiMesh physics interpolation favors speed over quality.

values.name: MULTIMESH_INTERP_QUALITY_HIGH
values.summary: MultiMesh physics interpolation favors quality over speed.

values.name: MULTIMESH_TRANSFORM_2D
values.summary: Use [Transform2D] to store MultiMesh transform.

values.name: MULTIMESH_TRANSFORM_3D
values.summary: Use [Transform3D] to store MultiMesh transform.

values.name: NINE_PATCH_STRETCH
values.summary: The nine patch gets stretched where needed.

values.name: NINE_PATCH_TILE
values.summary: The nine patch gets filled with tiles where needed.

values.name: NINE_PATCH_TILE_FIT
values.summary: The nine patch gets filled with tiles where needed and stretches them a bit if needed.

values.name: PARTICLES_COLLISION_HEIGHTFIELD_RESOLUTION_1024
values.summary: 1024×1024 heightfield resolution for [GPUParticlesCollisionHeightField3D].

values.name: PARTICLES_COLLISION_HEIGHTFIELD_RESOLUTION_2048
values.summary: 2048×2048 heightfield resolution for [GPUParticlesCollisionHeightField3D].

values.name: PARTICLES_COLLISION_HEIGHTFIELD_RESOLUTION_256
values.summary: 256×256 heightfield resolution for [GPUParticlesCollisionHeightField3D].

values.name: PARTICLES_COLLISION_HEIGHTFIELD_RESOLUTION_4096
values.summary: 4096×4096 heightfield resolution for [GPUParticlesCollisionHeightField3D].

values.name: PARTICLES_COLLISION_HEIGHTFIELD_RESOLUTION_512
values.summary: 512×512 heightfield resolution for [GPUParticlesCollisionHeightField3D].

values.name: PARTICLES_COLLISION_HEIGHTFIELD_RESOLUTION_8192
values.summary: 8192×8192 heightfield resolution for [GPUParticlesCollisionHeightField3D].

values.name: PARTICLES_COLLISION_HEIGHTFIELD_RESOLUTION_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum ParticlesCollisionHeightfieldResolution] enum.

values.name: PARTICLES_COLLISION_TYPE_BOX_ATTRACT
values.summary: Box attractor type for [GPUParticles3D] (see [GPUParticlesAttractorBox3D]).

values.name: PARTICLES_COLLISION_TYPE_BOX_COLLIDE
values.summary: Box collision type for [GPUParticles3D] (see [GPUParticlesCollisionBox3D]).

values.name: PARTICLES_COLLISION_TYPE_HEIGHTFIELD_COLLIDE
values.summary: Heightfield collision type for [GPUParticles3D] (see [GPUParticlesCollisionHeightField3D]).

values.name: PARTICLES_COLLISION_TYPE_SDF_COLLIDE
values.summary: Signed distance field collision type for [GPUParticles3D] (see [GPUParticlesCollisionSDF3D]).

values.name: PARTICLES_COLLISION_TYPE_SPHERE_ATTRACT
values.summary: Sphere attractor type for [GPUParticles3D] (see [GPUParticlesAttractorSphere3D]).

values.name: PARTICLES_COLLISION_TYPE_SPHERE_COLLIDE
values.summary: Sphere collision type for [GPUParticles3D] (see [GPUParticlesCollisionSphere3D]).

values.name: PARTICLES_COLLISION_TYPE_VECTOR_FIELD_ATTRACT
values.summary: Vector field attractor type for [GPUParticles3D] (see [GPUParticlesAttractorVectorField3D]).

values.name: PARTICLES_DRAW_ORDER_INDEX
values.summary: Draw particles in the order that they appear in the particles array.

values.name: PARTICLES_DRAW_ORDER_LIFETIME
values.summary: Sort particles based on their lifetime. In other words, the particle with the highest lifetime is drawn at the front.

values.name: PARTICLES_DRAW_ORDER_REVERSE_LIFETIME
values.summary: Sort particles based on the inverse of their lifetime. In other words, the particle with the lowest lifetime is drawn at the front.

values.name: PARTICLES_DRAW_ORDER_VIEW_DEPTH
values.summary: Sort particles based on their distance to the camera.

values.name: PARTICLES_MODE_2D
values.summary: 2D particles.

values.name: PARTICLES_MODE_3D
values.summary: 3D particles.

values.name: PARTICLES_TRANSFORM_ALIGN_DISABLED
values.summary: Do not align particle transforms relative to the camera or velocity.

values.name: PARTICLES_TRANSFORM_ALIGN_LOCAL_BILLBOARD
values.summary: Billboard each particles around a local axis.

values.name: PARTICLES_TRANSFORM_ALIGN_Y_TO_VELOCITY
values.summary: Align each particle's Y axis to the velocity vector.

values.name: PARTICLES_TRANSFORM_ALIGN_Z_BILLBOARD
values.summary: Align each particle's Z axis to face the camera.

values.name: PARTICLES_TRANSFORM_ALIGN_Z_BILLBOARD_Y_TO_VELOCITY
values.summary: Align each particle's Z axis to face the camera and Y axis to the velocity vector.

values.name: PARTICLES_ALIGN_AXIS_X
values.summary: Use the X axis for local billboarding.

values.name: PARTICLES_ALIGN_AXIS_Y
values.summary: Use the Y axis for local billboarding.

values.name: PARTICLES_ALIGN_CHANNEL_FILTER_DISABLED
values.summary: Do not read from CUSTOM when performing billboarding.

values.name: PARTICLES_ALIGN_CHANNEL_FILTER_W
values.summary: Read from [code]CUSTOM.w[/code] when performing billboarding and use it as an angle, in radians.

values.name: PARTICLES_ALIGN_CHANNEL_FILTER_X
values.summary: Read from [code]CUSTOM.x[/code] when performing billboarding and use it as an angle, in radians.

values.name: PARTICLES_ALIGN_CHANNEL_FILTER_Y
values.summary: Read from [code]CUSTOM.y[/code] when performing billboarding and use it as an angle, in radians.

values.name: PARTICLES_ALIGN_CHANNEL_FILTER_Z
values.summary: Read from [code]CUSTOM.z[/code] when performing billboarding and use it as an angle, in radians.

values.name: PIPELINE_SOURCE_CANVAS
values.summary: Pipeline compilation that was triggered by the 2D canvas renderer.

values.name: PIPELINE_SOURCE_DRAW
values.summary: Pipeline compilation that was triggered while drawing the scene.

values.name: PIPELINE_SOURCE_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum PipelineSource] enum.

values.name: PIPELINE_SOURCE_MESH
values.summary: Pipeline compilation that was triggered by loading a mesh.

values.name: PIPELINE_SOURCE_SPECIALIZATION
values.summary: Pipeline compilation that was triggered to optimize the current scene.

values.name: PIPELINE_SOURCE_SURFACE
values.summary: Pipeline compilation that was triggered by building the surface cache before rendering the scene.

values.name: PRIMITIVE_LINES
values.summary: Primitive to draw consists of lines.

values.name: PRIMITIVE_LINE_STRIP
values.summary: Primitive to draw consists of a line strip from start to end.

values.name: PRIMITIVE_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum PrimitiveType] enum.

values.name: PRIMITIVE_POINTS
values.summary: Primitive to draw consists of points.

values.name: PRIMITIVE_TRIANGLES
values.summary: Primitive to draw consists of triangles.

values.name: PRIMITIVE_TRIANGLE_STRIP
values.summary: Primitive to draw consists of a triangle strip (the last 3 vertices are always combined to make a triangle).

values.name: REFLECTION_PROBE_AMBIENT_COLOR
values.summary: Apply custom ambient lighting inside the reflection probe's box defined by its size. See [method reflection_probe_set_ambient_color] and [method reflection_probe_set_ambient_energy].

values.name: REFLECTION_PROBE_AMBIENT_DISABLED
values.summary: Do not apply any ambient lighting inside the reflection probe's box defined by its size.

values.name: REFLECTION_PROBE_AMBIENT_ENVIRONMENT
values.summary: Apply automatically-sourced environment lighting inside the reflection probe's box defined by its size.

values.name: REFLECTION_PROBE_UPDATE_ALWAYS
values.summary: Reflection probe will update each frame. This mode is necessary to capture moving objects.

values.name: REFLECTION_PROBE_UPDATE_ONCE
values.summary: Reflection probe will update reflections once and then stop.

values.name: RENDERING_INFO_BUFFER_MEM_USED
values.summary: Buffer memory used (in bytes). This includes vertex data, uniform buffers, and many miscellaneous buffer types used internally.

values.name: RENDERING_INFO_PIPELINE_COMPILATIONS_CANVAS
values.summary: Number of pipeline compilations that were triggered by the 2D canvas renderer.

values.name: RENDERING_INFO_PIPELINE_COMPILATIONS_DRAW
values.summary: Number of pipeline compilations that were triggered while drawing the scene. These compilations will show up as stutters during gameplay the first time a user runs the game and the pipeline is required.

values.name: RENDERING_INFO_PIPELINE_COMPILATIONS_MESH
values.summary: Number of pipeline compilations that were triggered by loading meshes. These compilations will show up as longer loading times the first time a user runs the game and the pipeline is required.

values.name: RENDERING_INFO_PIPELINE_COMPILATIONS_SPECIALIZATION
values.summary: Number of pipeline compilations that were triggered to optimize the current scene. These compilations are done in the background and should not cause any stutters whatsoever.

values.name: RENDERING_INFO_PIPELINE_COMPILATIONS_SURFACE
values.summary: Number of pipeline compilations that were triggered by building the surface cache before rendering the scene. These compilations will show up as a stutter when loading a scene the first time a user runs the game and the pipeline is required.

values.name: RENDERING_INFO_TEXTURE_MEM_USED
values.summary: Texture memory used (in bytes).

values.name: RENDERING_INFO_TOTAL_DRAW_CALLS_IN_FRAME
values.summary: Number of draw calls performed to render in the current 3D scene. This varies depending on camera position and rotation.

values.name: RENDERING_INFO_TOTAL_OBJECTS_IN_FRAME
values.summary: Number of objects rendered in the current 3D scene. This varies depending on camera position and rotation.

values.name: RENDERING_INFO_TOTAL_PRIMITIVES_IN_FRAME
values.summary: Number of points, lines, or triangles rendered in the current 3D scene. This varies depending on camera position and rotation.

values.name: RENDERING_INFO_VIDEO_MEM_USED
values.summary: Video memory used (in bytes). When using the Forward+ or Mobile renderers, this is always greater than the sum of [constant RENDERING_INFO_TEXTURE_MEM_USED] and [constant RENDERING_INFO_BUFFER_MEM_USED], since there is miscellaneous data not accounted for by those two metrics. When using the Compatibility renderer, this is equal to the sum of [constant RENDERING_INFO_TEXTURE_MEM_USED] and [constant RENDERING_INFO_BUFFER_MEM_USED].

values.name: SHADER_CANVAS_ITEM
values.summary: Shader is a 2D shader.

values.name: SHADER_FOG
values.summary: Shader is a 3D fog shader.

values.name: SHADER_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum ShaderMode] enum.

values.name: SHADER_PARTICLES
values.summary: Shader is a particle shader (can be used in both 2D and 3D).

values.name: SHADER_SKY
values.summary: Shader is a 3D sky shader.

values.name: SHADER_SPATIAL
values.summary: Shader is a 3D shader.

values.name: SHADER_TEXTURE_BLIT
values.summary: Shader is a texture_blit shader.

values.name: SHADOW_CASTING_SETTING_DOUBLE_SIDED
values.summary: Disable backface culling when rendering the shadow of the object. This is slightly slower but may result in more correct shadows.

values.name: SHADOW_CASTING_SETTING_OFF
values.summary: Disable shadows from this instance.

values.name: SHADOW_CASTING_SETTING_ON
values.summary: Cast shadows from this instance.

values.name: SHADOW_CASTING_SETTING_SHADOWS_ONLY
values.summary: Only render the shadows from the object. The object itself will not be drawn.

values.name: SHADOW_QUALITY_HARD
values.summary: Lowest shadow filtering quality (fastest). Soft shadows are not available with this quality setting, which means the [member Light3D.shadow_blur] property is ignored if [member Light3D.light_size] and [member Light3D.light_angular_distance] is [code]0.0[/code]. [b]Note:[/b] The variable shadow blur performed by [member Light3D.light_size] and [member Light3D.light_angular_distance] is still effective when using hard shadow filtering. In this case, [member Light3D.shadow_blur] [i]is[/i] taken into account. However, the results will not be blurred, instead the blur amount is treated as a maximum radius for the penumbra.

values.name: SHADOW_QUALITY_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum ShadowQuality] enum.

values.name: SHADOW_QUALITY_SOFT_HIGH
values.summary: High low shadow filtering quality (slow). When using this quality setting, [member Light3D.shadow_blur] is automatically multiplied by 1.5× to better make use of the high sample count. This increased blur also improves the stability of dynamic object shadows. This multiplier only applies to lights whose [member Light3D.light_size] or [member Light3D.light_angular_distance] is [code]0.0[/code]).

values.name: SHADOW_QUALITY_SOFT_LOW
values.summary: Low shadow filtering quality (fast).

values.name: SHADOW_QUALITY_SOFT_MEDIUM
values.summary: Medium low shadow filtering quality (average).

values.name: SHADOW_QUALITY_SOFT_ULTRA
values.summary: Highest low shadow filtering quality (slowest). When using this quality setting, [member Light3D.shadow_blur] is automatically multiplied by 2× to better make use of the high sample count. This increased blur also improves the stability of dynamic object shadows. This multiplier only applies to lights whose [member Light3D.light_size] or [member Light3D.light_angular_distance] is [code]0.0[/code]).

values.name: SHADOW_QUALITY_SOFT_VERY_LOW
values.summary: Very low shadow filtering quality (faster). When using this quality setting, [member Light3D.shadow_blur] is automatically multiplied by 0.75× to avoid introducing too much noise. This division only applies to lights whose [member Light3D.light_size] or [member Light3D.light_angular_distance] is [code]0.0[/code]).

values.name: SKY_MODE_AUTOMATIC
values.summary: Automatically selects the appropriate process mode based on your sky shader. If your shader uses [code]TIME[/code] or [code]POSITION[/code], this will use [constant SKY_MODE_REALTIME]. If your shader uses any of the [code]LIGHT_*[/code] variables or any custom uniforms, this uses [constant SKY_MODE_INCREMENTAL]. Otherwise, this defaults to [constant SKY_MODE_QUALITY].

values.name: SKY_MODE_INCREMENTAL
values.summary: Uses the same high quality importance sampling to process the radiance map as [constant SKY_MODE_QUALITY], but updates over several frames. The number of frames is determined by [member ProjectSettings.rendering/reflections/sky_reflections/roughness_layers]. Use this when you need highest quality radiance maps, but have a sky that updates slowly.

values.name: SKY_MODE_QUALITY
values.summary: Uses high quality importance sampling to process the radiance map. In general, this results in much higher quality than [constant SKY_MODE_REALTIME] but takes much longer to generate. This should not be used if you plan on changing the sky at runtime. If you are finding that the reflection is not blurry enough and is showing sparkles or fireflies, try increasing [member ProjectSettings.rendering/reflections/sky_reflections/ggx_samples].

values.name: SKY_MODE_REALTIME
values.summary: Uses the fast filtering algorithm to process the radiance map. In general this results in lower quality, but substantially faster run times. If you need better quality, but still need to update the sky every frame, consider turning on [member ProjectSettings.rendering/reflections/sky_reflections/fast_filter_high_quality]. [b]Note:[/b] The fast filtering algorithm is limited to 256×256 cubemaps, so [method sky_set_radiance_size] must be set to [code]256[/code]. Otherwise, a warning is printed and the overridden radiance size is ignored.

values.name: SPLASH_STRETCH_MODE_COVER
values.summary: Stretches the image to cover the entire screen while preserving aspect ratio.

values.name: SPLASH_STRETCH_MODE_DISABLED
values.summary: No stretching is applied.

values.name: SPLASH_STRETCH_MODE_IGNORE
values.summary: Stretches the image to cover the entire screen but doesn't preserve aspect ratio.

values.name: SPLASH_STRETCH_MODE_KEEP
values.summary: Stretches image to fullscreen while preserving aspect ratio.

values.name: SPLASH_STRETCH_MODE_KEEP_HEIGHT
values.summary: Stretches the width of the image based on the height of the screen.

values.name: SPLASH_STRETCH_MODE_KEEP_WIDTH
values.summary: Stretches the height of the image based on the width of the screen.

values.name: SUB_SURFACE_SCATTERING_QUALITY_DISABLED
values.summary: Disables subsurface scattering entirely, even on materials that have [member BaseMaterial3D.subsurf_scatter_enabled] set to [code]true[/code]. This has the lowest GPU requirements.

values.name: SUB_SURFACE_SCATTERING_QUALITY_HIGH
values.summary: High subsurface scattering quality. This has the highest GPU requirements.

values.name: SUB_SURFACE_SCATTERING_QUALITY_LOW
values.summary: Low subsurface scattering quality.

values.name: SUB_SURFACE_SCATTERING_QUALITY_MEDIUM
values.summary: Medium subsurface scattering quality.

values.name: TEXTURE_DRAWABLE_FORMAT_RGBA8
values.summary: OpenGL texture format RGBA with four components, each with a bitdepth of 8.

values.name: TEXTURE_DRAWABLE_FORMAT_RGBA8_SRGB
values.summary: OpenGL texture format RGBA with four components, each with a bitdepth of 8. When drawn to, an sRGB to linear color space conversion is performed.

values.name: TEXTURE_DRAWABLE_FORMAT_RGBAF
values.summary: OpenGL texture format GL_RGBA32F where there are four components, each a 32-bit floating-point value.

values.name: TEXTURE_DRAWABLE_FORMAT_RGBAH
values.summary: OpenGL texture format GL_RGBA16F where there are four components, each a 16-bit "half-precision" floating-point value.

values.name: TEXTURE_LAYERED_2D_ARRAY
values.summary: Array of 2-dimensional textures (see [Texture2DArray]).

values.name: TEXTURE_LAYERED_CUBEMAP
values.summary: Cubemap texture (see [Cubemap]).

values.name: TEXTURE_LAYERED_CUBEMAP_ARRAY
values.summary: Array of cubemap textures (see [CubemapArray]).

values.name: TEXTURE_TYPE_2D
values.summary: 2D texture.

values.name: TEXTURE_TYPE_3D
values.summary: 3D texture.

values.name: TEXTURE_TYPE_LAYERED
values.summary: Layered texture.

values.name: VIEWPORT_ANISOTROPY_16X
values.summary: Use 16× anisotropic filtering.

values.name: VIEWPORT_ANISOTROPY_2X
values.summary: Use 2× anisotropic filtering.

values.name: VIEWPORT_ANISOTROPY_4X
values.summary: Use 4× anisotropic filtering. This is the default value.

values.name: VIEWPORT_ANISOTROPY_8X
values.summary: Use 8× anisotropic filtering.

values.name: VIEWPORT_ANISOTROPY_DISABLED
values.summary: Anisotropic filtering is disabled.

values.name: VIEWPORT_ANISOTROPY_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum ViewportAnisotropicFiltering] enum.

values.name: VIEWPORT_CLEAR_ALWAYS
values.summary: Always clear the viewport's render target before drawing.

values.name: VIEWPORT_CLEAR_NEVER
values.summary: Never clear the viewport's render target.

values.name: VIEWPORT_CLEAR_ONLY_NEXT_FRAME
values.summary: Clear the viewport's render target on the next frame, then switch to [constant VIEWPORT_CLEAR_NEVER].

values.name: VIEWPORT_DEBUG_DRAW_CLUSTER_DECALS
values.summary: Draws the [Decal] cluster. Clustering determines where decals are positioned in screen-space, which allows the engine to only process these portions of the screen for decals. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when using the Forward+ rendering method.

values.name: VIEWPORT_DEBUG_DRAW_CLUSTER_OMNI_LIGHTS
values.summary: Draws the [OmniLight3D] cluster. Clustering determines where lights are positioned in screen-space, which allows the engine to only process these portions of the screen for lighting. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when using the Forward+ rendering method.

values.name: VIEWPORT_DEBUG_DRAW_CLUSTER_REFLECTION_PROBES
values.summary: Draws the [ReflectionProbe] cluster. Clustering determines where reflection probes are positioned in screen-space, which allows the engine to only process these portions of the screen for reflection probes. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when using the Forward+ rendering method.

values.name: VIEWPORT_DEBUG_DRAW_CLUSTER_SPOT_LIGHTS
values.summary: Draws the [SpotLight3D] cluster. Clustering determines where lights are positioned in screen-space, which allows the engine to only process these portions of the screen for lighting. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when using the Forward+ rendering method.

values.name: VIEWPORT_DEBUG_DRAW_DECAL_ATLAS
values.summary: Draws the decal atlas that stores decal textures from [Decal]s. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when using the Forward+ or Mobile rendering methods.

values.name: VIEWPORT_DEBUG_DRAW_DIRECTIONAL_SHADOW_ATLAS
values.summary: Draws the shadow atlas that stores shadows from [DirectionalLight3D]s in the upper left quadrant of the [Viewport]. The slice of the camera frustum related to the shadow map cascade is superimposed to visualize coverage. The color of each slice matches the colors used for [constant VIEWPORT_DEBUG_DRAW_PSSM_SPLITS]. When shadow cascades are blended the overlap is taken into account when drawing the frustum slices. The last cascade shows all frustum slices to illustrate the coverage of all slices.

values.name: VIEWPORT_DEBUG_DRAW_DISABLED
values.summary: Debug draw is disabled. Default setting.

values.name: VIEWPORT_DEBUG_DRAW_DISABLE_LOD
values.summary: Disable mesh LOD. All meshes are drawn with full detail, which can be used to compare performance.

values.name: VIEWPORT_DEBUG_DRAW_GI_BUFFER
values.summary: Draws the global illumination buffer from [VoxelGI] or SDFGI. Requires [VoxelGI] (at least one visible baked VoxelGI node) or SDFGI ([member Environment.sdfgi_enabled]) to be enabled to have a visible effect. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when using the Forward+ rendering method.

values.name: VIEWPORT_DEBUG_DRAW_INTERNAL_BUFFER
values.summary: Internal buffer is drawn instead of regular scene so you can see the per-pixel output that will be used by post-processing effects. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when using the Forward+ or Mobile rendering methods.

values.name: VIEWPORT_DEBUG_DRAW_LIGHTING
values.summary: Objects are displayed with only light information. [b]Note:[/b] When using this debug draw mode, custom shaders are ignored since all materials in the scene temporarily use a debug material. This means the result from custom shader functions (such as vertex displacement) won't be visible anymore when using this debug draw mode.

values.name: VIEWPORT_DEBUG_DRAW_MOTION_VECTORS
values.summary: Draws the motion vectors buffer. This is used by temporal antialiasing to correct for motion that occurs during gameplay. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when using the Forward+ rendering method.

values.name: VIEWPORT_DEBUG_DRAW_NORMAL_BUFFER
values.summary: Normal buffer is drawn instead of regular scene so you can see the per-pixel normals that will be used by post-processing effects.

values.name: VIEWPORT_DEBUG_DRAW_OCCLUDERS
values.summary: Draws the occlusion culling buffer. This low-resolution occlusion culling buffer is rasterized on the CPU and is used to check whether instances are occluded by other objects. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when using the Forward+ or Mobile rendering methods.

values.name: VIEWPORT_DEBUG_DRAW_OVERDRAW
values.summary: Objects are displayed semi-transparent with additive blending so you can see where they are drawing over top of one another. A higher overdraw (represented by brighter colors) means you are wasting performance on drawing pixels that are being hidden behind others. [b]Note:[/b] When using this debug draw mode, custom shaders are ignored since all materials in the scene temporarily use a debug material. This means the result from custom shader functions (such as vertex displacement) won't be visible anymore when using this debug draw mode.

values.name: VIEWPORT_DEBUG_DRAW_PSSM_SPLITS
values.summary: Colors each PSSM split for the [DirectionalLight3D]s in the scene a different color so you can see where the splits are. In order (from closest to furthest from the camera), they are colored red, green, blue, and yellow. [b]Note:[/b] When using this debug draw mode, custom shaders are ignored since all materials in the scene temporarily use a debug material. This means the result from custom shader functions (such as vertex displacement) won't be visible anymore when using this debug draw mode. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when using the Forward+ or Mobile rendering methods.

values.name: VIEWPORT_DEBUG_DRAW_SCENE_LUMINANCE
values.summary: Draws the estimated scene luminance. This is a 1×1 texture that is generated when autoexposure is enabled to control the scene's exposure. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when using the Forward+ or Mobile rendering methods.

values.name: VIEWPORT_DEBUG_DRAW_SDFGI
values.summary: Draws SDFGI cascade data. This is the data structure that is used to bounce lighting against and create reflections. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when using the Forward+ rendering method.

values.name: VIEWPORT_DEBUG_DRAW_SDFGI_PROBES
values.summary: Draws SDFGI probe data. This is the data structure that is used to give indirect lighting dynamic objects moving within the scene. When in the editor, left-clicking a probe will display additional bright dots that show its occlusion information. A white dot means the light is not occluded at all at the dot's position, while a red dot means the light is fully occluded. Intermediate values are possible. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when using the Forward+ rendering method.

values.name: VIEWPORT_DEBUG_DRAW_SHADOW_ATLAS
values.summary: Draws the shadow atlas that stores shadows from [OmniLight3D]s and [SpotLight3D]s in the upper left quadrant of the [Viewport].

values.name: VIEWPORT_DEBUG_DRAW_SSAO
values.summary: Draws the screen space ambient occlusion texture instead of the scene so that you can clearly see how it is affecting objects. In order for this display mode to work, you must have [member Environment.ssao_enabled] set in your [WorldEnvironment]. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when using the Forward+ rendering method.

values.name: VIEWPORT_DEBUG_DRAW_SSIL
values.summary: Draws the screen space indirect lighting texture instead of the scene so that you can clearly see how it is affecting objects. In order for this display mode to work, you must have [member Environment.ssil_enabled] set in your [WorldEnvironment]. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when using the Forward+ rendering method.

values.name: VIEWPORT_DEBUG_DRAW_UNSHADED
values.summary: Objects are displayed without light information.

values.name: VIEWPORT_DEBUG_DRAW_VOXEL_GI_ALBEDO
values.summary: Objects are displayed with only the albedo value from [VoxelGI]s. Requires at least one visible [VoxelGI] node that has been baked to have a visible effect. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when using the Forward+ rendering method.

values.name: VIEWPORT_DEBUG_DRAW_VOXEL_GI_EMISSION
values.summary: Objects are displayed with only the emission color from [VoxelGI]s. Requires at least one visible [VoxelGI] node that has been baked to have a visible effect. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when using the Forward+ rendering method.

values.name: VIEWPORT_DEBUG_DRAW_VOXEL_GI_LIGHTING
values.summary: Objects are displayed with only the lighting value from [VoxelGI]s. Requires at least one visible [VoxelGI] node that has been baked to have a visible effect. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when using the Forward+ rendering method.

values.name: VIEWPORT_DEBUG_DRAW_WIREFRAME
values.summary: Debug draw draws objects in wireframe. [b]Note:[/b] [method set_debug_generate_wireframes] must be called before loading any meshes for wireframes to be visible when using the Compatibility renderer. [b]Note:[/b] In the Compatibility renderer, backfaces are always visible when using wireframe rendering. In the Forward+ and Mobile renderers, wireframes follow the material's backface culling properties instead.

values.name: VIEWPORT_ENVIRONMENT_DISABLED
values.summary: Disable rendering of 3D environment over 2D canvas.

values.name: VIEWPORT_ENVIRONMENT_ENABLED
values.summary: Enable rendering of 3D environment over 2D canvas.

values.name: VIEWPORT_ENVIRONMENT_INHERIT
values.summary: Inherit enable/disable value from parent. If the topmost parent is also set to [constant VIEWPORT_ENVIRONMENT_INHERIT], then this has the same behavior as [constant VIEWPORT_ENVIRONMENT_ENABLED].

values.name: VIEWPORT_ENVIRONMENT_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum ViewportEnvironmentMode] enum.

values.name: VIEWPORT_MSAA_2X
values.summary: Multisample antialiasing uses 2 samples per pixel for 3D. This has a moderate impact on performance.

values.name: VIEWPORT_MSAA_4X
values.summary: Multisample antialiasing uses 4 samples per pixel for 3D. This has a high impact on performance.

values.name: VIEWPORT_MSAA_8X
values.summary: Multisample antialiasing uses 8 samples per pixel for 3D. This has a very high impact on performance. Likely unsupported on low-end and older hardware.

values.name: VIEWPORT_MSAA_DISABLED
values.summary: Multisample antialiasing for 3D is disabled. This is the default value, and also the fastest setting.

values.name: VIEWPORT_MSAA_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum ViewportMSAA] enum.

values.name: VIEWPORT_OCCLUSION_BUILD_QUALITY_HIGH
values.summary: High occlusion culling BVH build quality (as defined by Embree). Results in the highest CPU usage, but most effective culling.

values.name: VIEWPORT_OCCLUSION_BUILD_QUALITY_LOW
values.summary: Low occlusion culling BVH build quality (as defined by Embree). Results in the lowest CPU usage, but least effective culling.

values.name: VIEWPORT_OCCLUSION_BUILD_QUALITY_MEDIUM
values.summary: Medium occlusion culling BVH build quality (as defined by Embree).

values.name: VIEWPORT_RENDER_INFO_DRAW_CALLS_IN_FRAME
values.summary: Number of draw calls during this frame.

values.name: VIEWPORT_RENDER_INFO_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum ViewportRenderInfo] enum.

values.name: VIEWPORT_RENDER_INFO_OBJECTS_IN_FRAME
values.summary: Number of objects drawn in a single frame.

values.name: VIEWPORT_RENDER_INFO_PRIMITIVES_IN_FRAME
values.summary: Number of points, lines, or triangles drawn in a single frame.

values.name: VIEWPORT_RENDER_INFO_TYPE_CANVAS
values.summary: Canvas item rendering. This includes all 2D rendering.

values.name: VIEWPORT_RENDER_INFO_TYPE_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum ViewportRenderInfoType] enum.

values.name: VIEWPORT_RENDER_INFO_TYPE_SHADOW
values.summary: Shadow render pass. Objects will be rendered several times depending on the number of amounts of lights with shadows and the number of directional shadow splits.

values.name: VIEWPORT_RENDER_INFO_TYPE_VISIBLE
values.summary: Visible render pass (excluding shadows).

values.name: VIEWPORT_SDF_OVERSIZE_100_PERCENT
values.summary: Do not oversize the 2D signed distance field. Occluders may disappear when touching the viewport's edges, and [GPUParticles3D] collision may stop working earlier than intended. This has the lowest GPU requirements.

values.name: VIEWPORT_SDF_OVERSIZE_120_PERCENT
values.summary: 2D signed distance field covers 20% of the viewport's size outside the viewport on each side (top, right, bottom, left).

values.name: VIEWPORT_SDF_OVERSIZE_150_PERCENT
values.summary: 2D signed distance field covers 50% of the viewport's size outside the viewport on each side (top, right, bottom, left).

values.name: VIEWPORT_SDF_OVERSIZE_200_PERCENT
values.summary: 2D signed distance field covers 100% of the viewport's size outside the viewport on each side (top, right, bottom, left). This has the highest GPU requirements.

values.name: VIEWPORT_SDF_OVERSIZE_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum ViewportSDFOversize] enum.

values.name: VIEWPORT_SDF_SCALE_100_PERCENT
values.summary: Full resolution 2D signed distance field scale. This has the highest GPU requirements.

values.name: VIEWPORT_SDF_SCALE_25_PERCENT
values.summary: Quarter resolution 2D signed distance field scale on each axis (6.25% of the viewport pixel count). This has the lowest GPU requirements.

values.name: VIEWPORT_SDF_SCALE_50_PERCENT
values.summary: Half resolution 2D signed distance field scale on each axis (25% of the viewport pixel count).

values.name: VIEWPORT_SDF_SCALE_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum ViewportSDFScale] enum.

values.name: VIEWPORT_SCALING_3D_MODE_BILINEAR
values.summary: Use bilinear scaling for the viewport's 3D buffer. The amount of scaling can be set using [member Viewport.scaling_3d_scale]. Values less than [code]1.0[/code] will result in undersampling while values greater than [code]1.0[/code] will result in supersampling. A value of [code]1.0[/code] disables scaling.

values.name: VIEWPORT_SCALING_3D_MODE_FSR
values.summary: Use AMD FidelityFX Super Resolution 1.0 upscaling for the viewport's 3D buffer. The amount of scaling can be set using [member Viewport.scaling_3d_scale]. Values less than [code]1.0[/code] will result in the viewport being upscaled using FSR. Values greater than [code]1.0[/code] are not supported and bilinear downsampling will be used instead. A value of [code]1.0[/code] disables scaling.

values.name: VIEWPORT_SCALING_3D_MODE_FSR2
values.summary: Use AMD FidelityFX Super Resolution 2.2 upscaling for the viewport's 3D buffer. The amount of scaling can be set using [member Viewport.scaling_3d_scale]. Values less than [code]1.0[/code] will result in the viewport being upscaled using FSR2. Values greater than [code]1.0[/code] are not supported and bilinear downsampling will be used instead. A value of [code]1.0[/code] will use FSR2 at native resolution as a TAA solution.

values.name: VIEWPORT_SCALING_3D_MODE_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum ViewportScaling3DMode] enum.

values.name: VIEWPORT_SCALING_3D_MODE_METALFX_SPATIAL
values.summary: Use MetalFX spatial upscaling for the viewport's 3D buffer. The amount of scaling can be set using [member Viewport.scaling_3d_scale]. Values less than [code]1.0[/code] will result in the viewport being upscaled using MetalFX. Values greater than [code]1.0[/code] are not supported and bilinear downsampling will be used instead. A value of [code]1.0[/code] disables scaling. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when the Metal rendering driver is in use, which limits this scaling mode to macOS and iOS.

values.name: VIEWPORT_SCALING_3D_MODE_METALFX_TEMPORAL
values.summary: Use MetalFX temporal upscaling for the viewport's 3D buffer. The amount of scaling can be set using [member Viewport.scaling_3d_scale]. Values less than [code]1.0[/code] will result in the viewport being upscaled using MetalFX. Values greater than [code]1.0[/code] are not supported and bilinear downsampling will be used instead. A value of [code]1.0[/code] will use MetalFX at native resolution as a TAA solution. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when the Metal rendering driver is in use, which limits this scaling mode to macOS and iOS.

values.name: VIEWPORT_SCALING_3D_MODE_NEAREST
values.summary: Use nearest-neighbor filtering for the viewport's 3D buffer. This looks crisper than [constant VIEWPORT_SCALING_3D_MODE_BILINEAR] and has no additional rendering cost. The amount of scaling can be set using [member Viewport.scaling_3d_scale]. Values greater than [code]1.0[/code] are not supported and bilinear downsampling will be used instead. A value of [code]1.0[/code] disables scaling. [b]Note:[/b] When using the [b]Nearest[/b] scaling mode, to avoid uneven pixel scaling, it's highly recommended to use a value equal to an integer divisor with a dividend of [code]1[/code]. For example, it's best to use a scale of [code]0.5[/code] (1/2), [code]0.3333[/code] (1/3), [code]0.25[/code] (1/4), [code]0.2[/code] (1/5), and so on.

values.name: VIEWPORT_SCREEN_SPACE_AA_DISABLED
values.summary: Do not perform any antialiasing in the full screen post-process.

values.name: VIEWPORT_SCREEN_SPACE_AA_FXAA
values.summary: Use fast approximate antialiasing. FXAA is a popular screen-space antialiasing method, which is fast but will make the image look blurry, especially at lower resolutions. It can still work relatively well at large resolutions such as 1440p and 4K.

values.name: VIEWPORT_SCREEN_SPACE_AA_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum ViewportScreenSpaceAA] enum.

values.name: VIEWPORT_SCREEN_SPACE_AA_SMAA
values.summary: Use subpixel morphological antialiasing. SMAA may produce clearer results than FXAA, but at a slightly higher performance cost.

values.name: VIEWPORT_UPDATE_ALWAYS
values.summary: Always update the viewport's render target.

values.name: VIEWPORT_UPDATE_DISABLED
values.summary: Do not update the viewport's render target.

values.name: VIEWPORT_UPDATE_ONCE
values.summary: Update the viewport's render target once, then switch to [constant VIEWPORT_UPDATE_DISABLED].

values.name: VIEWPORT_UPDATE_WHEN_PARENT_VISIBLE
values.summary: Update the viewport's render target only when its parent is visible.

values.name: VIEWPORT_UPDATE_WHEN_VISIBLE
values.summary: Update the viewport's render target only when it is visible. This is the default value.

values.name: VIEWPORT_VRS_DISABLED
values.summary: Variable rate shading is disabled.

values.name: VIEWPORT_VRS_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum ViewportVRSMode] enum.

values.name: VIEWPORT_VRS_TEXTURE
values.summary: Variable rate shading uses a texture. Note, for stereoscopic use a texture atlas with a texture for each view.

values.name: VIEWPORT_VRS_XR
values.summary: Variable rate shading texture is supplied by the primary [XRInterface]. Note that this may override the update mode.

values.name: VIEWPORT_VRS_UPDATE_ALWAYS
values.summary: The input texture for variable rate shading will be processed each frame.

values.name: VIEWPORT_VRS_UPDATE_DISABLED
values.summary: The input texture for variable rate shading will not be processed.

values.name: VIEWPORT_VRS_UPDATE_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum ViewportVRSUpdateMode] enum.

values.name: VIEWPORT_VRS_UPDATE_ONCE
values.summary: The input texture for variable rate shading will be processed once.

values.name: VISIBILITY_RANGE_FADE_DEPENDENCIES
values.summary: Fade-in the given instance's dependencies when reaching its visibility range limits.

values.name: VISIBILITY_RANGE_FADE_DISABLED
values.summary: Disable visibility range fading for the given instance.

values.name: VISIBILITY_RANGE_FADE_SELF
values.summary: Fade-out the given instance when it approaches its visibility range limits.

values.name: VOXEL_GI_QUALITY_HIGH
values.summary: High [VoxelGI] rendering quality using 6 cones.

values.name: VOXEL_GI_QUALITY_LOW
values.summary: Low [VoxelGI] rendering quality using 4 cones.

constants.name: NO_INDEX_ARRAY
constants.summary: Marks an error that shows that the index array is empty.

constants.name: ARRAY_WEIGHTS_SIZE
constants.summary: Number of weights/bones per vertex.

constants.name: CANVAS_ITEM_Z_MIN
constants.summary: The minimum Z-layer for canvas items.

constants.name: CANVAS_ITEM_Z_MAX
constants.summary: The maximum Z-layer for canvas items.

constants.name: CANVAS_LAYER_MIN
constants.summary: The minimum canvas layer.

constants.name: CANVAS_LAYER_MAX
constants.summary: The maximum canvas layer.

constants.name: MAX_GLOW_LEVELS
constants.summary: The maximum number of glow levels that can be used with the glow post-processing effect.

constants.name: MAX_2D_DIRECTIONAL_LIGHTS
constants.summary: The maximum number of directional lights that can be rendered at a given time in 2D.

constants.name: MAX_MESH_SURFACES
constants.summary: The maximum number of surfaces a mesh can have.

constants.name: TEXTURE_TYPE_2D
constants.summary: 2D texture.

constants.name: TEXTURE_TYPE_LAYERED
constants.summary: Layered texture.

constants.name: TEXTURE_TYPE_3D
constants.summary: 3D texture.

constants.name: TEXTURE_LAYERED_2D_ARRAY
constants.summary: Array of 2-dimensional textures (see [Texture2DArray]).

constants.name: TEXTURE_LAYERED_CUBEMAP
constants.summary: Cubemap texture (see [Cubemap]).

constants.name: TEXTURE_LAYERED_CUBEMAP_ARRAY
constants.summary: Array of cubemap textures (see [CubemapArray]).

constants.name: CUBEMAP_LAYER_LEFT
constants.summary: Left face of a [Cubemap].

constants.name: CUBEMAP_LAYER_RIGHT
constants.summary: Right face of a [Cubemap].

constants.name: CUBEMAP_LAYER_BOTTOM
constants.summary: Bottom face of a [Cubemap].

constants.name: CUBEMAP_LAYER_TOP
constants.summary: Top face of a [Cubemap].

constants.name: CUBEMAP_LAYER_FRONT
constants.summary: Front face of a [Cubemap].

constants.name: CUBEMAP_LAYER_BACK
constants.summary: Back face of a [Cubemap].

constants.name: TEXTURE_DRAWABLE_FORMAT_RGBA8
constants.summary: OpenGL texture format RGBA with four components, each with a bitdepth of 8.

constants.name: TEXTURE_DRAWABLE_FORMAT_RGBA8_SRGB
constants.summary: OpenGL texture format RGBA with four components, each with a bitdepth of 8. When drawn to, an sRGB to linear color space conversion is performed.

constants.name: TEXTURE_DRAWABLE_FORMAT_RGBAH
constants.summary: OpenGL texture format GL_RGBA16F where there are four components, each a 16-bit "half-precision" floating-point value.

constants.name: TEXTURE_DRAWABLE_FORMAT_RGBAF
constants.summary: OpenGL texture format GL_RGBA32F where there are four components, each a 32-bit floating-point value.

constants.name: SHADER_SPATIAL
constants.summary: Shader is a 3D shader.

constants.name: SHADER_CANVAS_ITEM
constants.summary: Shader is a 2D shader.

constants.name: SHADER_PARTICLES
constants.summary: Shader is a particle shader (can be used in both 2D and 3D).

constants.name: SHADER_SKY
constants.summary: Shader is a 3D sky shader.

constants.name: SHADER_FOG
constants.summary: Shader is a 3D fog shader.

constants.name: SHADER_TEXTURE_BLIT
constants.summary: Shader is a texture_blit shader.

constants.name: SHADER_MAX
constants.summary: Represents the size of the [enum ShaderMode] enum.

constants.name: MATERIAL_RENDER_PRIORITY_MIN
constants.summary: The minimum renderpriority of all materials.

constants.name: MATERIAL_RENDER_PRIORITY_MAX
constants.summary: The maximum renderpriority of all materials.

constants.name: ARRAY_VERTEX
constants.summary: Array is a vertex position array.

constants.name: ARRAY_NORMAL
constants.summary: Array is a normal array.

constants.name: ARRAY_TANGENT
constants.summary: Array is a tangent array.

constants.name: ARRAY_COLOR
constants.summary: Array is a vertex color array.

constants.name: ARRAY_TEX_UV
constants.summary: Array is a UV coordinates array.

constants.name: ARRAY_TEX_UV2
constants.summary: Array is a UV coordinates array for the second set of UV coordinates.

constants.name: ARRAY_CUSTOM0
constants.summary: Array is a custom data array for the first set of custom data.

constants.name: ARRAY_CUSTOM1
constants.summary: Array is a custom data array for the second set of custom data.

constants.name: ARRAY_CUSTOM2
constants.summary: Array is a custom data array for the third set of custom data.

constants.name: ARRAY_CUSTOM3
constants.summary: Array is a custom data array for the fourth set of custom data.

constants.name: ARRAY_BONES
constants.summary: Array contains bone information.

constants.name: ARRAY_WEIGHTS
constants.summary: Array is weight information.

constants.name: ARRAY_INDEX
constants.summary: Array is an index array.

constants.name: ARRAY_MAX
constants.summary: Represents the size of the [enum ArrayType] enum.

constants.name: ARRAY_CUSTOM_COUNT
constants.summary: The number of custom data arrays available ([constant ARRAY_CUSTOM0], [constant ARRAY_CUSTOM1], [constant ARRAY_CUSTOM2], [constant ARRAY_CUSTOM3]).

constants.name: ARRAY_CUSTOM_RGBA8_UNORM
constants.summary: Custom data array contains 8-bit-per-channel red/green/blue/alpha color data. Values are normalized, unsigned floating-point in the [code][0.0, 1.0][/code] range.

constants.name: ARRAY_CUSTOM_RGBA8_SNORM
constants.summary: Custom data array contains 8-bit-per-channel red/green/blue/alpha color data. Values are normalized, signed floating-point in the [code][-1.0, 1.0][/code] range.

constants.name: ARRAY_CUSTOM_RG_HALF
constants.summary: Custom data array contains 16-bit-per-channel red/green color data. Values are floating-point in half precision.

constants.name: ARRAY_CUSTOM_RGBA_HALF
constants.summary: Custom data array contains 16-bit-per-channel red/green/blue/alpha color data. Values are floating-point in half precision.

constants.name: ARRAY_CUSTOM_R_FLOAT
constants.summary: Custom data array contains 32-bit-per-channel red color data. Values are floating-point in single precision.

constants.name: ARRAY_CUSTOM_RG_FLOAT
constants.summary: Custom data array contains 32-bit-per-channel red/green color data. Values are floating-point in single precision.

constants.name: ARRAY_CUSTOM_RGB_FLOAT
constants.summary: Custom data array contains 32-bit-per-channel red/green/blue color data. Values are floating-point in single precision.

constants.name: ARRAY_CUSTOM_RGBA_FLOAT
constants.summary: Custom data array contains 32-bit-per-channel red/green/blue/alpha color data. Values are floating-point in single precision.

constants.name: ARRAY_CUSTOM_MAX
constants.summary: Represents the size of the [enum ArrayCustomFormat] enum.

constants.name: ARRAY_FORMAT_VERTEX
constants.summary: Flag used to mark a vertex position array.

constants.name: ARRAY_FORMAT_NORMAL
constants.summary: Flag used to mark a normal array.

constants.name: ARRAY_FORMAT_TANGENT
constants.summary: Flag used to mark a tangent array.

constants.name: ARRAY_FORMAT_COLOR
constants.summary: Flag used to mark a vertex color array.

constants.name: ARRAY_FORMAT_TEX_UV
constants.summary: Flag used to mark a UV coordinates array.

constants.name: ARRAY_FORMAT_TEX_UV2
constants.summary: Flag used to mark a UV coordinates array for the second UV coordinates.

constants.name: ARRAY_FORMAT_CUSTOM0
constants.summary: Flag used to mark an array of custom per-vertex data for the first set of custom data.

constants.name: ARRAY_FORMAT_CUSTOM1
constants.summary: Flag used to mark an array of custom per-vertex data for the second set of custom data.

constants.name: ARRAY_FORMAT_CUSTOM2
constants.summary: Flag used to mark an array of custom per-vertex data for the third set of custom data.

constants.name: ARRAY_FORMAT_CUSTOM3
constants.summary: Flag used to mark an array of custom per-vertex data for the fourth set of custom data.

constants.name: ARRAY_FORMAT_BONES
constants.summary: Flag used to mark a bone information array.

constants.name: ARRAY_FORMAT_WEIGHTS
constants.summary: Flag used to mark a weights array.

constants.name: ARRAY_FORMAT_INDEX
constants.summary: Flag used to mark an index array.

constants.name: ARRAY_FORMAT_BLEND_SHAPE_MASK
constants.summary: Mask of mesh channels permitted in blend shapes.

constants.name: ARRAY_FORMAT_CUSTOM_BASE
constants.summary: Shift of first custom channel.

constants.name: ARRAY_FORMAT_CUSTOM_BITS
constants.summary: Number of format bits per custom channel. See [enum ArrayCustomFormat].

constants.name: ARRAY_FORMAT_CUSTOM0_SHIFT
constants.summary: Amount to shift [enum ArrayCustomFormat] for custom channel index 0.

constants.name: ARRAY_FORMAT_CUSTOM1_SHIFT
constants.summary: Amount to shift [enum ArrayCustomFormat] for custom channel index 1.

constants.name: ARRAY_FORMAT_CUSTOM2_SHIFT
constants.summary: Amount to shift [enum ArrayCustomFormat] for custom channel index 2.

constants.name: ARRAY_FORMAT_CUSTOM3_SHIFT
constants.summary: Amount to shift [enum ArrayCustomFormat] for custom channel index 3.

constants.name: ARRAY_FORMAT_CUSTOM_MASK
constants.summary: Mask of custom format bits per custom channel. Must be shifted by one of the SHIFT constants. See [enum ArrayCustomFormat].

constants.name: ARRAY_COMPRESS_FLAGS_BASE
constants.summary: Shift of first compress flag. Compress flags should be passed to [method ArrayMesh.add_surface_from_arrays] and [method SurfaceTool.commit].

constants.name: ARRAY_FLAG_USE_2D_VERTICES
constants.summary: Flag used to mark that the array contains 2D vertices.

constants.name: ARRAY_FLAG_USE_DYNAMIC_UPDATE
constants.summary: Flag used to mark that the mesh data will use [code]GL_DYNAMIC_DRAW[/code] on GLES. Unused on Vulkan.

constants.name: ARRAY_FLAG_USE_8_BONE_WEIGHTS
constants.summary: Flag used to mark that the array uses 8 bone weights instead of 4.

constants.name: ARRAY_FLAG_USES_EMPTY_VERTEX_ARRAY
constants.summary: Flag used to mark that the mesh does not have a vertex array and instead will infer vertex positions in the shader using indices and other information.

constants.name: ARRAY_FLAG_COMPRESS_ATTRIBUTES
constants.summary: Flag used to mark that a mesh is using compressed attributes (vertices, normals, tangents, UVs). When this form of compression is enabled, vertex positions will be packed into an RGBA16UNORM attribute and scaled in the vertex shader. The normal and tangent will be packed into an RG16UNORM representing an axis, and a 16-bit float stored in the A-channel of the vertex. UVs will use 16-bit normalized floats instead of full 32-bit signed floats. When using this compression mode you must use either vertices, normals, and tangents or only vertices. You cannot use normals without tangents. Importers will automatically enable this compression if they can.

constants.name: ARRAY_FLAG_FORMAT_VERSION_BASE
constants.summary: Flag used to mark the start of the bits used to store the mesh version.

constants.name: ARRAY_FLAG_FORMAT_VERSION_SHIFT
constants.summary: Flag used to shift a mesh format int to bring the version into the lowest digits.

constants.name: ARRAY_FLAG_FORMAT_VERSION_1
constants.summary: Flag used to record the format used by prior mesh versions before the introduction of a version.

constants.name: ARRAY_FLAG_FORMAT_VERSION_2
constants.summary: Flag used to record the second iteration of the mesh version flag. The primary difference between this and [constant ARRAY_FLAG_FORMAT_VERSION_1] is that this version supports [constant ARRAY_FLAG_COMPRESS_ATTRIBUTES] and in this version vertex positions are de-interleaved from normals and tangents.

constants.name: ARRAY_FLAG_FORMAT_CURRENT_VERSION
constants.summary: Flag used to record the current version that the engine expects. Currently this is the same as [constant ARRAY_FLAG_FORMAT_VERSION_2].

constants.name: ARRAY_FLAG_FORMAT_VERSION_MASK
constants.summary: Flag used to isolate the bits used for mesh version after using [constant ARRAY_FLAG_FORMAT_VERSION_SHIFT] to shift them into place.

constants.name: PRIMITIVE_POINTS
constants.summary: Primitive to draw consists of points.

constants.name: PRIMITIVE_LINES
constants.summary: Primitive to draw consists of lines.

constants.name: PRIMITIVE_LINE_STRIP
constants.summary: Primitive to draw consists of a line strip from start to end.

constants.name: PRIMITIVE_TRIANGLES
constants.summary: Primitive to draw consists of triangles.

constants.name: PRIMITIVE_TRIANGLE_STRIP
constants.summary: Primitive to draw consists of a triangle strip (the last 3 vertices are always combined to make a triangle).

constants.name: PRIMITIVE_MAX
constants.summary: Represents the size of the [enum PrimitiveType] enum.

constants.name: BLEND_SHAPE_MODE_NORMALIZED
constants.summary: Blend shapes are normalized.

constants.name: BLEND_SHAPE_MODE_RELATIVE
constants.summary: Blend shapes are relative to base weight.

constants.name: MULTIMESH_TRANSFORM_2D
constants.summary: Use [Transform2D] to store MultiMesh transform.

constants.name: MULTIMESH_TRANSFORM_3D
constants.summary: Use [Transform3D] to store MultiMesh transform.

constants.name: MULTIMESH_INTERP_QUALITY_FAST
constants.summary: MultiMesh physics interpolation favors speed over quality.

constants.name: MULTIMESH_INTERP_QUALITY_HIGH
constants.summary: MultiMesh physics interpolation favors quality over speed.

constants.name: LIGHT_PROJECTOR_FILTER_NEAREST
constants.summary: Nearest-neighbor filter for light projectors (use for pixel art light projectors). No mipmaps are used for rendering, which means light projectors at a distance will look sharp but grainy. This has roughly the same performance cost as using mipmaps.

constants.name: LIGHT_PROJECTOR_FILTER_LINEAR
constants.summary: Linear filter for light projectors (use for non-pixel art light projectors). No mipmaps are used for rendering, which means light projectors at a distance will look smooth but blurry. This has roughly the same performance cost as using mipmaps.

constants.name: LIGHT_PROJECTOR_FILTER_NEAREST_MIPMAPS
constants.summary: Nearest-neighbor filter for light projectors (use for pixel art light projectors). Isotropic mipmaps are used for rendering, which means light projectors at a distance will look smooth but blurry. This has roughly the same performance cost as not using mipmaps.

constants.name: LIGHT_PROJECTOR_FILTER_LINEAR_MIPMAPS
constants.summary: Linear filter for light projectors (use for non-pixel art light projectors). Isotropic mipmaps are used for rendering, which means light projectors at a distance will look smooth but blurry. This has roughly the same performance cost as not using mipmaps.

constants.name: LIGHT_PROJECTOR_FILTER_NEAREST_MIPMAPS_ANISOTROPIC
constants.summary: Nearest-neighbor filter for light projectors (use for pixel art light projectors). Anisotropic mipmaps are used for rendering, which means light projectors at a distance will look smooth and sharp when viewed from oblique angles. This looks better compared to isotropic mipmaps, but is slower. The level of anisotropic filtering is defined by [member ProjectSettings.rendering/textures/default_filters/anisotropic_filtering_level].

constants.name: LIGHT_PROJECTOR_FILTER_LINEAR_MIPMAPS_ANISOTROPIC
constants.summary: Linear filter for light projectors (use for non-pixel art light projectors). Anisotropic mipmaps are used for rendering, which means light projectors at a distance will look smooth and sharp when viewed from oblique angles. This looks better compared to isotropic mipmaps, but is slower. The level of anisotropic filtering is defined by [member ProjectSettings.rendering/textures/default_filters/anisotropic_filtering_level].

constants.name: LIGHT_DIRECTIONAL
constants.summary: Directional (sun/moon) light (see [DirectionalLight3D]).

constants.name: LIGHT_OMNI
constants.summary: Omni light (see [OmniLight3D]).

constants.name: LIGHT_SPOT
constants.summary: Spot light (see [SpotLight3D]).

constants.name: LIGHT_AREA
constants.summary: Area light (see [AreaLight3D]).

constants.name: LIGHT_PARAM_ENERGY
constants.summary: The light's energy multiplier.

constants.name: LIGHT_PARAM_INDIRECT_ENERGY
constants.summary: The light's indirect energy multiplier (final indirect energy is [constant LIGHT_PARAM_ENERGY] * [constant LIGHT_PARAM_INDIRECT_ENERGY]).

constants.name: LIGHT_PARAM_VOLUMETRIC_FOG_ENERGY
constants.summary: The light's volumetric fog energy multiplier (final volumetric fog energy is [constant LIGHT_PARAM_ENERGY] * [constant LIGHT_PARAM_VOLUMETRIC_FOG_ENERGY]).

constants.name: LIGHT_PARAM_SPECULAR
constants.summary: The light's influence on specularity.

constants.name: LIGHT_PARAM_RANGE
constants.summary: The light's range.

constants.name: LIGHT_PARAM_SIZE
constants.summary: The size of the light when using spot light or omni light. The angular size of the light when using directional light.

constants.name: LIGHT_PARAM_ATTENUATION
constants.summary: The light's attenuation.

constants.name: LIGHT_PARAM_SPOT_ANGLE
constants.summary: The spotlight's angle.

constants.name: LIGHT_PARAM_SPOT_ATTENUATION
constants.summary: The spotlight's attenuation.

constants.name: LIGHT_PARAM_SHADOW_MAX_DISTANCE
constants.summary: The maximum distance for shadow splits. Increasing this value will make directional shadows visible from further away, at the cost of lower overall shadow detail and performance (since more objects need to be included in the directional shadow rendering).

constants.name: LIGHT_PARAM_SHADOW_SPLIT_1_OFFSET
constants.summary: Proportion of shadow atlas occupied by the first split.

constants.name: LIGHT_PARAM_SHADOW_SPLIT_2_OFFSET
constants.summary: Proportion of shadow atlas occupied by the second split.

constants.name: LIGHT_PARAM_SHADOW_SPLIT_3_OFFSET
constants.summary: Proportion of shadow atlas occupied by the third split. The fourth split occupies the rest.

constants.name: LIGHT_PARAM_SHADOW_FADE_START
constants.summary: Proportion of shadow max distance where the shadow will start to fade out.

constants.name: LIGHT_PARAM_SHADOW_NORMAL_BIAS
constants.summary: Normal bias used to offset shadow lookup by object normal. Can be used to fix self-shadowing artifacts.

constants.name: LIGHT_PARAM_SHADOW_BIAS
constants.summary: Bias for the shadow lookup to fix self-shadowing artifacts.

constants.name: LIGHT_PARAM_SHADOW_PANCAKE_SIZE
constants.summary: Sets the size of the directional shadow pancake. The pancake offsets the start of the shadow's camera frustum to provide a higher effective depth resolution for the shadow. However, a high pancake size can cause artifacts in the shadows of large objects that are close to the edge of the frustum. Reducing the pancake size can help. Setting the size to [code]0[/code] turns off the pancaking effect.

constants.name: LIGHT_PARAM_SHADOW_OPACITY
constants.summary: The light's shadow opacity. Values lower than [code]1.0[/code] make the light appear through shadows. This can be used to fake global illumination at a low performance cost.

constants.name: LIGHT_PARAM_SHADOW_BLUR
constants.summary: Blurs the edges of the shadow. Can be used to hide pixel artifacts in low resolution shadow maps. A high value can make shadows appear grainy and can cause other unwanted artifacts. Try to keep as near default as possible.

constants.name: LIGHT_PARAM_INTENSITY
constants.summary: Constant representing the intensity of the light, measured in Lumens when dealing with a [SpotLight3D] or [OmniLight3D], or measured in Lux with a [DirectionalLight3D]. Only used when [member ProjectSettings.rendering/lights_and_shadows/use_physical_light_units] is [code]true[/code].

constants.name: LIGHT_PARAM_MAX
constants.summary: Represents the size of the [enum LightParam] enum.

constants.name: LIGHT_BAKE_DISABLED
constants.summary: Light is ignored when baking. This is the fastest mode, but the light will be taken into account when baking global illumination. This mode should generally be used for dynamic lights that change quickly, as the effect of global illumination is less noticeable on those lights.

constants.name: LIGHT_BAKE_STATIC
constants.summary: Light is taken into account in static baking ([VoxelGI], [LightmapGI], SDFGI ([member Environment.sdfgi_enabled])). The light can be moved around or modified, but its global illumination will not update in real-time. This is suitable for subtle changes (such as flickering torches), but generally not large changes such as toggling a light on and off.

constants.name: LIGHT_BAKE_DYNAMIC
constants.summary: Light is taken into account in dynamic baking ([VoxelGI] and SDFGI ([member Environment.sdfgi_enabled]) only). The light can be moved around or modified with global illumination updating in real-time. The light's global illumination appearance will be slightly different compared to [constant LIGHT_BAKE_STATIC]. This has a greater performance cost compared to [constant LIGHT_BAKE_STATIC]. When using SDFGI, the update speed of dynamic lights is affected by [member ProjectSettings.rendering/global_illumination/sdfgi/frames_to_update_lights].

constants.name: LIGHT_OMNI_SHADOW_DUAL_PARABOLOID
constants.summary: Use a dual paraboloid shadow map for omni lights.

constants.name: LIGHT_OMNI_SHADOW_CUBE
constants.summary: Use a cubemap shadow map for omni lights. Slower but better quality than dual paraboloid.

constants.name: LIGHT_DIRECTIONAL_SHADOW_ORTHOGONAL
constants.summary: Use orthogonal shadow projection for directional light.

constants.name: LIGHT_DIRECTIONAL_SHADOW_PARALLEL_2_SPLITS
constants.summary: Use 2 splits for shadow projection when using directional light.

constants.name: LIGHT_DIRECTIONAL_SHADOW_PARALLEL_4_SPLITS
constants.summary: Use 4 splits for shadow projection when using directional light.

constants.name: LIGHT_DIRECTIONAL_SKY_MODE_LIGHT_AND_SKY
constants.summary: Use DirectionalLight3D in both sky rendering and scene lighting.

constants.name: LIGHT_DIRECTIONAL_SKY_MODE_LIGHT_ONLY
constants.summary: Only use DirectionalLight3D in scene lighting.

constants.name: LIGHT_DIRECTIONAL_SKY_MODE_SKY_ONLY
constants.summary: Only use DirectionalLight3D in sky rendering.

constants.name: SHADOW_QUALITY_HARD
constants.summary: Lowest shadow filtering quality (fastest). Soft shadows are not available with this quality setting, which means the [member Light3D.shadow_blur] property is ignored if [member Light3D.light_size] and [member Light3D.light_angular_distance] is [code]0.0[/code]. [b]Note:[/b] The variable shadow blur performed by [member Light3D.light_size] and [member Light3D.light_angular_distance] is still effective when using hard shadow filtering. In this case, [member Light3D.shadow_blur] [i]is[/i] taken into account. However, the results will not be blurred, instead the blur amount is treated as a maximum radius for the penumbra.

constants.name: SHADOW_QUALITY_SOFT_VERY_LOW
constants.summary: Very low shadow filtering quality (faster). When using this quality setting, [member Light3D.shadow_blur] is automatically multiplied by 0.75× to avoid introducing too much noise. This division only applies to lights whose [member Light3D.light_size] or [member Light3D.light_angular_distance] is [code]0.0[/code]).

constants.name: SHADOW_QUALITY_SOFT_LOW
constants.summary: Low shadow filtering quality (fast).

constants.name: SHADOW_QUALITY_SOFT_MEDIUM
constants.summary: Medium low shadow filtering quality (average).

constants.name: SHADOW_QUALITY_SOFT_HIGH
constants.summary: High low shadow filtering quality (slow). When using this quality setting, [member Light3D.shadow_blur] is automatically multiplied by 1.5× to better make use of the high sample count. This increased blur also improves the stability of dynamic object shadows. This multiplier only applies to lights whose [member Light3D.light_size] or [member Light3D.light_angular_distance] is [code]0.0[/code]).

constants.name: SHADOW_QUALITY_SOFT_ULTRA
constants.summary: Highest low shadow filtering quality (slowest). When using this quality setting, [member Light3D.shadow_blur] is automatically multiplied by 2× to better make use of the high sample count. This increased blur also improves the stability of dynamic object shadows. This multiplier only applies to lights whose [member Light3D.light_size] or [member Light3D.light_angular_distance] is [code]0.0[/code]).

constants.name: SHADOW_QUALITY_MAX
constants.summary: Represents the size of the [enum ShadowQuality] enum.

constants.name: REFLECTION_PROBE_UPDATE_ONCE
constants.summary: Reflection probe will update reflections once and then stop.

constants.name: REFLECTION_PROBE_UPDATE_ALWAYS
constants.summary: Reflection probe will update each frame. This mode is necessary to capture moving objects.

constants.name: REFLECTION_PROBE_AMBIENT_DISABLED
constants.summary: Do not apply any ambient lighting inside the reflection probe's box defined by its size.

constants.name: REFLECTION_PROBE_AMBIENT_ENVIRONMENT
constants.summary: Apply automatically-sourced environment lighting inside the reflection probe's box defined by its size.

constants.name: REFLECTION_PROBE_AMBIENT_COLOR
constants.summary: Apply custom ambient lighting inside the reflection probe's box defined by its size. See [method reflection_probe_set_ambient_color] and [method reflection_probe_set_ambient_energy].

constants.name: DECAL_TEXTURE_ALBEDO
constants.summary: Albedo texture slot in a decal ([member Decal.texture_albedo]).

constants.name: DECAL_TEXTURE_NORMAL
constants.summary: Normal map texture slot in a decal ([member Decal.texture_normal]).

constants.name: DECAL_TEXTURE_ORM
constants.summary: Occlusion/Roughness/Metallic texture slot in a decal ([member Decal.texture_orm]).

constants.name: DECAL_TEXTURE_EMISSION
