constants.summary: Emission texture slot in a decal ([member Decal.texture_emission]).

constants.name: DECAL_TEXTURE_MAX
constants.summary: Represents the size of the [enum DecalTexture] enum.

constants.name: DECAL_FILTER_NEAREST
constants.summary: Nearest-neighbor filter for decals (use for pixel art decals). No mipmaps are used for rendering, which means decals at a distance will look sharp but grainy. This has roughly the same performance cost as using mipmaps.

constants.name: DECAL_FILTER_LINEAR
constants.summary: Linear filter for decals (use for non-pixel art decals). No mipmaps are used for rendering, which means decals at a distance will look smooth but blurry. This has roughly the same performance cost as using mipmaps.

constants.name: DECAL_FILTER_NEAREST_MIPMAPS
constants.summary: Nearest-neighbor filter for decals (use for pixel art decals). Isotropic mipmaps are used for rendering, which means decals at a distance will look smooth but blurry. This has roughly the same performance cost as not using mipmaps.

constants.name: DECAL_FILTER_LINEAR_MIPMAPS
constants.summary: Linear filter for decals (use for non-pixel art decals). Isotropic mipmaps are used for rendering, which means decals at a distance will look smooth but blurry. This has roughly the same performance cost as not using mipmaps.

constants.name: DECAL_FILTER_NEAREST_MIPMAPS_ANISOTROPIC
constants.summary: Nearest-neighbor filter for decals (use for pixel art decals). Anisotropic mipmaps are used for rendering, which means decals at a distance will look smooth and sharp when viewed from oblique angles. This looks better compared to isotropic mipmaps, but is slower. The level of anisotropic filtering is defined by [member ProjectSettings.rendering/textures/default_filters/anisotropic_filtering_level].

constants.name: DECAL_FILTER_LINEAR_MIPMAPS_ANISOTROPIC
constants.summary: Linear filter for decals (use for non-pixel art decals). Anisotropic mipmaps are used for rendering, which means decals at a distance will look smooth and sharp when viewed from oblique angles. This looks better compared to isotropic mipmaps, but is slower. The level of anisotropic filtering is defined by [member ProjectSettings.rendering/textures/default_filters/anisotropic_filtering_level].

constants.name: VOXEL_GI_QUALITY_LOW
constants.summary: Low [VoxelGI] rendering quality using 4 cones.

constants.name: VOXEL_GI_QUALITY_HIGH
constants.summary: High [VoxelGI] rendering quality using 6 cones.

constants.name: PARTICLES_MODE_2D
constants.summary: 2D particles.

constants.name: PARTICLES_MODE_3D
constants.summary: 3D particles.

constants.name: PARTICLES_TRANSFORM_ALIGN_DISABLED
constants.summary: Do not align particle transforms relative to the camera or velocity.

constants.name: PARTICLES_TRANSFORM_ALIGN_Z_BILLBOARD
constants.summary: Align each particle's Z axis to face the camera.

constants.name: PARTICLES_TRANSFORM_ALIGN_Y_TO_VELOCITY
constants.summary: Align each particle's Y axis to the velocity vector.

constants.name: PARTICLES_TRANSFORM_ALIGN_Z_BILLBOARD_Y_TO_VELOCITY
constants.summary: Align each particle's Z axis to face the camera and Y axis to the velocity vector.

constants.name: PARTICLES_TRANSFORM_ALIGN_LOCAL_BILLBOARD
constants.summary: Billboard each particles around a local axis.

constants.name: PARTICLES_ALIGN_CHANNEL_FILTER_DISABLED
constants.summary: Do not read from CUSTOM when performing billboarding.

constants.name: PARTICLES_ALIGN_CHANNEL_FILTER_X
constants.summary: Read from [code]CUSTOM.x[/code] when performing billboarding and use it as an angle, in radians.

constants.name: PARTICLES_ALIGN_CHANNEL_FILTER_Y
constants.summary: Read from [code]CUSTOM.y[/code] when performing billboarding and use it as an angle, in radians.

constants.name: PARTICLES_ALIGN_CHANNEL_FILTER_Z
constants.summary: Read from [code]CUSTOM.z[/code] when performing billboarding and use it as an angle, in radians.

constants.name: PARTICLES_ALIGN_CHANNEL_FILTER_W
constants.summary: Read from [code]CUSTOM.w[/code] when performing billboarding and use it as an angle, in radians.

constants.name: PARTICLES_ALIGN_AXIS_X
constants.summary: Use the X axis for local billboarding.

constants.name: PARTICLES_ALIGN_AXIS_Y
constants.summary: Use the Y axis for local billboarding.

constants.name: PARTICLES_EMIT_FLAG_POSITION
constants.summary: Particle starts at the specified position.

constants.name: PARTICLES_EMIT_FLAG_ROTATION_SCALE
constants.summary: Particle starts with specified rotation and scale.

constants.name: PARTICLES_EMIT_FLAG_VELOCITY
constants.summary: Particle starts with the specified velocity vector, which defines the emission direction and speed.

constants.name: PARTICLES_EMIT_FLAG_COLOR
constants.summary: Particle starts with specified color.

constants.name: PARTICLES_EMIT_FLAG_CUSTOM
constants.summary: Particle starts with specified [code]CUSTOM[/code] data.

constants.name: PARTICLES_DRAW_ORDER_INDEX
constants.summary: Draw particles in the order that they appear in the particles array.

constants.name: PARTICLES_DRAW_ORDER_LIFETIME
constants.summary: Sort particles based on their lifetime. In other words, the particle with the highest lifetime is drawn at the front.

constants.name: PARTICLES_DRAW_ORDER_REVERSE_LIFETIME
constants.summary: Sort particles based on the inverse of their lifetime. In other words, the particle with the lowest lifetime is drawn at the front.

constants.name: PARTICLES_DRAW_ORDER_VIEW_DEPTH
constants.summary: Sort particles based on their distance to the camera.

constants.name: PARTICLES_COLLISION_TYPE_SPHERE_ATTRACT
constants.summary: Sphere attractor type for [GPUParticles3D] (see [GPUParticlesAttractorSphere3D]).

constants.name: PARTICLES_COLLISION_TYPE_BOX_ATTRACT
constants.summary: Box attractor type for [GPUParticles3D] (see [GPUParticlesAttractorBox3D]).

constants.name: PARTICLES_COLLISION_TYPE_VECTOR_FIELD_ATTRACT
constants.summary: Vector field attractor type for [GPUParticles3D] (see [GPUParticlesAttractorVectorField3D]).

constants.name: PARTICLES_COLLISION_TYPE_SPHERE_COLLIDE
constants.summary: Sphere collision type for [GPUParticles3D] (see [GPUParticlesCollisionSphere3D]).

constants.name: PARTICLES_COLLISION_TYPE_BOX_COLLIDE
constants.summary: Box collision type for [GPUParticles3D] (see [GPUParticlesCollisionBox3D]).

constants.name: PARTICLES_COLLISION_TYPE_SDF_COLLIDE
constants.summary: Signed distance field collision type for [GPUParticles3D] (see [GPUParticlesCollisionSDF3D]).

constants.name: PARTICLES_COLLISION_TYPE_HEIGHTFIELD_COLLIDE
constants.summary: Heightfield collision type for [GPUParticles3D] (see [GPUParticlesCollisionHeightField3D]).

constants.name: PARTICLES_COLLISION_HEIGHTFIELD_RESOLUTION_256
constants.summary: 256×256 heightfield resolution for [GPUParticlesCollisionHeightField3D].

constants.name: PARTICLES_COLLISION_HEIGHTFIELD_RESOLUTION_512
constants.summary: 512×512 heightfield resolution for [GPUParticlesCollisionHeightField3D].

constants.name: PARTICLES_COLLISION_HEIGHTFIELD_RESOLUTION_1024
constants.summary: 1024×1024 heightfield resolution for [GPUParticlesCollisionHeightField3D].

constants.name: PARTICLES_COLLISION_HEIGHTFIELD_RESOLUTION_2048
constants.summary: 2048×2048 heightfield resolution for [GPUParticlesCollisionHeightField3D].

constants.name: PARTICLES_COLLISION_HEIGHTFIELD_RESOLUTION_4096
constants.summary: 4096×4096 heightfield resolution for [GPUParticlesCollisionHeightField3D].

constants.name: PARTICLES_COLLISION_HEIGHTFIELD_RESOLUTION_8192
constants.summary: 8192×8192 heightfield resolution for [GPUParticlesCollisionHeightField3D].

constants.name: PARTICLES_COLLISION_HEIGHTFIELD_RESOLUTION_MAX
constants.summary: Represents the size of the [enum ParticlesCollisionHeightfieldResolution] enum.

constants.name: FOG_VOLUME_SHAPE_ELLIPSOID
constants.summary: [FogVolume] will be shaped like an ellipsoid (stretched sphere).

constants.name: FOG_VOLUME_SHAPE_CONE
constants.summary: [FogVolume] will be shaped like a cone pointing upwards (in local coordinates). The cone's angle is set automatically to fill the size. The cone will be adjusted to fit within the size. Rotate the [FogVolume] node to reorient the cone. Non-uniform scaling via size is not supported (scale the [FogVolume] node instead).

constants.name: FOG_VOLUME_SHAPE_CYLINDER
constants.summary: [FogVolume] will be shaped like an upright cylinder (in local coordinates). Rotate the [FogVolume] node to reorient the cylinder. The cylinder will be adjusted to fit within the size. Non-uniform scaling via size is not supported (scale the [FogVolume] node instead).

constants.name: FOG_VOLUME_SHAPE_BOX
constants.summary: [FogVolume] will be shaped like a box.

constants.name: FOG_VOLUME_SHAPE_WORLD
constants.summary: [FogVolume] will have no shape, will cover the whole world and will not be culled.

constants.name: FOG_VOLUME_SHAPE_MAX
constants.summary: Represents the size of the [enum FogVolumeShape] enum.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_SCALING_3D_MODE_BILINEAR
constants.summary: Use bilinear scaling for the viewport's 3D buffer. The amount of scaling can be set using [member Viewport.scaling_3d_scale]. Values less than [code]1.0[/code] will result in undersampling while values greater than [code]1.0[/code] will result in supersampling. A value of [code]1.0[/code] disables scaling.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_SCALING_3D_MODE_FSR
constants.summary: Use AMD FidelityFX Super Resolution 1.0 upscaling for the viewport's 3D buffer. The amount of scaling can be set using [member Viewport.scaling_3d_scale]. Values less than [code]1.0[/code] will result in the viewport being upscaled using FSR. Values greater than [code]1.0[/code] are not supported and bilinear downsampling will be used instead. A value of [code]1.0[/code] disables scaling.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_SCALING_3D_MODE_FSR2
constants.summary: Use AMD FidelityFX Super Resolution 2.2 upscaling for the viewport's 3D buffer. The amount of scaling can be set using [member Viewport.scaling_3d_scale]. Values less than [code]1.0[/code] will result in the viewport being upscaled using FSR2. Values greater than [code]1.0[/code] are not supported and bilinear downsampling will be used instead. A value of [code]1.0[/code] will use FSR2 at native resolution as a TAA solution.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_SCALING_3D_MODE_METALFX_SPATIAL
constants.summary: Use MetalFX spatial upscaling for the viewport's 3D buffer. The amount of scaling can be set using [member Viewport.scaling_3d_scale]. Values less than [code]1.0[/code] will result in the viewport being upscaled using MetalFX. Values greater than [code]1.0[/code] are not supported and bilinear downsampling will be used instead. A value of [code]1.0[/code] disables scaling. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when the Metal rendering driver is in use, which limits this scaling mode to macOS and iOS.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_SCALING_3D_MODE_METALFX_TEMPORAL
constants.summary: Use MetalFX temporal upscaling for the viewport's 3D buffer. The amount of scaling can be set using [member Viewport.scaling_3d_scale]. Values less than [code]1.0[/code] will result in the viewport being upscaled using MetalFX. Values greater than [code]1.0[/code] are not supported and bilinear downsampling will be used instead. A value of [code]1.0[/code] will use MetalFX at native resolution as a TAA solution. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when the Metal rendering driver is in use, which limits this scaling mode to macOS and iOS.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_SCALING_3D_MODE_NEAREST
constants.summary: Use nearest-neighbor filtering for the viewport's 3D buffer. This looks crisper than [constant VIEWPORT_SCALING_3D_MODE_BILINEAR] and has no additional rendering cost. The amount of scaling can be set using [member Viewport.scaling_3d_scale]. Values greater than [code]1.0[/code] are not supported and bilinear downsampling will be used instead. A value of [code]1.0[/code] disables scaling. [b]Note:[/b] When using the [b]Nearest[/b] scaling mode, to avoid uneven pixel scaling, it's highly recommended to use a value equal to an integer divisor with a dividend of [code]1[/code]. For example, it's best to use a scale of [code]0.5[/code] (1/2), [code]0.3333[/code] (1/3), [code]0.25[/code] (1/4), [code]0.2[/code] (1/5), and so on.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_SCALING_3D_MODE_MAX
constants.summary: Represents the size of the [enum ViewportScaling3DMode] enum.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_UPDATE_DISABLED
constants.summary: Do not update the viewport's render target.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_UPDATE_ONCE
constants.summary: Update the viewport's render target once, then switch to [constant VIEWPORT_UPDATE_DISABLED].

constants.name: VIEWPORT_UPDATE_WHEN_VISIBLE
constants.summary: Update the viewport's render target only when it is visible. This is the default value.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_UPDATE_WHEN_PARENT_VISIBLE
constants.summary: Update the viewport's render target only when its parent is visible.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_UPDATE_ALWAYS
constants.summary: Always update the viewport's render target.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_CLEAR_ALWAYS
constants.summary: Always clear the viewport's render target before drawing.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_CLEAR_NEVER
constants.summary: Never clear the viewport's render target.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_CLEAR_ONLY_NEXT_FRAME
constants.summary: Clear the viewport's render target on the next frame, then switch to [constant VIEWPORT_CLEAR_NEVER].

constants.name: VIEWPORT_ENVIRONMENT_DISABLED
constants.summary: Disable rendering of 3D environment over 2D canvas.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_ENVIRONMENT_ENABLED
constants.summary: Enable rendering of 3D environment over 2D canvas.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_ENVIRONMENT_INHERIT
constants.summary: Inherit enable/disable value from parent. If the topmost parent is also set to [constant VIEWPORT_ENVIRONMENT_INHERIT], then this has the same behavior as [constant VIEWPORT_ENVIRONMENT_ENABLED].

constants.name: VIEWPORT_ENVIRONMENT_MAX
constants.summary: Represents the size of the [enum ViewportEnvironmentMode] enum.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_SDF_OVERSIZE_100_PERCENT
constants.summary: Do not oversize the 2D signed distance field. Occluders may disappear when touching the viewport's edges, and [GPUParticles3D] collision may stop working earlier than intended. This has the lowest GPU requirements.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_SDF_OVERSIZE_120_PERCENT
constants.summary: 2D signed distance field covers 20% of the viewport's size outside the viewport on each side (top, right, bottom, left).

constants.name: VIEWPORT_SDF_OVERSIZE_150_PERCENT
constants.summary: 2D signed distance field covers 50% of the viewport's size outside the viewport on each side (top, right, bottom, left).

constants.name: VIEWPORT_SDF_OVERSIZE_200_PERCENT
constants.summary: 2D signed distance field covers 100% of the viewport's size outside the viewport on each side (top, right, bottom, left). This has the highest GPU requirements.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_SDF_OVERSIZE_MAX
constants.summary: Represents the size of the [enum ViewportSDFOversize] enum.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_SDF_SCALE_100_PERCENT
constants.summary: Full resolution 2D signed distance field scale. This has the highest GPU requirements.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_SDF_SCALE_50_PERCENT
constants.summary: Half resolution 2D signed distance field scale on each axis (25% of the viewport pixel count).

constants.name: VIEWPORT_SDF_SCALE_25_PERCENT
constants.summary: Quarter resolution 2D signed distance field scale on each axis (6.25% of the viewport pixel count). This has the lowest GPU requirements.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_SDF_SCALE_MAX
constants.summary: Represents the size of the [enum ViewportSDFScale] enum.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_MSAA_DISABLED
constants.summary: Multisample antialiasing for 3D is disabled. This is the default value, and also the fastest setting.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_MSAA_2X
constants.summary: Multisample antialiasing uses 2 samples per pixel for 3D. This has a moderate impact on performance.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_MSAA_4X
constants.summary: Multisample antialiasing uses 4 samples per pixel for 3D. This has a high impact on performance.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_MSAA_8X
constants.summary: Multisample antialiasing uses 8 samples per pixel for 3D. This has a very high impact on performance. Likely unsupported on low-end and older hardware.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_MSAA_MAX
constants.summary: Represents the size of the [enum ViewportMSAA] enum.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_ANISOTROPY_DISABLED
constants.summary: Anisotropic filtering is disabled.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_ANISOTROPY_2X
constants.summary: Use 2× anisotropic filtering.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_ANISOTROPY_4X
constants.summary: Use 4× anisotropic filtering. This is the default value.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_ANISOTROPY_8X
constants.summary: Use 8× anisotropic filtering.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_ANISOTROPY_16X
constants.summary: Use 16× anisotropic filtering.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_ANISOTROPY_MAX
constants.summary: Represents the size of the [enum ViewportAnisotropicFiltering] enum.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_SCREEN_SPACE_AA_DISABLED
constants.summary: Do not perform any antialiasing in the full screen post-process.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_SCREEN_SPACE_AA_FXAA
constants.summary: Use fast approximate antialiasing. FXAA is a popular screen-space antialiasing method, which is fast but will make the image look blurry, especially at lower resolutions. It can still work relatively well at large resolutions such as 1440p and 4K.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_SCREEN_SPACE_AA_SMAA
constants.summary: Use subpixel morphological antialiasing. SMAA may produce clearer results than FXAA, but at a slightly higher performance cost.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_SCREEN_SPACE_AA_MAX
constants.summary: Represents the size of the [enum ViewportScreenSpaceAA] enum.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_OCCLUSION_BUILD_QUALITY_LOW
constants.summary: Low occlusion culling BVH build quality (as defined by Embree). Results in the lowest CPU usage, but least effective culling.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_OCCLUSION_BUILD_QUALITY_MEDIUM
constants.summary: Medium occlusion culling BVH build quality (as defined by Embree).

constants.name: VIEWPORT_OCCLUSION_BUILD_QUALITY_HIGH
constants.summary: High occlusion culling BVH build quality (as defined by Embree). Results in the highest CPU usage, but most effective culling.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_RENDER_INFO_OBJECTS_IN_FRAME
constants.summary: Number of objects drawn in a single frame.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_RENDER_INFO_PRIMITIVES_IN_FRAME
constants.summary: Number of points, lines, or triangles drawn in a single frame.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_RENDER_INFO_DRAW_CALLS_IN_FRAME
constants.summary: Number of draw calls during this frame.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_RENDER_INFO_MAX
constants.summary: Represents the size of the [enum ViewportRenderInfo] enum.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_RENDER_INFO_TYPE_VISIBLE
constants.summary: Visible render pass (excluding shadows).

constants.name: VIEWPORT_RENDER_INFO_TYPE_SHADOW
constants.summary: Shadow render pass. Objects will be rendered several times depending on the number of amounts of lights with shadows and the number of directional shadow splits.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_RENDER_INFO_TYPE_CANVAS
constants.summary: Canvas item rendering. This includes all 2D rendering.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_RENDER_INFO_TYPE_MAX
constants.summary: Represents the size of the [enum ViewportRenderInfoType] enum.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_DEBUG_DRAW_DISABLED
constants.summary: Debug draw is disabled. Default setting.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_DEBUG_DRAW_UNSHADED
constants.summary: Objects are displayed without light information.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_DEBUG_DRAW_LIGHTING
constants.summary: Objects are displayed with only light information. [b]Note:[/b] When using this debug draw mode, custom shaders are ignored since all materials in the scene temporarily use a debug material. This means the result from custom shader functions (such as vertex displacement) won't be visible anymore when using this debug draw mode.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_DEBUG_DRAW_OVERDRAW
constants.summary: Objects are displayed semi-transparent with additive blending so you can see where they are drawing over top of one another. A higher overdraw (represented by brighter colors) means you are wasting performance on drawing pixels that are being hidden behind others. [b]Note:[/b] When using this debug draw mode, custom shaders are ignored since all materials in the scene temporarily use a debug material. This means the result from custom shader functions (such as vertex displacement) won't be visible anymore when using this debug draw mode.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_DEBUG_DRAW_WIREFRAME
constants.summary: Debug draw draws objects in wireframe. [b]Note:[/b] [method set_debug_generate_wireframes] must be called before loading any meshes for wireframes to be visible when using the Compatibility renderer. [b]Note:[/b] In the Compatibility renderer, backfaces are always visible when using wireframe rendering. In the Forward+ and Mobile renderers, wireframes follow the material's backface culling properties instead.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_DEBUG_DRAW_NORMAL_BUFFER
constants.summary: Normal buffer is drawn instead of regular scene so you can see the per-pixel normals that will be used by post-processing effects.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_DEBUG_DRAW_VOXEL_GI_ALBEDO
constants.summary: Objects are displayed with only the albedo value from [VoxelGI]s. Requires at least one visible [VoxelGI] node that has been baked to have a visible effect. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when using the Forward+ rendering method.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_DEBUG_DRAW_VOXEL_GI_LIGHTING
constants.summary: Objects are displayed with only the lighting value from [VoxelGI]s. Requires at least one visible [VoxelGI] node that has been baked to have a visible effect. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when using the Forward+ rendering method.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_DEBUG_DRAW_VOXEL_GI_EMISSION
constants.summary: Objects are displayed with only the emission color from [VoxelGI]s. Requires at least one visible [VoxelGI] node that has been baked to have a visible effect. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when using the Forward+ rendering method.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_DEBUG_DRAW_SHADOW_ATLAS
constants.summary: Draws the shadow atlas that stores shadows from [OmniLight3D]s and [SpotLight3D]s in the upper left quadrant of the [Viewport].

constants.name: VIEWPORT_DEBUG_DRAW_DIRECTIONAL_SHADOW_ATLAS
constants.summary: Draws the shadow atlas that stores shadows from [DirectionalLight3D]s in the upper left quadrant of the [Viewport]. The slice of the camera frustum related to the shadow map cascade is superimposed to visualize coverage. The color of each slice matches the colors used for [constant VIEWPORT_DEBUG_DRAW_PSSM_SPLITS]. When shadow cascades are blended the overlap is taken into account when drawing the frustum slices. The last cascade shows all frustum slices to illustrate the coverage of all slices.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_DEBUG_DRAW_SCENE_LUMINANCE
constants.summary: Draws the estimated scene luminance. This is a 1×1 texture that is generated when autoexposure is enabled to control the scene's exposure. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when using the Forward+ or Mobile rendering methods.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_DEBUG_DRAW_SSAO
constants.summary: Draws the screen space ambient occlusion texture instead of the scene so that you can clearly see how it is affecting objects. In order for this display mode to work, you must have [member Environment.ssao_enabled] set in your [WorldEnvironment]. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when using the Forward+ rendering method.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_DEBUG_DRAW_SSIL
constants.summary: Draws the screen space indirect lighting texture instead of the scene so that you can clearly see how it is affecting objects. In order for this display mode to work, you must have [member Environment.ssil_enabled] set in your [WorldEnvironment]. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when using the Forward+ rendering method.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_DEBUG_DRAW_PSSM_SPLITS
constants.summary: Colors each PSSM split for the [DirectionalLight3D]s in the scene a different color so you can see where the splits are. In order (from closest to furthest from the camera), they are colored red, green, blue, and yellow. [b]Note:[/b] When using this debug draw mode, custom shaders are ignored since all materials in the scene temporarily use a debug material. This means the result from custom shader functions (such as vertex displacement) won't be visible anymore when using this debug draw mode. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when using the Forward+ or Mobile rendering methods.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_DEBUG_DRAW_DECAL_ATLAS
constants.summary: Draws the decal atlas that stores decal textures from [Decal]s. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when using the Forward+ or Mobile rendering methods.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_DEBUG_DRAW_SDFGI
constants.summary: Draws SDFGI cascade data. This is the data structure that is used to bounce lighting against and create reflections. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when using the Forward+ rendering method.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_DEBUG_DRAW_SDFGI_PROBES
constants.summary: Draws SDFGI probe data. This is the data structure that is used to give indirect lighting dynamic objects moving within the scene. When in the editor, left-clicking a probe will display additional bright dots that show its occlusion information. A white dot means the light is not occluded at all at the dot's position, while a red dot means the light is fully occluded. Intermediate values are possible. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when using the Forward+ rendering method.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_DEBUG_DRAW_GI_BUFFER
constants.summary: Draws the global illumination buffer from [VoxelGI] or SDFGI. Requires [VoxelGI] (at least one visible baked VoxelGI node) or SDFGI ([member Environment.sdfgi_enabled]) to be enabled to have a visible effect. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when using the Forward+ rendering method.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_DEBUG_DRAW_DISABLE_LOD
constants.summary: Disable mesh LOD. All meshes are drawn with full detail, which can be used to compare performance.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_DEBUG_DRAW_CLUSTER_OMNI_LIGHTS
constants.summary: Draws the [OmniLight3D] cluster. Clustering determines where lights are positioned in screen-space, which allows the engine to only process these portions of the screen for lighting. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when using the Forward+ rendering method.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_DEBUG_DRAW_CLUSTER_SPOT_LIGHTS
constants.summary: Draws the [SpotLight3D] cluster. Clustering determines where lights are positioned in screen-space, which allows the engine to only process these portions of the screen for lighting. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when using the Forward+ rendering method.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_DEBUG_DRAW_CLUSTER_DECALS
constants.summary: Draws the [Decal] cluster. Clustering determines where decals are positioned in screen-space, which allows the engine to only process these portions of the screen for decals. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when using the Forward+ rendering method.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_DEBUG_DRAW_CLUSTER_REFLECTION_PROBES
constants.summary: Draws the [ReflectionProbe] cluster. Clustering determines where reflection probes are positioned in screen-space, which allows the engine to only process these portions of the screen for reflection probes. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when using the Forward+ rendering method.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_DEBUG_DRAW_OCCLUDERS
constants.summary: Draws the occlusion culling buffer. This low-resolution occlusion culling buffer is rasterized on the CPU and is used to check whether instances are occluded by other objects. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when using the Forward+ or Mobile rendering methods.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_DEBUG_DRAW_MOTION_VECTORS
constants.summary: Draws the motion vectors buffer. This is used by temporal antialiasing to correct for motion that occurs during gameplay. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when using the Forward+ rendering method.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_DEBUG_DRAW_INTERNAL_BUFFER
constants.summary: Internal buffer is drawn instead of regular scene so you can see the per-pixel output that will be used by post-processing effects. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when using the Forward+ or Mobile rendering methods.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_VRS_DISABLED
constants.summary: Variable rate shading is disabled.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_VRS_TEXTURE
constants.summary: Variable rate shading uses a texture. Note, for stereoscopic use a texture atlas with a texture for each view.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_VRS_XR
constants.summary: Variable rate shading texture is supplied by the primary [XRInterface]. Note that this may override the update mode.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_VRS_MAX
constants.summary: Represents the size of the [enum ViewportVRSMode] enum.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_VRS_UPDATE_DISABLED
constants.summary: The input texture for variable rate shading will not be processed.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_VRS_UPDATE_ONCE
constants.summary: The input texture for variable rate shading will be processed once.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_VRS_UPDATE_ALWAYS
constants.summary: The input texture for variable rate shading will be processed each frame.

constants.name: VIEWPORT_VRS_UPDATE_MAX
constants.summary: Represents the size of the [enum ViewportVRSUpdateMode] enum.

constants.name: SKY_MODE_AUTOMATIC
constants.summary: Automatically selects the appropriate process mode based on your sky shader. If your shader uses [code]TIME[/code] or [code]POSITION[/code], this will use [constant SKY_MODE_REALTIME]. If your shader uses any of the [code]LIGHT_*[/code] variables or any custom uniforms, this uses [constant SKY_MODE_INCREMENTAL]. Otherwise, this defaults to [constant SKY_MODE_QUALITY].

constants.name: SKY_MODE_QUALITY
constants.summary: Uses high quality importance sampling to process the radiance map. In general, this results in much higher quality than [constant SKY_MODE_REALTIME] but takes much longer to generate. This should not be used if you plan on changing the sky at runtime. If you are finding that the reflection is not blurry enough and is showing sparkles or fireflies, try increasing [member ProjectSettings.rendering/reflections/sky_reflections/ggx_samples].

constants.name: SKY_MODE_INCREMENTAL
constants.summary: Uses the same high quality importance sampling to process the radiance map as [constant SKY_MODE_QUALITY], but updates over several frames. The number of frames is determined by [member ProjectSettings.rendering/reflections/sky_reflections/roughness_layers]. Use this when you need highest quality radiance maps, but have a sky that updates slowly.

constants.name: SKY_MODE_REALTIME
constants.summary: Uses the fast filtering algorithm to process the radiance map. In general this results in lower quality, but substantially faster run times. If you need better quality, but still need to update the sky every frame, consider turning on [member ProjectSettings.rendering/reflections/sky_reflections/fast_filter_high_quality]. [b]Note:[/b] The fast filtering algorithm is limited to 256×256 cubemaps, so [method sky_set_radiance_size] must be set to [code]256[/code]. Otherwise, a warning is printed and the overridden radiance size is ignored.

constants.name: COMPOSITOR_EFFECT_FLAG_ACCESS_RESOLVED_COLOR
constants.summary: The rendering effect requires the color buffer to be resolved if MSAA is enabled.

constants.name: COMPOSITOR_EFFECT_FLAG_ACCESS_RESOLVED_DEPTH
constants.summary: The rendering effect requires the depth buffer to be resolved if MSAA is enabled.

constants.name: COMPOSITOR_EFFECT_FLAG_NEEDS_MOTION_VECTORS
constants.summary: The rendering effect requires motion vectors to be produced.

constants.name: COMPOSITOR_EFFECT_FLAG_NEEDS_ROUGHNESS
constants.summary: The rendering effect requires normals and roughness g-buffer to be produced (Forward+ only).

constants.name: COMPOSITOR_EFFECT_FLAG_NEEDS_SEPARATE_SPECULAR
constants.summary: The rendering effect requires specular data to be separated out (Forward+ only).

constants.name: COMPOSITOR_EFFECT_CALLBACK_TYPE_PRE_OPAQUE
constants.summary: The callback is called before our opaque rendering pass, but after depth prepass (if applicable).

constants.name: COMPOSITOR_EFFECT_CALLBACK_TYPE_POST_OPAQUE
constants.summary: The callback is called after our opaque rendering pass, but before our sky is rendered.

constants.name: COMPOSITOR_EFFECT_CALLBACK_TYPE_POST_SKY
constants.summary: The callback is called after our sky is rendered, but before our back buffers are created (and if enabled, before subsurface scattering and/or screen space reflections).

constants.name: COMPOSITOR_EFFECT_CALLBACK_TYPE_PRE_TRANSPARENT
constants.summary: The callback is called before our transparent rendering pass, but after our sky is rendered and we've created our back buffers.

constants.name: COMPOSITOR_EFFECT_CALLBACK_TYPE_POST_TRANSPARENT
constants.summary: The callback is called after our transparent rendering pass, but before any built-in post-processing effects and output to our render target.

constants.name: ENV_BG_CLEAR_COLOR
constants.summary: Use the clear color as background.

constants.name: ENV_BG_COLOR
constants.summary: Use a specified color as the background.

constants.name: ENV_BG_SKY
constants.summary: Use a sky resource for the background.

constants.name: ENV_BG_CANVAS
constants.summary: Use a specified canvas layer as the background. This can be useful for instantiating a 2D scene in a 3D world.

constants.name: ENV_BG_KEEP
constants.summary: Do not clear the background, use whatever was rendered last frame as the background.

constants.name: ENV_BG_CAMERA_FEED
constants.summary: Displays a camera feed in the background.

constants.name: ENV_BG_MAX
constants.summary: Represents the size of the [enum EnvironmentBG] enum.

constants.name: ENV_AMBIENT_SOURCE_BG
constants.summary: Gather ambient light from whichever source is specified as the background.

constants.name: ENV_AMBIENT_SOURCE_DISABLED
constants.summary: Disable ambient light.

constants.name: ENV_AMBIENT_SOURCE_COLOR
constants.summary: Specify a specific [Color] for ambient light.

constants.name: ENV_AMBIENT_SOURCE_SKY
constants.summary: Gather ambient light from the [Sky] regardless of what the background is.

constants.name: ENV_REFLECTION_SOURCE_BG
constants.summary: Use the background for reflections.

constants.name: ENV_REFLECTION_SOURCE_DISABLED
constants.summary: Disable reflections.

constants.name: ENV_REFLECTION_SOURCE_SKY
constants.summary: Use the [Sky] for reflections regardless of what the background is.

constants.name: ENV_GLOW_BLEND_MODE_ADDITIVE
constants.summary: Adds the glow effect to the scene.

constants.name: ENV_GLOW_BLEND_MODE_SCREEN
constants.summary: Adds the glow effect to the scene after modifying the glow influence based on the scene value; dark values will be highly influenced by glow and bright values will not be influenced by glow. This approach avoids bright values becoming overly bright from the glow effect. [member Environment.tonemap_white] is used to determine the maximum scene value where the glow should have no influence. When [member Environment.tonemap_mode] is set to [constant Environment.TONE_MAPPER_LINEAR] and [member Viewport.use_hdr_2d] is [code]true[/code], the parent window's [method Window.get_output_max_linear_value] will be used as the maximum scene value.

constants.name: ENV_GLOW_BLEND_MODE_SOFTLIGHT
constants.summary: Adds the glow effect to the tonemapped image after modifying the glow influence based on the image value; dark values and bright values will not be influenced by glow and mid-range values will be highly influenced by glow. This approach avoids bright values becoming overly bright from the glow effect. The glow will have the largest influence on image values of [code]0.25[/code] and will have no influence when applied to image values greater than [code]1.0[/code]. [b]Note:[/b] This blend mode does not support HDR output because expects a maximum output value of [code]1.0[/code]. It is recommended to use a different blend mode when rendering to an HDR screen.

constants.name: ENV_GLOW_BLEND_MODE_REPLACE
constants.summary: Replaces all pixels' color by the glow effect. This can be used to simulate a full-screen blur effect by tweaking the glow parameters to match the original image's brightness or to preview glow configuration in the editor.

constants.name: ENV_GLOW_BLEND_MODE_MIX
constants.summary: Mixes the glow image with the scene image. Best used with [member Environment.glow_bloom] to avoid darkening the scene.

constants.name: ENV_FOG_MODE_EXPONENTIAL
constants.summary: Use a physically-based fog model defined primarily by fog density.

constants.name: ENV_FOG_MODE_DEPTH
constants.summary: Use a simple fog model defined by start and end positions and a custom curve. While not physically accurate, this model can be useful when you need more artistic control.

constants.name: ENV_TONE_MAPPER_LINEAR
constants.summary: Does not modify color data, resulting in a linear tonemapping curve which unnaturally clips bright values, causing bright lighting to look blown out. The simplest and fastest tonemapper.

constants.name: ENV_TONE_MAPPER_REINHARD
constants.summary: A simple tonemapping curve that rolls off bright values to prevent clipping. This results in an image that can appear dull and low contrast. Slower than [constant ENV_TONE_MAPPER_LINEAR]. [b]Note:[/b] When [member Environment.tonemap_white] is left at the default value of [code]1.0[/code], [constant ENV_TONE_MAPPER_REINHARD] produces an identical image to [constant ENV_TONE_MAPPER_LINEAR].

constants.name: ENV_TONE_MAPPER_FILMIC
constants.summary: Uses a film-like tonemapping curve to prevent clipping of bright values and provide better contrast than [constant ENV_TONE_MAPPER_REINHARD]. Slightly slower than [constant ENV_TONE_MAPPER_REINHARD]. [b]Note:[/b] This tonemapper does not support HDR output because it produces output in the SDR range. It is recommended to use a different tonemapper when rendering to an HDR screen.

constants.name: ENV_TONE_MAPPER_ACES
constants.summary: Uses a high-contrast film-like tonemapping curve and desaturates bright values for a more realistic appearance. Slightly slower than [constant ENV_TONE_MAPPER_FILMIC]. [b]Note:[/b] This tonemapping operator is called "ACES Fitted" in Godot 3.x. [b]Note:[/b] This tonemapper does not support HDR output because it produces output in the SDR range. It is recommended to use a different tonemapper when rendering to an HDR screen.

constants.name: ENV_TONE_MAPPER_AGX
constants.summary: Uses an adjustable film-like tonemapping curve and desaturates bright values for a more realistic appearance. Better than other tonemappers at maintaining the hue of colors as they become brighter. The slowest tonemapping option.

constants.name: ENV_SSR_ROUGHNESS_QUALITY_DISABLED
constants.summary: Lowest quality of roughness filter for screen-space reflections. Rough materials will not have blurrier screen-space reflections compared to smooth (non-rough) materials. This is the fastest option.

constants.name: ENV_SSR_ROUGHNESS_QUALITY_LOW
constants.summary: Low quality of roughness filter for screen-space reflections.

constants.name: ENV_SSR_ROUGHNESS_QUALITY_MEDIUM
constants.summary: Medium quality of roughness filter for screen-space reflections.

constants.name: ENV_SSR_ROUGHNESS_QUALITY_HIGH
constants.summary: High quality of roughness filter for screen-space reflections. This is the slowest option.

constants.name: ENV_SSAO_QUALITY_VERY_LOW
constants.summary: Lowest quality of screen-space ambient occlusion.

constants.name: ENV_SSAO_QUALITY_LOW
constants.summary: Low quality screen-space ambient occlusion.

constants.name: ENV_SSAO_QUALITY_MEDIUM
constants.summary: Medium quality screen-space ambient occlusion.

constants.name: ENV_SSAO_QUALITY_HIGH
constants.summary: High quality screen-space ambient occlusion.

constants.name: ENV_SSAO_QUALITY_ULTRA
constants.summary: Highest quality screen-space ambient occlusion. Uses the adaptive target setting which can be dynamically adjusted to smoothly balance performance and visual quality.

constants.name: ENV_SSIL_QUALITY_VERY_LOW
constants.summary: Lowest quality of screen-space indirect lighting.

constants.name: ENV_SSIL_QUALITY_LOW
constants.summary: Low quality screen-space indirect lighting.

constants.name: ENV_SSIL_QUALITY_MEDIUM
constants.summary: High quality screen-space indirect lighting.

constants.name: ENV_SSIL_QUALITY_HIGH
constants.summary: High quality screen-space indirect lighting.

constants.name: ENV_SSIL_QUALITY_ULTRA
constants.summary: Highest quality screen-space indirect lighting. Uses the adaptive target setting which can be dynamically adjusted to smoothly balance performance and visual quality.

constants.name: ENV_SDFGI_Y_SCALE_50_PERCENT
constants.summary: Use 50% scale for SDFGI on the Y (vertical) axis. SDFGI cells will be twice as short as they are wide. This allows providing increased GI detail and reduced light leaking with thin floors and ceilings. This is usually the best choice for scenes that don't feature much verticality.

constants.name: ENV_SDFGI_Y_SCALE_75_PERCENT
constants.summary: Use 75% scale for SDFGI on the Y (vertical) axis. This is a balance between the 50% and 100% SDFGI Y scales.

constants.name: ENV_SDFGI_Y_SCALE_100_PERCENT
constants.summary: Use 100% scale for SDFGI on the Y (vertical) axis. SDFGI cells will be as tall as they are wide. This is usually the best choice for highly vertical scenes. The downside is that light leaking may become more noticeable with thin floors and ceilings.

constants.name: ENV_SDFGI_RAY_COUNT_4
constants.summary: Throw 4 rays per frame when converging SDFGI. This has the lowest GPU requirements, but creates the most noisy result.

constants.name: ENV_SDFGI_RAY_COUNT_8
constants.summary: Throw 8 rays per frame when converging SDFGI.

constants.name: ENV_SDFGI_RAY_COUNT_16
constants.summary: Throw 16 rays per frame when converging SDFGI.

constants.name: ENV_SDFGI_RAY_COUNT_32
constants.summary: Throw 32 rays per frame when converging SDFGI.

constants.name: ENV_SDFGI_RAY_COUNT_64
constants.summary: Throw 64 rays per frame when converging SDFGI.

constants.name: ENV_SDFGI_RAY_COUNT_96
constants.summary: Throw 96 rays per frame when converging SDFGI. This has high GPU requirements.

constants.name: ENV_SDFGI_RAY_COUNT_128
constants.summary: Throw 128 rays per frame when converging SDFGI. This has very high GPU requirements, but creates the least noisy result.

constants.name: ENV_SDFGI_RAY_COUNT_MAX
constants.summary: Represents the size of the [enum EnvironmentSDFGIRayCount] enum.

constants.name: ENV_SDFGI_CONVERGE_IN_5_FRAMES
constants.summary: Converge SDFGI over 5 frames. This is the most responsive, but creates the most noisy result with a given ray count.

constants.name: ENV_SDFGI_CONVERGE_IN_10_FRAMES
constants.summary: Configure SDFGI to fully converge over 10 frames.

constants.name: ENV_SDFGI_CONVERGE_IN_15_FRAMES
constants.summary: Configure SDFGI to fully converge over 15 frames.

constants.name: ENV_SDFGI_CONVERGE_IN_20_FRAMES
constants.summary: Configure SDFGI to fully converge over 20 frames.

constants.name: ENV_SDFGI_CONVERGE_IN_25_FRAMES
constants.summary: Configure SDFGI to fully converge over 25 frames.

constants.name: ENV_SDFGI_CONVERGE_IN_30_FRAMES
constants.summary: Configure SDFGI to fully converge over 30 frames. This is the least responsive, but creates the least noisy result with a given ray count.

constants.name: ENV_SDFGI_CONVERGE_MAX
constants.summary: Represents the size of the [enum EnvironmentSDFGIFramesToConverge] enum.

constants.name: ENV_SDFGI_UPDATE_LIGHT_IN_1_FRAME
constants.summary: Update indirect light from dynamic lights in SDFGI over 1 frame. This is the most responsive, but has the highest GPU requirements.

constants.name: ENV_SDFGI_UPDATE_LIGHT_IN_2_FRAMES
constants.summary: Update indirect light from dynamic lights in SDFGI over 2 frames.

constants.name: ENV_SDFGI_UPDATE_LIGHT_IN_4_FRAMES
constants.summary: Update indirect light from dynamic lights in SDFGI over 4 frames.

constants.name: ENV_SDFGI_UPDATE_LIGHT_IN_8_FRAMES
constants.summary: Update indirect light from dynamic lights in SDFGI over 8 frames.

constants.name: ENV_SDFGI_UPDATE_LIGHT_IN_16_FRAMES
constants.summary: Update indirect light from dynamic lights in SDFGI over 16 frames. This is the least responsive, but has the lowest GPU requirements.

constants.name: ENV_SDFGI_UPDATE_LIGHT_MAX
constants.summary: Represents the size of the [enum EnvironmentSDFGIFramesToUpdateLight] enum.

constants.name: SUB_SURFACE_SCATTERING_QUALITY_DISABLED
constants.summary: Disables subsurface scattering entirely, even on materials that have [member BaseMaterial3D.subsurf_scatter_enabled] set to [code]true[/code]. This has the lowest GPU requirements.

constants.name: SUB_SURFACE_SCATTERING_QUALITY_LOW
constants.summary: Low subsurface scattering quality.

constants.name: SUB_SURFACE_SCATTERING_QUALITY_MEDIUM
constants.summary: Medium subsurface scattering quality.

constants.name: SUB_SURFACE_SCATTERING_QUALITY_HIGH
constants.summary: High subsurface scattering quality. This has the highest GPU requirements.

constants.name: DOF_BOKEH_BOX
constants.summary: Calculate the DOF blur using a box filter. The fastest option, but results in obvious lines in blur pattern.

constants.name: DOF_BOKEH_HEXAGON
constants.summary: Calculates DOF blur using a hexagon shaped filter.

constants.name: DOF_BOKEH_CIRCLE
constants.summary: Calculates DOF blur using a circle shaped filter. Best quality and most realistic, but slowest. Use only for areas where a lot of performance can be dedicated to post-processing (e.g. cutscenes).

constants.name: DOF_BLUR_QUALITY_VERY_LOW
constants.summary: Lowest quality DOF blur. This is the fastest setting, but you may be able to see filtering artifacts.

constants.name: DOF_BLUR_QUALITY_LOW
constants.summary: Low quality DOF blur.

constants.name: DOF_BLUR_QUALITY_MEDIUM
constants.summary: Medium quality DOF blur.

constants.name: DOF_BLUR_QUALITY_HIGH
constants.summary: Highest quality DOF blur. Results in the smoothest looking blur by taking the most samples, but is also significantly slower.

constants.name: INSTANCE_NONE
constants.summary: The instance does not have a type.

constants.name: INSTANCE_MESH
constants.summary: The instance is a mesh.

constants.name: INSTANCE_MULTIMESH
constants.summary: The instance is a multimesh.

constants.name: INSTANCE_PARTICLES
constants.summary: The instance is a particle emitter.

constants.name: INSTANCE_PARTICLES_COLLISION
constants.summary: The instance is a GPUParticles collision shape.

constants.name: INSTANCE_LIGHT
constants.summary: The instance is a light.

constants.name: INSTANCE_REFLECTION_PROBE
constants.summary: The instance is a reflection probe.

constants.name: INSTANCE_DECAL
constants.summary: The instance is a decal.

constants.name: INSTANCE_VOXEL_GI
constants.summary: The instance is a VoxelGI.

constants.name: INSTANCE_LIGHTMAP
constants.summary: The instance is a lightmap.

constants.name: INSTANCE_OCCLUDER
constants.summary: The instance is an occlusion culling occluder.

constants.name: INSTANCE_VISIBLITY_NOTIFIER
constants.summary: The instance is a visible on-screen notifier.

constants.name: INSTANCE_FOG_VOLUME
constants.summary: The instance is a fog volume.

constants.name: INSTANCE_MAX
constants.summary: Represents the size of the [enum InstanceType] enum.

constants.name: INSTANCE_GEOMETRY_MASK
constants.summary: A combination of the flags of geometry instances (mesh, multimesh, immediate and particles).

constants.name: INSTANCE_FLAG_USE_BAKED_LIGHT
constants.summary: Allows the instance to be used in baked lighting.

constants.name: INSTANCE_FLAG_USE_DYNAMIC_GI
constants.summary: Allows the instance to be used with dynamic global illumination.

constants.name: INSTANCE_FLAG_DRAW_NEXT_FRAME_IF_VISIBLE
constants.summary: When set, manually requests to draw geometry on next frame.

constants.name: INSTANCE_FLAG_IGNORE_OCCLUSION_CULLING
constants.summary: Always draw, even if the instance would be culled by occlusion culling. Does not affect view frustum culling.

constants.name: INSTANCE_FLAG_MAX
constants.summary: Represents the size of the [enum InstanceFlags] enum.

constants.name: SHADOW_CASTING_SETTING_OFF
constants.summary: Disable shadows from this instance.

constants.name: SHADOW_CASTING_SETTING_ON
constants.summary: Cast shadows from this instance.

constants.name: SHADOW_CASTING_SETTING_DOUBLE_SIDED
constants.summary: Disable backface culling when rendering the shadow of the object. This is slightly slower but may result in more correct shadows.

constants.name: SHADOW_CASTING_SETTING_SHADOWS_ONLY
constants.summary: Only render the shadows from the object. The object itself will not be drawn.

constants.name: VISIBILITY_RANGE_FADE_DISABLED
constants.summary: Disable visibility range fading for the given instance.

constants.name: VISIBILITY_RANGE_FADE_SELF
constants.summary: Fade-out the given instance when it approaches its visibility range limits.

constants.name: VISIBILITY_RANGE_FADE_DEPENDENCIES
constants.summary: Fade-in the given instance's dependencies when reaching its visibility range limits.

constants.name: BAKE_CHANNEL_ALBEDO_ALPHA
constants.summary: Index of [Image] in array of [Image]s returned by [method bake_render_uv2]. Image uses [constant Image.FORMAT_RGBA8] and contains albedo color in the [code].rgb[/code] channels and alpha in the [code].a[/code] channel.

constants.name: BAKE_CHANNEL_NORMAL
constants.summary: Index of [Image] in array of [Image]s returned by [method bake_render_uv2]. Image uses [constant Image.FORMAT_RGBA8] and contains the per-pixel normal of the object in the [code].rgb[/code] channels and nothing in the [code].a[/code] channel. The per-pixel normal is encoded as [code]normal * 0.5 + 0.5[/code].

constants.name: BAKE_CHANNEL_ORM
constants.summary: Index of [Image] in array of [Image]s returned by [method bake_render_uv2]. Image uses [constant Image.FORMAT_RGBA8] and contains ambient occlusion (from material and decals only) in the [code].r[/code] channel, roughness in the [code].g[/code] channel, metallic in the [code].b[/code] channel and sub surface scattering amount in the [code].a[/code] channel.

constants.name: BAKE_CHANNEL_EMISSION
constants.summary: Index of [Image] in array of [Image]s returned by [method bake_render_uv2]. Image uses [constant Image.FORMAT_RGBAH] and contains emission color in the [code].rgb[/code] channels and nothing in the [code].a[/code] channel.

constants.name: CANVAS_TEXTURE_CHANNEL_DIFFUSE
constants.summary: Diffuse canvas texture ([member CanvasTexture.diffuse_texture]).

constants.name: CANVAS_TEXTURE_CHANNEL_NORMAL
constants.summary: Normal map canvas texture ([member CanvasTexture.normal_texture]).

constants.name: CANVAS_TEXTURE_CHANNEL_SPECULAR
constants.summary: Specular map canvas texture ([member CanvasTexture.specular_texture]).

constants.name: NINE_PATCH_STRETCH
constants.summary: The nine patch gets stretched where needed.

constants.name: NINE_PATCH_TILE
constants.summary: The nine patch gets filled with tiles where needed.

constants.name: NINE_PATCH_TILE_FIT
constants.summary: The nine patch gets filled with tiles where needed and stretches them a bit if needed.

constants.name: CANVAS_ITEM_TEXTURE_FILTER_DEFAULT
constants.summary: Uses the default filter mode for this [Viewport].

constants.name: CANVAS_ITEM_TEXTURE_FILTER_NEAREST
constants.summary: The texture filter reads from the nearest pixel only. This makes the texture look pixelated from up close, and grainy from a distance (due to mipmaps not being sampled).

constants.name: CANVAS_ITEM_TEXTURE_FILTER_LINEAR
constants.summary: The texture filter blends between the nearest 4 pixels. This makes the texture look smooth from up close, and grainy from a distance (due to mipmaps not being sampled).

constants.name: CANVAS_ITEM_TEXTURE_FILTER_NEAREST_WITH_MIPMAPS
constants.summary: The texture filter reads from the nearest pixel and blends between the nearest 2 mipmaps (or uses the nearest mipmap if [member ProjectSettings.rendering/textures/default_filters/use_nearest_mipmap_filter] is [code]true[/code]). This makes the texture look pixelated from up close, and smooth from a distance. Use this for non-pixel art textures that may be viewed at a low scale (e.g. due to [Camera2D] zoom or sprite scaling), as mipmaps are important to smooth out pixels that are smaller than on-screen pixels.

constants.name: CANVAS_ITEM_TEXTURE_FILTER_LINEAR_WITH_MIPMAPS
constants.summary: The texture filter blends between the nearest 4 pixels and between the nearest 2 mipmaps (or uses the nearest mipmap if [member ProjectSettings.rendering/textures/default_filters/use_nearest_mipmap_filter] is [code]true[/code]). This makes the texture look smooth from up close, and smooth from a distance. Use this for non-pixel art textures that may be viewed at a low scale (e.g. due to [Camera2D] zoom or sprite scaling), as mipmaps are important to smooth out pixels that are smaller than on-screen pixels.

constants.name: CANVAS_ITEM_TEXTURE_FILTER_NEAREST_WITH_MIPMAPS_ANISOTROPIC
constants.summary: The texture filter reads from the nearest pixel and blends between 2 mipmaps (or uses the nearest mipmap if [member ProjectSettings.rendering/textures/default_filters/use_nearest_mipmap_filter] is [code]true[/code]) based on the angle between the surface and the camera view. This makes the texture look pixelated from up close, and smooth from a distance. Anisotropic filtering improves texture quality on surfaces that are almost in line with the camera, but is slightly slower. The anisotropic filtering level can be changed by adjusting [member ProjectSettings.rendering/textures/default_filters/anisotropic_filtering_level]. [b]Note:[/b] This texture filter is rarely useful in 2D projects. [constant CANVAS_ITEM_TEXTURE_FILTER_NEAREST_WITH_MIPMAPS] is usually more appropriate in this case.

constants.name: CANVAS_ITEM_TEXTURE_FILTER_LINEAR_WITH_MIPMAPS_ANISOTROPIC
constants.summary: The texture filter blends between the nearest 4 pixels and blends between 2 mipmaps (or uses the nearest mipmap if [member ProjectSettings.rendering/textures/default_filters/use_nearest_mipmap_filter] is [code]true[/code]) based on the angle between the surface and the camera view. This makes the texture look smooth from up close, and smooth from a distance. Anisotropic filtering improves texture quality on surfaces that are almost in line with the camera, but is slightly slower. The anisotropic filtering level can be changed by adjusting [member ProjectSettings.rendering/textures/default_filters/anisotropic_filtering_level]. [b]Note:[/b] This texture filter is rarely useful in 2D projects. [constant CANVAS_ITEM_TEXTURE_FILTER_LINEAR_WITH_MIPMAPS] is usually more appropriate in this case.

constants.name: CANVAS_ITEM_TEXTURE_FILTER_MAX
constants.summary: Max value for [enum CanvasItemTextureFilter] enum.

constants.name: CANVAS_ITEM_TEXTURE_REPEAT_DEFAULT
constants.summary: Uses the default repeat mode for this [Viewport].

constants.name: CANVAS_ITEM_TEXTURE_REPEAT_DISABLED
constants.summary: Disables textures repeating. Instead, when reading UVs outside the 0-1 range, the value will be clamped to the edge of the texture, resulting in a stretched out look at the borders of the texture.

constants.name: CANVAS_ITEM_TEXTURE_REPEAT_ENABLED
constants.summary: Enables the texture to repeat when UV coordinates are outside the 0-1 range. If using one of the linear filtering modes, this can result in artifacts at the edges of a texture when the sampler filters across the edges of the texture.

constants.name: CANVAS_ITEM_TEXTURE_REPEAT_MIRROR
constants.summary: Flip the texture when repeating so that the edge lines up instead of abruptly changing.

constants.name: CANVAS_ITEM_TEXTURE_REPEAT_MAX
constants.summary: Max value for [enum CanvasItemTextureRepeat] enum.

constants.name: CANVAS_GROUP_MODE_DISABLED
constants.summary: Child draws over parent and is not clipped.

constants.name: CANVAS_GROUP_MODE_CLIP_ONLY
constants.summary: Parent is used for the purposes of clipping only. Child is clipped to the parent's visible area, parent is not drawn.

constants.name: CANVAS_GROUP_MODE_CLIP_AND_DRAW
constants.summary: Parent is used for clipping child, but parent is also drawn underneath child as normal before clipping child to its visible area.

constants.name: CANVAS_LIGHT_MODE_POINT
constants.summary: 2D point light (see [PointLight2D]).

constants.name: CANVAS_LIGHT_MODE_DIRECTIONAL
constants.summary: 2D directional (sun/moon) light (see [DirectionalLight2D]).

constants.name: CANVAS_LIGHT_BLEND_MODE_ADD
constants.summary: Adds light color additive to the canvas.

constants.name: CANVAS_LIGHT_BLEND_MODE_SUB
constants.summary: Adds light color subtractive to the canvas.

constants.name: CANVAS_LIGHT_BLEND_MODE_MIX
constants.summary: The light adds color depending on transparency.

constants.name: CANVAS_LIGHT_FILTER_NONE
constants.summary: Do not apply a filter to canvas light shadows.

constants.name: CANVAS_LIGHT_FILTER_PCF5
constants.summary: Use PCF5 filtering to filter canvas light shadows.

constants.name: CANVAS_LIGHT_FILTER_PCF13
constants.summary: Use PCF13 filtering to filter canvas light shadows.

constants.name: CANVAS_LIGHT_FILTER_MAX
constants.summary: Max value of the [enum CanvasLightShadowFilter] enum.

constants.name: CANVAS_OCCLUDER_POLYGON_CULL_DISABLED
constants.summary: Culling of the canvas occluder is disabled.

constants.name: CANVAS_OCCLUDER_POLYGON_CULL_CLOCKWISE
constants.summary: Culling of the canvas occluder is clockwise.

constants.name: CANVAS_OCCLUDER_POLYGON_CULL_COUNTER_CLOCKWISE
constants.summary: Culling of the canvas occluder is counterclockwise.

constants.name: GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_BOOL
constants.summary: Boolean global shader parameter ([code]global uniform bool ...[/code]).

constants.name: GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_BVEC2
constants.summary: 2-dimensional boolean vector global shader parameter ([code]global uniform bvec2 ...[/code]).

constants.name: GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_BVEC3
constants.summary: 3-dimensional boolean vector global shader parameter ([code]global uniform bvec3 ...[/code]).

constants.name: GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_BVEC4
constants.summary: 4-dimensional boolean vector global shader parameter ([code]global uniform bvec4 ...[/code]).

constants.name: GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_INT
constants.summary: Integer global shader parameter ([code]global uniform int ...[/code]).

constants.name: GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_IVEC2
constants.summary: 2-dimensional integer vector global shader parameter ([code]global uniform ivec2 ...[/code]).

constants.name: GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_IVEC3
constants.summary: 3-dimensional integer vector global shader parameter ([code]global uniform ivec3 ...[/code]).

constants.name: GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_IVEC4
constants.summary: 4-dimensional integer vector global shader parameter ([code]global uniform ivec4 ...[/code]).

constants.name: GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_RECT2I
constants.summary: 2-dimensional integer rectangle global shader parameter ([code]global uniform ivec4 ...[/code]). Equivalent to [constant GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_IVEC4] in shader code, but exposed as a [Rect2i] in the editor UI.

constants.name: GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_UINT
constants.summary: Unsigned integer global shader parameter ([code]global uniform uint ...[/code]).

constants.name: GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_UVEC2
constants.summary: 2-dimensional unsigned integer vector global shader parameter ([code]global uniform uvec2 ...[/code]).

constants.name: GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_UVEC3
constants.summary: 3-dimensional unsigned integer vector global shader parameter ([code]global uniform uvec3 ...[/code]).

constants.name: GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_UVEC4
constants.summary: 4-dimensional unsigned integer vector global shader parameter ([code]global uniform uvec4 ...[/code]).

constants.name: GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_FLOAT
constants.summary: Single-precision floating-point global shader parameter ([code]global uniform float ...[/code]).

constants.name: GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_VEC2
constants.summary: 2-dimensional floating-point vector global shader parameter ([code]global uniform vec2 ...[/code]).

constants.name: GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_VEC3
constants.summary: 3-dimensional floating-point vector global shader parameter ([code]global uniform vec3 ...[/code]).

constants.name: GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_VEC4
constants.summary: 4-dimensional floating-point vector global shader parameter ([code]global uniform vec4 ...[/code]).

constants.name: GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_COLOR
constants.summary: Color global shader parameter ([code]global uniform vec4 ...[/code]). Equivalent to [constant GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_VEC4] in shader code, but exposed as a [Color] in the editor UI.

constants.name: GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_RECT2
constants.summary: 2-dimensional floating-point rectangle global shader parameter ([code]global uniform vec4 ...[/code]). Equivalent to [constant GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_VEC4] in shader code, but exposed as a [Rect2] in the editor UI.

constants.name: GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_MAT2
constants.summary: 2×2 matrix global shader parameter ([code]global uniform mat2 ...[/code]). Exposed as a [PackedInt32Array] in the editor UI.

constants.name: GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_MAT3
constants.summary: 3×3 matrix global shader parameter ([code]global uniform mat3 ...[/code]). Exposed as a [Basis] in the editor UI.

constants.name: GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_MAT4
constants.summary: 4×4 matrix global shader parameter ([code]global uniform mat4 ...[/code]). Exposed as a [Projection] in the editor UI.

constants.name: GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_TRANSFORM_2D
constants.summary: 2-dimensional transform global shader parameter ([code]global uniform mat2x3 ...[/code]). Exposed as a [Transform2D] in the editor UI.

constants.name: GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_TRANSFORM
constants.summary: 3-dimensional transform global shader parameter ([code]global uniform mat3x4 ...[/code]). Exposed as a [Transform3D] in the editor UI.

constants.name: GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_SAMPLER2D
constants.summary: 2D sampler global shader parameter ([code]global uniform sampler2D ...[/code]). Exposed as a [Texture2D] in the editor UI.

constants.name: GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_SAMPLER2DARRAY
constants.summary: 2D sampler array global shader parameter ([code]global uniform sampler2DArray ...[/code]). Exposed as a [Texture2DArray] in the editor UI.

constants.name: GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_SAMPLER3D
constants.summary: 3D sampler global shader parameter ([code]global uniform sampler3D ...[/code]). Exposed as a [Texture3D] in the editor UI.

constants.name: GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_SAMPLERCUBE
constants.summary: Cubemap sampler global shader parameter ([code]global uniform samplerCube ...[/code]). Exposed as a [Cubemap] in the editor UI.

constants.name: GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_SAMPLEREXT
constants.summary: External sampler global shader parameter ([code]global uniform samplerExternalOES ...[/code]). Exposed as an [ExternalTexture] in the editor UI.

constants.name: GLOBAL_VAR_TYPE_MAX
constants.summary: Represents the size of the [enum GlobalShaderParameterType] enum.

constants.name: RENDERING_INFO_TOTAL_OBJECTS_IN_FRAME
constants.summary: Number of objects rendered in the current 3D scene. This varies depending on camera position and rotation.

constants.name: RENDERING_INFO_TOTAL_PRIMITIVES_IN_FRAME
constants.summary: Number of points, lines, or triangles rendered in the current 3D scene. This varies depending on camera position and rotation.

constants.name: RENDERING_INFO_TOTAL_DRAW_CALLS_IN_FRAME
constants.summary: Number of draw calls performed to render in the current 3D scene. This varies depending on camera position and rotation.

constants.name: RENDERING_INFO_TEXTURE_MEM_USED
constants.summary: Texture memory used (in bytes).

constants.name: RENDERING_INFO_BUFFER_MEM_USED
constants.summary: Buffer memory used (in bytes). This includes vertex data, uniform buffers, and many miscellaneous buffer types used internally.

constants.name: RENDERING_INFO_VIDEO_MEM_USED
constants.summary: Video memory used (in bytes). When using the Forward+ or Mobile renderers, this is always greater than the sum of [constant RENDERING_INFO_TEXTURE_MEM_USED] and [constant RENDERING_INFO_BUFFER_MEM_USED], since there is miscellaneous data not accounted for by those two metrics. When using the Compatibility renderer, this is equal to the sum of [constant RENDERING_INFO_TEXTURE_MEM_USED] and [constant RENDERING_INFO_BUFFER_MEM_USED].

constants.name: RENDERING_INFO_PIPELINE_COMPILATIONS_CANVAS
constants.summary: Number of pipeline compilations that were triggered by the 2D canvas renderer.

constants.name: RENDERING_INFO_PIPELINE_COMPILATIONS_MESH
constants.summary: Number of pipeline compilations that were triggered by loading meshes. These compilations will show up as longer loading times the first time a user runs the game and the pipeline is required.

constants.name: RENDERING_INFO_PIPELINE_COMPILATIONS_SURFACE
constants.summary: Number of pipeline compilations that were triggered by building the surface cache before rendering the scene. These compilations will show up as a stutter when loading a scene the first time a user runs the game and the pipeline is required.

constants.name: RENDERING_INFO_PIPELINE_COMPILATIONS_DRAW
constants.summary: Number of pipeline compilations that were triggered while drawing the scene. These compilations will show up as stutters during gameplay the first time a user runs the game and the pipeline is required.

constants.name: RENDERING_INFO_PIPELINE_COMPILATIONS_SPECIALIZATION
constants.summary: Number of pipeline compilations that were triggered to optimize the current scene. These compilations are done in the background and should not cause any stutters whatsoever.

constants.name: PIPELINE_SOURCE_CANVAS
constants.summary: Pipeline compilation that was triggered by the 2D canvas renderer.

constants.name: PIPELINE_SOURCE_MESH
constants.summary: Pipeline compilation that was triggered by loading a mesh.

constants.name: PIPELINE_SOURCE_SURFACE
constants.summary: Pipeline compilation that was triggered by building the surface cache before rendering the scene.

constants.name: PIPELINE_SOURCE_DRAW
constants.summary: Pipeline compilation that was triggered while drawing the scene.

constants.name: PIPELINE_SOURCE_SPECIALIZATION
constants.summary: Pipeline compilation that was triggered to optimize the current scene.

constants.name: PIPELINE_SOURCE_MAX
constants.summary: Represents the size of the [enum PipelineSource] enum.

constants.name: SPLASH_STRETCH_MODE_DISABLED
constants.summary: No stretching is applied.

constants.name: SPLASH_STRETCH_MODE_KEEP
constants.summary: Stretches image to fullscreen while preserving aspect ratio.

constants.name: SPLASH_STRETCH_MODE_KEEP_WIDTH
constants.summary: Stretches the height of the image based on the width of the screen.

constants.name: SPLASH_STRETCH_MODE_KEEP_HEIGHT
constants.summary: Stretches the width of the image based on the height of the screen.

constants.name: SPLASH_STRETCH_MODE_COVER
constants.summary: Stretches the image to cover the entire screen while preserving aspect ratio.

constants.name: SPLASH_STRETCH_MODE_IGNORE
constants.summary: Stretches the image to cover the entire screen but doesn't preserve aspect ratio.
